View::Tasks,\n91\t        View::Costs,\n92\t        View::Metrics,\n93\t        View::Perf,\n94\t        View::Logs,\n95\t        View::Subscriptions,\n96\t        View::Alerts,\n97\t pub fn next(&self) -> View {\n105\t        let idx = Self::ALL.iter().position(|v| v == self).unwrap_or(0);\n106\t        Self::ALL[(idx + 1) % Self::ALL.len()]\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    /// if idx == 0 {\n113\t            Self::ALL[Self::ALL.len() - 1]\n114\t        } else {\n115\t            Self::ALL[idx - 1]\n116\t        }\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    /// Try to parse a view from a // lowercase only\n124\t            'W' => Some(View::Workspaces), // uppercase only\n125\t            't' | 'T' => Some(View::Tasks),\n126\t            'c' | 'C' => Some(View::Costs),\n127\t should show a highlight border.\n176\t    pub fn is_highlighted(&self) -> bool {\n177\t        !matches!(self, FocusPanel::None)\n178\t    }\n179\t}\n180\t\n181\t/// Layout mode based on terminal dimensions.\n182\t///\n183\t/// The TUI adapts its layout based on available screen real estate.\n184\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n185\tpub enum LayoutMode {\n186\t    /// Ultra-wide layout (199+ cols): 3-column with all 6 panels visible.\n187\t    /// Left (66 cols): Workers + Subscriptions\n188\t    /// Middle (66 cols): Tasks + Activity\n189\t    /// Right (65 cols): Costs + Actions\n190\t    UltraWide,\n191\t    /// Wide layout (120-198 cols): 2-column with 4 panels visible.\n192\t    Wide,\n193\t    /// Narrow layout (<120 cols): single-view mode.\n194\t    Narrow,\n195\t}\n196\t\n197\timpl LayoutMode {\n198\t    /// Determine the layout mode based on terminal width.\n199\t    pub fn from_width(width: u16) -> Self {\n200\t        if width >= 199 {\n201\t            LayoutMode::UltraWide\n202\t        } else if width >= 120 {\n203\t            LayoutMode::Wide\n204\t        } else {\n205\t            LayoutMode::Narrow\n206\t        }\n207\t    }\n208\t\n209\t    /// Get the minimum terminal height for this layout mode.\n210\t    pub fn min_height(&self) -> u16 {\n211\t        match self {\n212\t            LayoutMode::UltraWide => 38,\n213\t            LayoutMode::Wide => 30,\n214\t            LayoutMode::Narrow => 20,\n215\t        }\n216\t    }\n217\t\n218\t    /// Check if the terminal dimensions meet the layout requirements.\n219\t    pub fn meets_requirements(&self, width: u16, height: u16) -> bool {\n220\t        let required_mode = Self::from_width(width);\n221\t        let height_ok = height >= self.min_height();\n222\t\n223\t        // Mode is valid if it matches width requirement and height is sufficient\n224\t        *self == required_mode && height_ok\n225\t    }\n226\t}\n227\t\n228\t#[cfg(test)]\n229\tmod tests {\n230\t    use super::*;\n231\t\n232\t    #[test]\n233\t    fn test_view_hotkeys() {\n234\t        assert_eq!(View::Overview.hotkey(), 'o');\n235\t {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_cbf413cb0045499abefa5a88","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Logging infrastructure for FORGE.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module provides structured logging using the `tracing` ecosystem.\n4\t//! Per ADR 0014, FORGE maintains its own logs separate from worker logs,\n5\t//! enabling developers to iterate on test FORGE instances in different terminals.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! ## Features\n8\t//!\n9\t//! - JSON lines format for machine parsing\n10\t//! - File output to `~/.forge/logs/forge.log`\n11\t//! - Console output with configurable verbosity\n12\t//! - `--debug` flag support for verbose logging\n13\t//!\n14\t//! ## Example\n15\t//!\n16\t//! ```no_run\n17\t//! use forge_core::logging;\n18\t//!\n19\t//! // Initialize logging (call once at startup)\n20\t//! let _guard = logging::init_logging(None, false).expect(\"logging init\");\n21\t//!\n22\t//! // Use tracing macros\n23\t//! tracing::info!(\"FORGE started\");\n24\t//! tracing::debug!(worker_id = \"sonnet-alpha\", \"spawning worker\");\n25\t//! ```\n26\t\n27\tuse std::path::PathBuf;\n28\t\n29\tuse tracing_appender::non_blocking::WorkerGuard;\n30\tuse tracing_subscriber::{\n31\t    EnvFilter,\n32\t    fmt::{self, format::FmtSpan},\n33\t    layer::SubscriberExt,\n34\t    util::SubscriberInitExt,\n35\t};\n36\t\n37\tuse crate::error::{ForgeError, Result};\n38\t\n39\t/// Guard that must be held to ensure log flushing on shutdown.\n40\t///\n41\t/// When this guard is dropped, it flushes any pending log entries.\n42\t/// Keep this guard alive for the lifetime of the application.\n43\tpub struct LogGuard {\n44\t    _file_guard: Option<WorkerGuard>,\n45\t}\n46\t\n47\t/// Initialize the FORGE logging system.\n48\t///\n49\t/// This sets up:\n50\t/// - File logging to `~/.forge/logs/forge.log` (JSON lines format)\n51\t/// - Console logging to stderr (human-readable format)\n52\t///\n53\t/// # Arguments\n54\t///\n55\t/// * `log_dir` - Optional custom log directory. Defaults to `~/.forge/logs/`\n56\t/// * `verbose` - If true, sets log level to DEBUG. Otherwise uses INFO.\n57\t///\n58\t/// # Returns\n59\t///\n60\t/// A [`LogGuard`] that must be held for the application lifetime to ensure\n61\t/// logs are properly flushed on shutdown.\n62\t///\n63\t/// # Example\n64\t///\n65\t/// ```no_run\n66\t/// use forge_core::logging;\n67\t///\n68\t/// fn main() -> forge_core::Result<()> {\n69\t///     let _guard = logging::init_logging(None, false)?;\n70\t///     tracing::info!(\"Application started\");\n71\t///     Ok(())\n72\t/// }\n73\t/// ```\n74\tpub fn init_logging(log_dir: Option<PathBuf>, verbose: bool) -> Result<LogGuard> {\n75\t    // Determine log directory\n76\t    let log_dir = match log_dir {\n77\t        Some(dir) => dir,\n78\t        None => default_log_dir()?,\n79\t    };\n80\t\n81\t    // Ensure log directory exists\n82\t    std::fs::create_dir_all(&log_dir).map_err(|e| ForgeError::DirectoryCreation {\n83\t        path: log_dir.clone(),\n84\t        source: e,\n85\t    })?;\n86\t\n87\t    // Set up file appender for JSON logs\n88\t    let file_appender = tracing_appender::rolling::daily(&log_dir, \"forge.log\");\n89\t    let (non_blocking_file, file_guard) = tracing_appender::non_blocking(file_appender);\n90\t\n91\t    // Determine log level based on verbose flag and environment\n92\t    let default_level = if verbose { \"debug\" } else { \"info\" };\n93\t    let env_filter = EnvFilter::try_from_default_env()\n94\t        .unwrap_or_else(|_| EnvFilter::new(format!(\"forge={default_level}\")));\n95\t\n96\t    // JSON layer for file output\n97\t    let file_layer = fmt::layer()\n98\t        .with_writer(non_blocking_file)\n99\t        .with_ansi(false)\n100\t        .json()\n101\t        .with_span_events(FmtSpan::CLOSE)\n102\t        .with_current_span(true)\n103\t        .with_span_list(true);\n104\t\n105\t    // Human-readable layer for console output\n106\t    let console_layer = fmt::layer()\n107\t        .with_writer(std::io::stderr)\n108\t        .with_ansi(true)\n109\t        .with_target(true)\n110\t        .with_thread_ids(false)\n111\t        .with_thread_names(false)\n112\t        .with_file(verbose)\n113\t        .with_line_number(verbose)\n114\t        .compact();\n115\t\n116\t    // Combine layers with filter\n117\t    tracing_subscriber::registry()\n118\t        .with(env_filter)\n119\t        .with(file_layer)\n120\t        .with(console_layer)\n121\t        .init();\n122\t\n123\t    tracing::debug!(log_dir = %log_dir.display(), verbose, \"logging initialized\");\n124\t\n125\t    Ok(LogGuard {\n126\t        _file_guard: Some(file_guard),\n127\t    })\n128\t}\n129\t\n130\t/// Initialize minimal console-only logging for testing.\n131\t///\n132\t/// This is a simpler alternative to [`init_logging`] that only logs to stderr.\n133\t/// Useful for tests and development.\n134\tpub fn init_test_logging() {\n135\t    let _ = tracing_subscriber::fmt()\n136\t        .with_env_filter(EnvFilter::new(\"debug\"))\n137\t        .with_test_writer()\n138\t        .try_init();\n139\t}\n140\t\n141\t/// Get the default log directory path.\n142\t///\n143\t/// Returns `~/.forge/logs/`\n144\tpub fn default_log_dir() -> Result<PathBuf> {\n145\t    let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").map_err(|_| ForgeError::Internal {\n146\t        message: \"HOME environment variable not set\".into(),\n147\t    })?;\n148\t\n149\t    Ok(PathBuf::from(home).join(\".forge\").join(\"logs\"))\n150\t}\n151\t\n152\t/// Get the default FORGE log file path.\n153\t///\n154\t/// Returns `~/.forge/logs/forge.log`\n155\tpub fn default_log_file() -> Result<PathBuf> {\n156\t    Ok(default_log_dir()?.join(\"forge.log\"))\n157\t}\n158\t\n159\t/// Convenience macro for logging worker-related events.\n160\t///\n161\t/// # Example\n162\t///\n163\t/// ```ignore\n164\t/// log_worker_event!(\"sonnet-alpha\", \"spawned\");\n165\t/// log_worker_event!(\"sonnet-alpha\", \"failed\", reason = \"timeout\");\n166\t/// ```\n167\t#[macro_export]\n168\tmacro_rules! log_worker_event {\n169\t    ($worker_id:expr, $event:expr) => {\n170\t        tracing::info!(\n171\t            worker_id = $worker_id,\n172\t            event = $event,\n173\t            target: \"forge::worker\",\n174\t            \"worker event\"\n175\t        )\n176\t    };\n177\t    ($worker_id:expr, $event:expr, $($field:tt)*) => {\n178\t        tracing::info!(\n179\t            worker_id = $worker_id,\n180\t            event = $event,\n181\t            target: \"forge::worker\",\n182\t            $($field)*,\n183\t            \"worker event\"\n184\t        )\n185\t    };\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\t/// Convenience macro for logging tool execution.\n189\t///\n190\t/// # Example\n191\t///\n192\t/// ```ignore\n193\t/// log_tool_call!(\"spawn_worker\", success = true);\n194\t/// log_tool_call!(\"kill_worker\", success = false, error = \"not found\");\n195\t/// ```\n196\t#[macro_export]\n197\tmacro_rules! log_tool_call {\n198\t    ($tool_name:expr, $($field:tt)*) => {\n199\t        tracing::info!(\n200\t            tool = Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n359\t        let mut params: Vec<Box<dyn rusqlite::ToSql>> = Vec::new();\n360\t\n361\t        if let Some(start) = &self.start_time {\n362\t            conditions.push(\"timestamp >= ?\".to_string());\n363\t            params.push(Box::new(start.to_rfc3339()));\n364\t        }\n365\t        if let Some(end) = &self.end_time {\n366\t            conditions.push(\"timestamp <= ?\".to_string());\n367\t            params.push(Box::new(end.to_rfc3339()));\n368\t        }\n369\t        if let Some(types) = &self.event_types {\n370\t            let placeholders: Vec<String> = (0..types.len()).map(|_| \"?\".to_string()).collect();\n371\t            conditions.push(format!(\"event_type IN ({})\", placeholders.join(\", \")));\n372\t            for t in types {\n373\t                params.push(Box::new(t.to_string()));\n374\t            }\n375\t        }\n376\t        if let Some(actor) = &self.actor {\n377\t            conditions.push(\"actor = ?\".to_string());\n378\t            params.push(Box::new(actor.clone()));\n379\t        }\n380\t        if let Some(entity_type) = &self.entity_type {\n381\t            conditions.push(\"entity_type = ?\".to_string());\n382\t            params.push(Box::new(entity_type.clone()));\n383\t        }\n384\t        if let Some(entity_id) = &self.entity_id {\n385\t            conditions.push(\"entity_id = ?\".to_string());\n386\t            params.push(Box::new(entity_id.clone()));\n387\t        }\n388\t        if let Some(severity) = &self.severity {\n389\t            conditions.push(\"severity = ?\".to_string());\n390\t            params.push(Box::new(severity.to_string()));\n391\t        }\n392\t\n393\t        let where_clause = if conditions.is_empty() {\n394\t            String::new()\n395\t        } else {\n396\t            format!(\"WHERE {}\", conditions.join(\" AND \"))\n397\t        };\n398\t\n399\t        (where_clause, params)\n400\t    }\n401\t}\n402\t\n403\t/// Retention policy for audit logs.\n404\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n405\tpub struct RetentionPolicy {\n406\t    /// Number of days to retain logs\n407\t    pub days: i64,\n408\t }\n428\t    }\n429\t\n430\t    /// Get the cutoff timestamp for this policy.\n431\t    pub fn cutoff(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {\n432\t        Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::days(self.days)\n433\t fs::create_dir_all(parent)\n452\t                .map_err(|e| ForgeError::io(\"create_dir_all\", parent, e))?;\n453\t        }\n454\t\n455\t        let conn = Connection::open(&db_path)\n456\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::Audit { message: format!(\"failed to open database: {}\", e) })?;\n457\t\n458\t        // Enable WAL mode for better concurrent access\n459\t        // Returns the new mode, so we need to use query_row\n460\t        conn.query_row(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\", [], |row| {\n461\t            row.get::<_, String>(0)\n462\t        })\n463\t        .map_err(|e| logger.migrate()?;\n478\t\n479\t        // Apply retention if configured\n480\t        if logger.retention.apply_on_startup {\n481\t            logger.apply_retention()?;\n482\t        }\n483\t\n484\t self.apply_retention().map(|_| ())\n514\t    }\n515\t\n516\t    /// Run database migrations.\n517\t    fn migrate(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n518\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n519\t            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n520\t        })?;\n521\t\n522\t        // Create schema version table\n523\t        conn.execute(\n524\t            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_schema_version (\n525\t                version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY\n526\t            )\",\n527\t            [],\n528\t        ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create schema table: {}\", e)))?;\n529\t\n530\t        // Get current version\n531\t        let current_version: i32 = conn\n532\t            .query_row(\n533\t                \"SELECT COALESCE(MAX(version), 0) FROM audit_schema_version\",\n534\t                [],\n535\t                |row| row.get(0),\n536\t            )\n537\t            .unwrap_or(0);\n538\t\n539\t        if current_version < AUDIT_SCHEMA_VERSION {\n540\t            info!(\n541\t                current = current_version,\n542\t                target = AUDIT_SCHEMA_VERSION,\n543\t                \"Running audit database migrations\"\n544\t            );\n545\t\n546\t            // Create audit logs table\n547\t            conn.execute(\n548\t                \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (\n549\t                    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n550\t                    timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,\n551\t                    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n552\t                    actor TEXT NOT NULL,\n553\t                    entity_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n554\t                    entity_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n555\t                    old_value TEXT,\n556\t                    new_value TEXT,\n557\t                    metadata TEXT,\n558\t                    severity TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'info'\n559\t                )\",\n560\t                [],\n561\t            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create audit_logs table: {}\", e)))?;\n562\t\n563\t            // Create indexes for efficient queries\n564\t            conn.execute(\n565\t                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_timestamp\n566\t                 ON audit_logs(timestamp DESC)\",\n567\t                [],\n568\t            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create timestamp index: {}\", e)))?;\n569\t\n570\t            conn.execute(\n571\t                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_event_type\n572\t                 ON audit_logs(event_type)\",\n573\t                [],\n574\t            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create event_type index: {}\", e)))?;\n575\t\n576\t            conn.execute(\n577\t                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_actor\n578\t                 ON audit_logs(actor)\",\n579\t                [],\n580\t            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create actor index: {}\", e)))?;\n581\t\n582\t            conn.execute(\n583\t                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_entity\n584\t                 ON audit_logs(entity_type, entity_id)\",\n585\t                [],\n586\t            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create entity index: {}\", e)))?;\n587\t\n588\t            conn.execute(\n589\t                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_severity\n590\t                 ON audit_logs(severity)\",\n591\t                [],\n592\t            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create severity index: {}\", e)))?;\n593\t\n594\t            // Record schema version\n595\t            conn.execute(\n596\t                \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO audit_schema_version (version) VALUES (?)\",\n597\t                params![AUDIT_SCHEMA_VERSION],\n598\t            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to record schema version: {}\", e)))?;\n599\t\n600\t            info!(\"Audit database initialized at schema version {}\", AUDIT_SCHEMA_VERSION);\n601\t        }\n602\t\n603\t        Ok(())\n604\t    }\n605\t\n606\t    /// Log an audit event.\n607\t    pub fn log(&self, event: AuditEvent) -> Result<i64> {\n608\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n609\t            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n610\t        })?;\n611\t\n612\t        debug!(\n613\t            event_type = %event.event_type,\n614\t            actor = %event.actor,\n615\t            entity = %format!(\"{}:{}\", event.entity_type, event.entity_id),\n616\t            \"Logging audit event\"\n617\t        );\n618\t\n619\t        conn.execute(\n620\t            \"INSERT INTO audit_logs (\n621\t                timestamp, event_type, actor, entity_type, entity_id,\n622\t                old_value, new_value, metadata, severity\n623\t            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\",\n624\t            params![\n625\t                event.timestamp.to_rfc3339(),\n626\t                event.event_type.to_string(),\n627\t                event.actor,\n628\t                event.entity_type,\n629\t                event.entity_id,\n630\t                event.old_value,\n631\t                event.new_value,\n632\t                event.metadata,\n633\t                event.severity.to_string(),\n634\t            ],\n635\t        )\n636\t        .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to insert audit log: {}\", e)))?;\n637\t\n638\t        Ok(conn.last_insert_rowid())\n639\t    }\n640\t\n641\t    /// Query audit logs with optional filters.\n642\t    pub fn query(&self, filter: &AuditFilter) -> Result<Vec<AuditEvent>> {\n643\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n644\t            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n645\t        })?;\n646\t\n647\t        let (where_clause, params) = filter.build_where_clause();\n648\t        let limit = filter.limit.unwrap_or(MAX_QUERY_RESULTS);\n649\t        let offset = filter.offset.unwrap_or(0);\n650\t\n651\t        let sql = format!(\n652\t            \"SELECT timestamp, event_type, actor, entity_type, entity_id,\n653\t                    old_value, new_value, metadata, severity\n654\t             FROM audit_logs\n655\t             {}\n656\t             ORDER BY timestamp DESC\n657\t             LIMIT {} OFFSET {}\",\n658\t            where_clause, limit, offset\n659\t        );\n660\t\n661\t        let mut stmt = conn\n662\t            .prepare(&sql)\n663\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to prepare query: {}\", e)))?;\n664\t\n665\t        let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = params.iter().map(|p| p.as_ref()).collect();\n666\t\n667\t        // Helper to parse timestamp from string\n668\t        let parse_timestamp = |s: String| -> std::result::Result<DateTime<Utc>, String> {\n669\t            DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&s)\n670\t                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc))\n671\t                .map_err(|e| format!(\"invalid timestamp: {}\", e))\n672\t        };\n673\t\n674\t        // Helper to parse event type from string\n675\t        let parse_event_type = |s: String| -> std::result::Result<EventType, String> {\n676\t            s.parse().map_err(|e| format!(\"invalid event type: {}\", e))\n677\t        };\n678\t\n679\t        // Helper to parse severity from string\n680\t        let parse_severity = |s: String| -> std::result::Result<Severity, String> {\n681\t            s.parse().map_err(|e| format!(\"invalid severity: {}\", e))\n682\t        };\n683\t\n684\t        let events = stmt\n685\t            .query_map(param_refs.as_slice(), |row| {\n686\t                let ts_str: String = row.get(0)?;\n687\t                let event_type_str: String = row.get(1)?;\n688\t                let severity_str: String = row.get(8)?;\n689\t\n690\t                Ok(AuditEvent {\n691\t                    timestamp: parse_timestamp(ts_str).map_err(|e| {\n692\t                        rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(\n693\t                            Box::new(ParseError(e))\n694\t                        )\n695\t                    })?,\n696\t                    event_type: parse_event_type(event_type_str).map_err(|e| {\n697\t                        rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(\n698\t                            Box::new(ParseError(e))\n699\t                        )\n700\t                    })?,\n701\t                    actor: row.get(2)?,\n702\t                    entity_type: row.get(3)?,\n703\t                    entity_id: row.get(4)?,\n704\t                    old_value: row.get(5)?,\n705\t                    new_value: row.get(6)?,\n706\t                    metadata: row.get(7)?,\n707\t                    severity: parse_severity(severity_str).map_err(|e| {\n708\t                        rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(\n709\t                            Box::new(ParseError(e))\n710\t                        )\n711\t                    })?,\n712\t                })\n713\t            })\n714\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to execute query: {}\", e)))?\n715\t            .collect::<std::result::Result<Vec<_>, _>>()\n716\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to parse results: {}\", e)))?;\n717\t\n718\t        Ok(events)\n719\t    }\n720\t\n721\t    /// Get a count of audit logs matching the filter.\n722\t    pub fn count(&self, filter: &AuditFilter) -> Result<i64> {\n723\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n724\t            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n725\t        })?;\n726\t\n727\t        let (where_clause, params) = filter.build_where_clause();\n728\t\n729\t        let sql = format!(\n730\t            \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_logs {}\",\n731\t            where_clause\n732\t        );\n733\t\n734\t        let mut stmt = conn\n735\t            .prepare(&sql)\n736\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to prepare count query: {}\", e)))?;\n737\t\n738\t        let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = params.iter().map(|p| p.as_ref()).collect();\n739\t\n740\t        let count: i64 = stmt\n741\t            .query_row(param_refs.as_slice(), |row| row.get(0))\n742\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to execute count query: {}\", e)))?;\n743\t\n744\t        Ok(count)\n745\t    }\n746\t\n747\t    /// Get audit logs for a specific entity.\n748\t    pub fn get_entity_history(\n749\t        &self,\n750\t        entity_type: &str,\n751\t        entity_id: &str,\n752\t        limit: Option<i64>,\n753\t    ) -> Result<Vec<AuditEvent>> {\n754\t        let filter = AuditFilter::new()\n755\t            .with_entity(entity_type, entity_id)\n756\t            .with_pagination(limit.unwrap_or(1000), 0);\n757\t\n758\t        self.query(&filter)\n759\t    }\n760\t\n761\t    /// Apply retention policy - delete old logs.\n762\t    pub fn apply_retention(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n763\t        let cutoff = self.retention.cutoff();\n764\t        let cutoff_str = cutoff.to_rfc3339();\n765\t\n766\t        info!(\n767\t            cutoff = %cutoff_str,\n768\t            days = self.retention.days,\n769\t            \"Applying audit log retention\"\n770\t        );\n771\t\n772\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n773\t            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n774\t        })?;\n775\t\n776\t        let affected = conn\n777\t            .execute(\n778\t                \"DELETE FROM audit_logs WHERE timestamp < ?\",\n779\t                params![cutoff_str],\n780\t            )\n781\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to apply retention: {}\", e)))?;\n782\t\n783\t        if affected > 0 {\n784\t            info!(\n785\t                deleted = affected,\n786\t                \"Deleted audit logs past retention period\"\n787\t            );\n788\t        }\n789\t\n790\t        Ok(affected)\n791\t    }\n792\t\n793\t    /// Export audit logs to JSON.\n794\t    pub fn export_json(&self, filter: &AuditFilter, output: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n795\t        let events = self.query(filter)?;\n796\t\n797\t        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&events)\n798\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to serialize JSON: {}\", e)))?;\n799\t\n800\t        fs::write(output, json)\n801\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to write export: {}\", e)))?;\n802\t\n803\t        info!(\n804\t            count = events.len(),\n805\t            path = %output.display(),\n806\t            \"Exported audit logs to JSON\"\n807\t        );\n808\t\n809\t        Ok(())\n810\t    }\n811\t\n812\t    /// Export audit logs to CSV.\n813\t    pub fn export_csv(&self, filter: &AuditFilter, output: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n814\t        let events = self.query(filter)?;\n815\t\n816\t        let mut wtr = csv::Writer::from_path(output)\n817\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create CSV writer: {}\", e)))?;\n818\t\n819\t        // Write header\n820\t        wtr.write_record(&[\n821\t            \"timestamp\", \"event_type\", \"actor\", \"entity_type\", \"entity_id\",\n822\t            \"old_value\", \"new_value\", \"metadata\", \"severity\",\n823\t        ])\n824\t        .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to write CSV header: {}\", e)))?;\n825\t\n826\t        // Write records\n827\t        for event in &events {\n828\t            wtr.write_record(&[\n829\t                event.timestamp.to_rfc3339().as_str(),\n830\t                &event.event_type.to_string(),\n831\t                &event.actor,\n832\t                &event.entity_type,\n833\t                &event.entity_id,\n834\t                event.old_value.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n835\t                event.new_value.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n836\t                event.metadata.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n837\t                &event.severity.to_string(),\n838\t            ])\n839\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to write CSV record: {}\", e)))?;\n840\t        }\n841\t\n842\t        wtr.flush()\n843\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to flush CSV: {}\", e)))?;\n844\t\n845\t        info!(\n846\t            count = events.len(),\n847\t            path = %output.display(),\n848\t            \"Exported audit logs to CSV\"\n849\t        );\n850\t\n851\t        Ok(())\n852\t    }\n853\t\n854\t    /// Get statistics about the audit log.\n855\t    pub fn stats(&self) -> Result<AuditStats> {\n856\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n857\t            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n858\t        })?;\n859\t\n860\t        let total_events: i64 = conn\n861\t            .query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_logs\", [], |row| row.get(0))\n862\t            .unwrap_or(0);\n863\t\n864\t        let oldest_event: Option<String> = conn\n865\t            .query_row(\n866\t                \"SELECT MIN(timestamp) FROM audit_logs\",\n867\t                [],\n868\t                |row| row.get(0),\n869\t            )\n870\t            .unwrap_or(None);\n871\t\n872\t        let newest_event: Option<String> = conn\n873\t            .query_row(\n874\t                \"SELECT MAX(timestamp) FROM audit_logs\",\n875\t                [],\n876\t                |row| row.get(0),\n877\t            )\n878\t            .unwrap_or(None);\n879\t\n880\t        // Count by event type\n881\t        let mut type_counts = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n882\t        let mut stmt = conn\n883\t            .prepare(\n884\t                \"SELECT event_type, COUNT(*) as count FROM audit_logs GROUP BY event_type\"\n885\t            )\n886\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to prepare stats query: {}\", e)))?;\n887\t\n888\t        let rows = stmt\n889\t            .query_map([], |row| {\n890\t                Ok((\n891\t                    row.get::<_, String>(0)?,\n892\t                    row.get::<_, i64>(1)?\n893\t                ))\n894\t            })\n895\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to execute stats query: {}\", e)))?;\n896\t\n897\t        for row in rows {\n898\t            if let Ok((event_type, count)) = row {\n899\t                type_counts.insert(event_type, count);\n900\t            }\n901\t        }\n902\t\n903\t        Ok(AuditStats {\n904\t            total_events,\n905\t            oldest_timestamp: oldest_event.and_then(|s| DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&s).ok())\n906\t                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc)),\n907\t            newest_timestamp: newest_event.and_then(|s| DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&s).ok())\n908\t                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc)),\n909\t            event_type_counts: type_counts,\n910\t        })\n911\t    }\n912\t\n913\t    /// Vacuum the database to reclaim space.\n914\t    pub fn vacuum(&self) -> Result<()> {\n915\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n916\t            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n917\t        })?;\n918\t\n919\t        info!(\"Vacuuming audit database\");\n920\t\n921\t        conn.execute(\"VACUUM\", [])\n922\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to vacuum: {}\", e)))?;\n923\t\n924\t        Ok(())\n925\t    }\n926\t}\n927\t\n928\t/// Statistics about the audit log.\n929\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n930\tpub struct AuditStats {\n931\t    /// Total number of events\n932\t    pub total_events: i64,\n933\t    /// Oldest event timestamp\n934\t    pub oldest_timestamp: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n935\t    /// Newest event timestamp\n936\t    pub newest_timestamp: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n937\t    /// Count of events by type\n938\t    pub event_type_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, i64>,\n939\t}\n940\t\n941\t#[cfg(test)]\n942\tmod tests {\n943\t    use super::*;\n944\t    use tempfile::tempdir;\n945\t\n946\t    #[test]\n947\t    fn )\n953\t        .with_new_value(r#\"{\"status\":\"active\"}\"#)\n954\t        .with_severity(Severity::Info);\n955\t\n956\t        assert_eq!(event.event_type, EventType::WorkerSpawn);\n957\t Main application state and logic for the FORGE TUI.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! The `App` struct manages overall application state, view switching,\n4\t//! and coordinates between different components.\n5\t//!\n6\t//! # Chat Backend Integration Architecture\n7\t//!\n8\t//! The chat functionality integrates an async `ChatBackend` into the synchronous\n9\t//! TUI event loop using the following design:\n10\t//!\n11\t//! ## Async-to-Sync Bridge\n12\t//!\n13\t//! 1. **Tokio Runtime**: A single-threaded tokio runtime is created during App::new()\n14\t//!    and lives for the duration of the application. This runtime handles all async\n15\t//!    chat operations.\n16\t//!\n17\t//! 2. **Message Passing**: An `mpsc` channel connects async chat tasks to the sync UI:\n18\t//!    - `chat_tx`: Sender held by App, used to submit chat requests\n19\t//!    - `chat_rx`: Receiver held by a background task, processes requests\n20\t//!    - `response_rx`: Receiver held by App, receives responses\n21\t//!\n22\t//! 3. **Non-Blocking Execution**: When user submits chat input:\n23\t//!    - Send request to background task via `chat_tx`\n24\t//!    - Set `chat_pending` flag and display loading indicator\n25\t//!    - Continue UI event loop without blocking\n26\t//!    - Poll `response_rx` each frame to check for responses\n27\t//!\n28\t//! ## State Management\n29\t//!\n30\t//! - `chat_pending: bool` - Whether a request is in flight\n31\t//! - `chat_history: Vec<ChatExchange>` - Last 10 exchanges (user query + response)\n32\t//! - `chat_backend: Option<ChatBackend>` - Initialized from config.yaml\n33\t//!\n34\t//! ## Error Handling\n35\t//!\n36\t//! - Backend initialization failures: App runs without chat (display error in UI)\n37\t//! - Request timeout: Background task implements 30s timeout\n38\t//! - API errors: Captured in response, displayed in chat history as error messages\n39\t//!\n40\t//! ## Performance\n41\t//!\n42\t//! - Background task prevents UI blocking\n43\t//! - Response polling adds <1ms per frame\n44\t//! - Chat history limited to 10 exchanges (~1KB memory)\n45\t\n46\tuse std::io;\n47\tuse std::path::PathBuf;\n48\tuse Activity monitoring for worker health tracking.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module provides comprehensive activity monitoring that distinguishes between:\n4\t//! - **Idle**: Worker waiting for work (no task assigned)\n5\t//! - **Active**: Worker working on a task with recent activity\n6\t//! - **Stuck**: Worker has a task but no activity for > 15 minutes\n7\t//!\n8\t//! ## Heartbeat Files\n9\t//!\n10\t//! |--------|----------|---------------|----------------|\n20\t//! | Active | Yes      | < 15 min      | Working        |\n21\t//! | Active | Yes      | >= 15 min     | **Stuck**      |\n22\t//! | Idle fs::rename(&temp_path, &path).map_err(|e| {\n289\t            ForgeError::io(\"renaming heartbeat file\", &path, e)\n290\t        })?;\n291\t\n292\t        debug!(worker_id = %self.worker_id, /// Read heartbeat data for a worker.\n343\t    pub fn read_heartbeat(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Option<HeartbeatData> {\n344\t        let path = self.config.heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.heartbeat\", worker_id));\n345\t\n346\t        if !path.exists() {\n347\t            return None;\n348\t        }\n349\t\n350\t        match fs::read_to_string(&path) {\n351\t            Ok(content) => match serde_json::from_str(&content) {\n352\t                Ok(data) => Some(data),\n353\t                Err(e) => {\n354\t                    warn!(worker_id, error = %e, \"Failed to parse heartbeat file\");\n355\t                    None\n356\t                }\n357\t            },\n358\t            Err(e) => {\n359\t                warn!(worker_id, error = %e, \"Failed to read heartbeat file\");\n360\t                None\n361\t            }\n362\t        }\n363\t    }\n364\t\n365\t    /// Classify the activity state of a worker.\n366\t    pub fn classify_activity(\n367\t        &self,\n368\t        worker_id: &str,\n369\t        has_task: bool,\n370\t        last_activity: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n371\t        worker_status: &str,\n372\t    ) -> ActivityState {\n373\t        let now = Utc::now();\n374\t        let heartbeat = self.read_heartbeat(worker_id);\n375\t        let timeout = Duration::seconds(self.config.activity_timeout_secs);\n376\t        let heartbeat_stale = Duration::seconds(self.config.heartbeat_stale_secs);\n377\t\n378\t        // Check heartbeat staleness\n379\t        if let Some(ref hb) = heartbeat {\n380\t            let hb_age = now.signed_duration_since(hb.timestamp);\n381\t            if hb_age > heartbeat_stale {\n382\t                // Heartbeat is stale - worker might be unresponsive\n383\t                if has_task {\n384\t                    return ActivityState::Stuck;\n385\t                }\n386\t            }\n387\t        }\n388\t\n389\t        // If worker has no task, it's idle\n390\t        if !has_task {\n391\t            return ActivityState::Idle;\n392\t        }\n393\t\n394\t        // Worker has a task - check activity\n395\t        if let Some(activity) = last_activity {\n396\t            let activity_age = now.signed_duration_since(activity);\n397\t\n398\t            if activity_age > timeout {\n399\t                // No activity for > 15 minutes with a task = stuck\n400\t                return ActivityState::Stuck;\n401\t            } else {\n402\t                // Recent activity = working\n403\t                return ActivityState::Working;\n404\t            }\n405\t        }\n406\t\n407\t        // Has task but no activity timestamp - check heartbeat\n408\t        if let Some(hb) = heartbeat {\n409\t            let hb_age = now.signed_duration_since(hb.timestamp);\n410\t            if hb_age <= heartbeat_stale {\n411\t                return ActivityState::Working;\n412\t            } else {\n413\t                return ActivityState::Stuck;\n414\t            }\n415\t        }\n416\t\n417\t        // Has task, no activity, no heartbeat - likely stuck or starting\n418\t        if worker_status == \"starting\" {\n419\t            ActivityState::Working\n420\t        } else {\n421\t            ActivityState::Unknown\n422\t        }\n423\t    }\n424\t\n425\t    /// Get detailed activity status for a worker.\n426\t    pub fn get_activity(\n427\t        &self,\n428\t        worker_id: &str,\n429\t        has_task: bool,\n430\t        current_task: Option<String>,\n431\t        last_activity: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n432\t        worker_status: &str,\n433\t    ) -> WorkerActivity {\n434\t        let now = Utc::now();\n435\t        let heartbeat = self.read_heartbeat(worker_id);\n436\t\n437\t        let time_since_activity = last_activity.map(|t| now.signed_duration_since(t));\n438\t        let time_since_heartbeat = heartbeat.as_ref().map(|hb| now.signed_duration_since(hb.timestamp));\n439\t\n440\t        let state = self.classify_activity(worker_id, has_task, last_activity, worker_status);\n441\t\n442\t        let recommendation = match state {\n443\t            ActivityState::Stuck => Some(format!(\n444\t                \"Worker has been stuck for {}. Consider restarting.\",\n445\t                time_since_activity\n446\t                    .map(|d| format!(\"{} minutes\", d.num_minutes()))\n447\t                    .unwrap_or_else(|| \"unknown time\".to_string())\n448\t            )),\n449\t            ActivityState::Unresponsive => Some(\"Worker is not responding. Check process health.\".to_string()),\n450\t            _ => None,\n451\t        };\n452\t\n453\t        WorkerActivity {\n454\t            worker_id: worker_id.to_string(),\n455\t            state,\n456\t            time_since_activity,\n457\t            time_since_heartbeat,\n458\t            has_task,\n459\t            current_task,\n460\t            last_activity,\n461\t            last_heartbeat: heartbeat.map(|hb| hb.timestamp),\n462\t            recommendation,\n463\t        }\n464\t    }\n465\t\n466\t    /// Scan all heartbeat files and return activity data.\n467\t    pub fn scan_all_heartbeats(&self) -> HashMap<String, HeartbeatData> {\n468\t        let mut heartbeats = HashMap::new();\n469\t\n470\t        if !self.config.heartbeat_dir.exists() {\n471\t            return heartbeats;\n472\t        }\n473\t\n474\t        if let Ok(entries) = fs::read_dir(&self.config.heartbeat_dir) {\n475\t            for entry in entries.flatten() {\n476\t                let path = entry.path();\n477\t                if path.extension().is_some_and(|ext| ext == \"heartbeat\")\n478\t                    && let Some(stem) = path.file_stem()\n479\t                {\n480\t                    let worker_id = stem.to_string_lossy().to_string();\n481\t                    if let Some(hb) = self.read_heartbeat(&worker_id) {\n482\t                        heartbeats.insert(worker_id, hb);\n483\t                    }\n484\t                }\n485\t            }\n486\t        }\n487\t\n488\t        heartbeats\n489\t    }\n490\t\n491\t    /// Find workers that are potentially stuck.\n492\t    pub fn find_stuck_workers(&self, workers: &[(String, bool, Option<DateTime<Utc>>, String)]) -> Vec<WorkerActivity> {\n493\t        workers\n494\t            .iter()\n495\t            .filter_map(|(worker_id, has_task, last_activity, status)| {\n496\t                let activity = self.get_activity(\n497\t                    worker_id,\n498\t                    *has_task,\n499\t                    None, // We don't have task ID here\n500\t                    *last_activity,\n501\t                    status,\n502\t                );\n503\t                if activity.needs_attention() {\n504\t                    Some(activity)\n505\t                } else {\n506\t                    None\n507\t                }\n508\t            })\n509\t            .collect()\n510\t    }\n511\t\n512\t    /// Clean up stale heartbeat files (workers that no longer exist).\n513\t    pub fn cleanup_stale_heartbeats(&self, active_worker_ids: &[String]) -> Result<usize> {\n514\t        let mut cleaned = 0;\n515\t\n516\t        if !self.config.heartbeat_dir.exists() {\n517\t            return Ok(0);\n518\t        }\n519\t\n520\t        if let Ok(entries) = fs::read_dir(&self.config.heartbeat_dir) {\n521\t            for entry in entries.flatten() {\n522\t                let path = entry.path();\n523\t                if path.extension().is_some_and(|ext| ext == \"heartbeat\")\n524\t                    && let Some(stem) = path.file_stem()\n525\t                {\n526\t                    let worker_id = stem.to_string_lossy().to_string();\n527\t                    if !active_worker_ids.contains(&worker_id) {\n528\t                        // Check if heartbeat is old enough to clean up\n529\t                        if let Some(hb) = self.read_heartbeat(&worker_id) {\n530\t                            let age = Utc::now().signed_duration_since(hb.timestamp);\n531\t                            // Clean up heartbeats older than 1 hour for non-existent workers\n532\t                            if age.num_hours() >= 1 {\n533\t                                if let Err(e) = fs::remove_file(&path) {\n534\t                                    warn!(path = ?path, error = %e, \"Failed to clean up stale heartbeat\");\n535\t                                } else {\n536\t                                    info!(worker_id = %worker_id, \"Cleaned up stale heartbeat file\");\n537\t                                    cleaned += 1;\n538\t                                }\n539\t                            }\n540\t                        }\n541\t                    }\n542\t                }\n543\t            }\n544\t        }\n545\t\n546\t        Ok(cleaned)\n547\t    }\n548\t\n549\t    /// Get the {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_05351a9c83ef4d0f85f70cd4","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! This module provides functionality to read and parse worker status files,\n4\t//! handling missing files, invalid JSON, and partial data gracefully.\n5\t//!\n6\t//! ## Example\n7\t//!\n8\t//! ```no_run\n9\t//! use forge_core::status::{StatusReader, WorkerStatusInfo};\n10\t//!\n11\t//! fn main() -> forge_core::Result<()> {\n12\t//!     let reader = StatusReader::new(None)?;\n13\t//!\n14\t//!     // Read all worker statuses\n15\t//!     let workers = reader.read_all()?;\n16\t//!     for worker in workers {\n17\t//!         println!(\"{}: {}\", worker.worker_id, worker.status);\n18\t//!     }\n19\t//!\n20\t//!     // Read a specific worker's status\n21\t//!     if let Some(worker) = reader.read_worker(\"my-worker\")? {\n22\t//!         println!(\"Worker {} is {}\", worker.worker_id, worker.status);\n23\t//!     }\n24\t//!\n25\t//!     Ok(())\n26\t//! }\n27\t//! ```\n28\t\n29\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n30\t\n31\tuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n32\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n33\tuse tracing::{debug, warn};\n34\t\n35\tuse crate::error::{ForgeError, Result};\n36\tuse crate::types::WorkerStatus;\n37\t\n38\t/// Custom deserializer for current_task field that handles both string and object formats.\n39\t///\n40\t/// Accepts:\n41\t/// - String: \"bd-abc\"\n42\t/// - Object: {\"bead_id\": \"bd-abc\", \"bead_title\": \"...\", \"priority\": 1}\n43\tfn deserialize_current_task<'de, D>(\n44\t    deserializer: D,\n45\t) -> std::result::Result<Option<String>, D::Error>\n46\twhere\n47\t    D: serde::Deserializer<'de>,\n48\t{\n49\t    use serde::de::{self, Deserialize};\n50\t    use serde_json::Value;\n51\t\n52\t    let value = Value::deserialize(deserializer)?;\n53\t\n54\t    match value {\n55\t        Value::Null => Ok(None),\n56\t        Value::String(s) => Ok(Some(s)),\n57\t        Value::Object(map) => {\n58\t            // Extract bead_id from object format\n59\t            if let Some(Value::String(bead_id)) = map.get(\"bead_id\") {\n60\t                Ok(Some(bead_id.clone()))\n61\t            } else {\n62\t                Err(de::Error::custom(\n63\t                    \"current_task object must have bead_id field\",\n64\t                ))\n65\t            }\n66\t        }\n67\t        _ => Err(de::Error::custom(\"current_task must be a string or object\")),\n68\t    }\n69\t}\n70\t\n71\t/// Complete worker status information as stored in status files.\n72\t///\n73\t/// This struct represents the full contents of a worker's status file\n74\t/// at `~/.forge/status/<worker_id>.json`.\n75\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n76\tpub struct WorkerStatusInfo {\n77\t    /// Unique identifier for the worker\n78\t    pub worker_id: String,\n79\t\n80\t    /// Current status of the worker\n81\t    #[serde(default)]\n82\t    pub status: WorkerStatus,\n83\t\n84\t    /// Model being used by this worker (e.g., \"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\")\n85\t    #[serde(default)]\n86\t    pub model: Option<String>,\n87\t\n88\t    /// Workspace directory the worker is operating in\n89\t    #[serde(default)]\n90\t    pub workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n91\t\n92\t    /// Process ID of the worker\n93\t    #[serde(default)]\n94\t    pub pid: Option<u32>,\n95\t\n96\t    /// Timestamp when the worker was started\n97\t    #[serde(default)]\n98\t    pub started_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n99\t\n100\t    /// Timestamp of the worker's last activity\n101\t    #[serde(default)]\n102\t    pub last_activity: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n103\t\n104\t    /// ID of the bead/task currently being processed (handles both string and object formats)\n105\t    #[serde(default, deserialize_with = \"deserialize_current_task\")]\n106\t    pub current_task: Option<String>,\n107\t\n108\t    /// Number of tasks completed by this worker\n109\t    #[serde(default)]\n110\t    pub tasks_completed: u32,\n111\t\n112\t    /// Timestamp when the worker was paused (if status is Paused)\n113\t    #[serde(default)]\n114\t    pub paused_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n115\t\n116\t    /// Reason why the worker was paused (if status is Paused)\n117\t    #[serde(default)]\n118\t    pub pause_reason: Option<String>,\n119\t}\n120\t\n121\timpl WorkerStatusInfo {\n122\t    /// Create a new WorkerStatusInfo with just an ID and status.\n123\t    ///\n124\t    /// This is useful for creating placeholder entries when a status file\n125\t    /// is missing or corrupted.\n126\t    pub fn new(worker_id: impl Into<String>, status: WorkerStatus) -> Self {\n127\t        Self {\n128\t            worker_id: worker_id.into(),\n129\t            status,\n130\t            model: None,\n131\t            workspace: None,\n132\t            pid: None,\n133\t            started_at: None,\n134\t            last_activity: None,\n135\t            current_task: None,\n136\t            tasks_completed: 0,\n137\t            paused_at: None,\n138\t            pause_reason: None,\n139\t        }\n140\t    }\n141\t\n142\t    /// Create an error placeholder for a worker with a corrupted or unreadable status file.\n143\t    pub fn error(worker_id: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n144\t        Self::new(worker_id, WorkerStatus::Error)\n145\t    }\n146\t\n147\t    /// Returns true if the worker is considered healthy.\n148\t    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n149\t        self.status.is_healthy()\n150\t    }\n151\t\n152\t    /// Returns true if the worker appears to be stale (no activity for a long time).\n153\t    ///\n154\t    /// A worker is considered stale if its last_activity is more than 5 minutes ago.\n155\t    pub fn is_stale(&self, threshold_secs: i64) -> bool {\n156\t        match self.last_activity {\n157\t            Some(last) => {\n158\t                let now = Utc::now();\n159\t                let elapsed = now.signed_duration_since(last);\n160\t                elapsed.num_seconds() > threshold_secs\n161\t            }\n162\t            None => false, // No activity data, can't determine staleness\n163\t        }\n164\t    }\n165\t}\n166\t\n167\timpl Default for WorkerStatusInfo {\n168\t    fn default() -> Self {\n169\t        Self::new(\"unknown\", WorkerStatus::Idle)\n170\t    }\n171\t}\n172\t\n173\t/// Reader for worker status files from `~/.forge/status/`.\n174\t///\n175\t/// The reader provides methods to read individual worker statuses or all\n176\t/// workers at once. It handles errors gracefully, returning Error status\n177\t/// for workers with corrupted or unreadable files.\n178\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n179\tpub struct StatusReader {\n180\t    /// Directory containing status files\n181\t    status_dir: PathBuf,\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\timpl StatusReader {\n185\t    /// Create a new StatusReader.\n186\t    ///\n187\t    /// If `status_dir` is None, uses the default `~/.forge/status/` directory.\n188\t    pub fn new(status_dir: Option<PathBuf>) -> Result<Self> {\n189\t        let status_dir = match status_dir {\n190\t            Some(dir) => dir,\n191\t            None => Self::default_status_dir()?,\n192\t        };\n193\t\n194\t        debug!(\"StatusReader initialized with directory: {:?}\", status_dir);\n195\t\n196\t        Ok(Self { status_dir })\n197\t    }\n198\t\n199\t    /// Get the default status directory (`~/.forge/status/`).\n200\t    pub fn default_status_dir() -> Result<PathBuf> {\n201\t        let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").map_err(|_| ForgeError::ConfigMissingField {\n202\t            field: \"HOME environment variable\".to_string(),\n203\t        })?;\n204\t\n205\t        Ok(PathBuf::from(home).join(\".forge\").join(\"status\"))\n206\t    }\n207\t\n208\t    /// Get the path to a worker's status file.\n209\t    pub fn status_file_path(&self, worker_id: &str) -> PathBuf {\n210\t        self.status_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", worker_id))\n211\t    }\n212\t\n213\t    /// Read a specific worker's status file.\n214\t    ///\n215\t    /// Returns `None` if the file doesn't exist.\n216\t    /// Returns `Some(WorkerStatusInfo)` with Error status if the file is corrupted.\n217\t    pub fn read_worker(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<Option<WorkerStatusInfo>> {\n218\t        let path = self.status_file_path(worker_id);\n219\t\n220\t        if !path.exists() {\n221\t            debug!(\"Status file not found: {:?}\", path);\n222\t            return Ok(None);\n223\t        }\n224\t\n225\t        match self.parse_status_file(&path) {\n226\t            Ok(status) => Ok(Some(status)),\n227\t            Err(e) => {\n228\t                warn!(\"Failed to parse status file {:?}: {}\", path, e);\n229\t                Ok(Some(WorkerStatusInfo::error(worker_id)))\n230\t            }\n231\t        }\n232\t    }\n233\t\n234\t    /// Read all worker status files in the status directory.\n235\t    ///\n236\t    /// Returns a list of all workers found. Workers with corrupted files\n237\t    /// will have Error status.\n238\t    ///\n239\t    /// If the status directory doesn't exist, returns an empty list.\n240\t    pub fn read_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<WorkerStatusInfo>> {\n241\t        if !self.status_dir.exists() {\n242\t            debug!(\"Status directory does not exist: {:?}\", self.status_dir);\n243\t            return Ok(Vec::new());\n244\t        }\n245\t\n246\t        let entries = std::fs::read_dir(&self.status_dir).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n247\t            operation: \"reading status directory\".to_string(),\n248\t            path: self.status_dir.clone(),\n249\t            source: e,\n250\t        })?;\n251\t\n252\t        let mut workers = Vec::new();\n253\t\n254\t        for entry in entries {\n255\t            let entry = match entry {\n256\t                Ok(e) => e,\n257\t                Err(e) => {\n258\t                    warn!(\"Failed to read directory entry: {}\", e);\n259\t                    continue;\n260\t                }\n261\t            };\n262\t\n263\t            let path = entry.path();\n264\t\n265\t            // Only process .json files\n266\t            if path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()) != Some(\"json\") {\n267\t                continue;\n268\t            }\n269\t\n270\t            let worker_id = match path.file_stem().and_then(|s| s.to_str()) {\n271\t                Some(id) => id.to_string(),\n272\t                None => continue,\n273\t            };\n274\t\n275\t            match self.parse_status_file(&path) {\n276\t                Ok(status) => workers.push(status),\n277\t                Err(e) => {\n278\t                    warn!(\"Failed to parse status file {:?}: {}\", path, e);\n279\t                    workers.push(WorkerStatusInfo::error(worker_id));\n280\t                }\n281\t            }\n282\t        }\n283\t\n284\t        // Sort by worker_id for consistent ordering\n285\t        workers.sort_by(|a, b| a.worker_id.cmp(&b.worker_id));\n286\t\n287\t        debug!(\"Read {} worker statuses\", workers.len());\n288\t        Ok(workers)\n289\t    }\n290\t\n291\t    /// Parse a status file into a WorkerStatusInfo.\n292\t    fn parse_status_file(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<WorkerStatusInfo> {\n293\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n294\t            operation: \"reading status file\".to_string(),\n295\t            path: path.to_path_buf(),\n296\t            source: e,\n297\t        })?;\n298\t\n299\t        serde_json::from_str(&content).map_err(|e| ForgeError::StatusFileParse {\n300\t            path: path.to_path_buf(),\n301\t            message: e.to_string(),\n302\t        })\n303\t    }\n304\t\n305\t    /// Get list of worker IDs from status files (without parsing).\n306\t    ///\n307\t    /// This is faster than `read_all()` when you only need the worker IDs.\n308\t    pub fn list_workers(&self) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n309\t        if !self.status_dir.exists() {\n310\t            return Ok(Vec::new());\n311\t        }\n312\t\n313\t        let entries = std::fs::read_dir(&self.status_dir).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n314\t            operation: \"listing status directory\".to_string(),\n315\t            path: self.status_dir.clone(),\n316\t            source: e,\n317\t        })?;\n318\t\n319\t        let mut worker_ids = Vec::new();\n320\t\n321\t        for entry in entries.flatten() {\n322\t            let path = entry.path();\n323\t\n324\t            if path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()) != Some(\"json\") {\n325\t                continue;\n326\t            }\n327\t\n328\t            if let Some(id) = path.file_stem().and_then(|s| s.to_str()) {\n329\t                worker_ids.push(id.to_string());\n330\t            }\n331\t        }\n332\t\n333\t        worker_ids.sort();\n334\t        Ok(worker_ids)\n335\t    }\n336\t\n337\t    /// Check if the status directory exists.\n338\t    pub fn status_dir_exists(&self) -> bool {\n339\t        self.status_dir.exists()\n340\t    }\n341\t}\n342\t\n343\t/// Writer for worker status files to `~/.forge/status/`.\n344\t///\n345\t/// The writer provides methods to update individual worker statuses or\n346\t/// perform batch operations on multiple workers.\n347\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n348\tpub struct StatusWriter {\n349\t    /// Directory containing status files\n350\t    status_dir: PathBuf,\n351\t}\n352\t\n353\timpl StatusWriter {\n354\t    /// Create a new StatusWriter.\n355\t    ///\n356\t    /// If `status_dir` is None, uses the default `~/.forge/status/` directory.\n357\t    pub fn new(status_dir: Option<PathBuf>) -> Result<Self> {\n358\t        let status_dir = match status_dir {\n359\t            Some(dir) => dir,\n360\t            None => StatusReader::default_status_dir()?,\n361\t        };\n362\t\n363\t        // Ensure the status directory exists\n364\t        if !status_dir.exists() {\n365\t            std::fs::create_dir_all(&status_dir).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n366\t                operation: \"creating status directory\".to_string(),\n367\t                path: status_dir.clone(),\n368\t                source: e,\n369\t            })?;\n370\t        }\n371\t\n372\t        debug!(\"StatusWriter initialized with directory: {:?}\", status_dir);\n373\t\n374\t        Ok(Self { status_dir })\n375\t    }\n376\t\n377\t    /// Get the path to a worker's status file.\n378\t    pub fn status_file_path(&self, worker_id: &str) -> PathBuf {\n379\t        self.status_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", worker_id))\n380\t    }\n381\t\n382\t    /// Update a worker's status in their status file.\n383\t    ///\n384\t    /// If the status file exists, it reads the existing data and updates only the status.\n385\t    /// If the file doesn't exist, it creates a new status file with the given status.\n386\t    pub fn update_status(&self, worker_id: &str, status: WorkerStatus) -> Result<()> {\n387\t        let path = self.status_file_path(worker_id);\n388\t\n389\t        // Read existing status if file exists, otherwise create new\n390\t        let mut info = if path.exists() {\n391\t            let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&path).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n392\t                operation: \"reading status file\".to_string(),\n393\t                path: path.clone(),\n394\t                source: e,\n395\t            })?;\n396\t\n397\t            serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap_or_else(|_| {\n398\t                WorkerStatusInfo::new(worker_id, status)\n399\t            })\n400\t        } else {\n401\t            WorkerStatusInfo::new(worker_id, status)\n402\t        };\n403\t\n404\t        // Update the status\n405\t        info.status = status;\n406\t        info.last_activity = Some(Utc::now());\n407\t\n408\t        // Write atomically using temp file\n409\t        self.write_status(&path, &info)\n410\t    }\n411\t\n412\t    /// Write a complete worker status info to file.\n413\t    pub fn write_worker(&self, info: &WorkerStatusInfo) -> Result<()> {\n414\t        let path = self.status_file_path(&info.worker_id);\n415\t        self.write_status(&path, info)\n416\t    }\n417\t\n418\t    /// Write status to file atomically.\n419\t    fn write_status(&self, path: &Path, info: &WorkerStatusInfo) -> Result<()> {\n420\t        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(info).map_err(|e| ForgeError::StatusFileParse {\n421\t            path: path.to_path_buf(),\n422\t            message: format!(\"Failed to serialize status: {}\", e),\n423\t        })?;\n424\t\n425\t        // Write to temp file first, then rename for atomicity\n426\t        let temp_path = path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n427\t        std::fs::write(&temp_path, &json).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n428\t            operation: \"writing status file\".to_string(),\n429\t            path: temp_path.clone(),\n430\t            source: e,\n431\t        })?;\n432\t\n433\t        std::fs::rename(&temp_path, path).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n434\t            operation: \"renaming status file\".to_string(),\n435\t            path: path.to_path_buf(),\n436\t            source: e,\n437\t        })?;\n438\t\n439\t        debug!(\"Wrote status file: {:?}\", path);\n440\t        Ok(())\n441\t    }\n442\t\n443\t    /// Pause a worker by setting their status to Paused.\n444\t    pub fn pause_worker(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n445\t        self.pause_worker_with_reason(worker_id, None)\n446\t    }\n447\t\n448\t    /// Pause a worker with a specific reason.\n449\t    ///\n450\t    /// The pause reason is stored in the status file and can be displayed\n451\t    /// in health reports and monitoring tools.\n452\t    pub fn pause_worker_with_reason(\n453\t        &self,\n454\t        worker_id: &str,\n455\t        reason: Option<&str>,\n456\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n457\t        let path = self.status_file_path(worker_id);\n458\t\n459\t        // Read existing status if file exists, otherwise create new\n460\t        let mut info = if path.exists() {\n461\t            let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&path).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n462\t                operation: \"reading status file\".to_string(),\n463\t                path: path.clone(),\n464\t                source: e,\n465\t            })?;\n466\t\n467\t            serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap_or_else(|_| {\n468\t                WorkerStatusInfo::new(worker_id, WorkerStatus::Paused)\n469\t            })\n470\t        } else {\n471\t            WorkerStatusInfo::new(worker_id, WorkerStatus::Paused)\n472\t        };\n473\t\n474\t        // Update the status and pause fields\n475\t        info.status = WorkerStatus::Paused;\n476\t        info.last_activity = Some(Utc::now());\n477\t        info.paused_at = Some(Utc::now());\n478\t        info.pause_reason = reason.map(|s| s.to_string());\n479\t\n480\t        // Write atomically using temp file\n481\t        self.write_status(&path, &info)\n482\t    }\n483\t\n484\t    /// Resume a worker by setting their status to Idle.\n485\t    ///\n486\t    /// This clears the paused_at and pause_reason fields.\n487\t    pub fn resume_worker(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n488\t        let path = self.status_file_path(worker_id);\n489\t\n490\t        // Read existing status if file exists, otherwise create new\n491\t        let mut info = if path.exists() {\n492\t            let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&path).map_err(|e| ForgeError::Io {\n493\t                operation: \"reading status file\".to_string(),\n494\t                path: path.clone(),\n495\t                source: e,\n496\t            })?;\n497\t\n498\t            serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap_or_else(|_| {\n499\t                WorkerStatusInfo::new(worker_id, WorkerStatus::Idle)\n500\t            })\n501\t        } else {\n502\t            WorkerStatusInfo::new(worker_id, WorkerStatus::Idle)\n503\t        };\n504\t\n505\t        // Update the status and clear pause fields\n506\t        info.status = WorkerStatus::Idle;\n507\t        info.last_activity = Some(Utc::now());\n508\t        info.paused_at = None;\n509\t        info.pause_reason = None;\n510\t\n511\t        // Write atomically using temp file\n512\t        self.write_status(&path, &info)\n513\t    }\n514\t\n515\t    /// Pause all workers with status files in the status directory.\n516\t    ///\n517\t    /// Returns the number of workers paused.\n518\t    pub fn pause_all(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n519\t        let reader = StatusReader::new(Some(self.status_dir.clone()))?;\n520\t        let workers = reader.list_workers()?;\n521\t        let mut count = 0;\n522\t\n523\t        for worker_id in workers {\n524\t            if let Ok(Some(info)) = reader.read_worker(&worker_id) {\n525\t                // Only pause if not already paused, stopped, or failed\n526\t                if info.status != WorkerStatus::Paused\n527\t                    && info.status != WorkerStatus::Stopped\n528\t                    && info.status != WorkerStatus::Failed\n529\t                {\n530\t                    self.pause_worker(&worker_id)?;\n531\t                    count += 1;\n532\t                }\n533\t            }\n534\t        }\n535\t\n536\t        Ok(count)\n537\t    }\n538\t\n539\t    /// Resume all paused workers in the status directory.\n540\t    ///\n541\t    /// Returns the number of workers resumed.\n542\t    pub fn resume_all(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n543\t        let reader = StatusReader::new(Some(self.status_dir.clone()))?;\n544\t        let workers = reader.list_workers()?;\n545\t        let mut count = 0;\n546\t\n547\t        for worker_id in workers {\n548\t            if let Ok(Some(info)) = reader.read_worker(&worker_id) {\n549\t                // Only resume if currently paused\n550\t                if info.status == WorkerStatus::Paused {\n551\t                    self.resume_worker(&worker_id)?;\n552\t                    count += 1;\n553\t                }\n554\t            }\n555\t        }\n556\t\n557\t        Ok(count)\n558\t    }\n559\t}\n560\t\n561\t#[cfg(test)]\n562\tmod tests {\n563\t    use super::*;\n564\t    use tempfile::TempDir;\n565\t\n566\t    fn  }\n570\t\n571\t    #[test]\n572\t    fn test_worker_status_info_new() {\n573\t        let status = WorkerStatusInfo::new(\"test-worker\", WorkerStatus::Active);\n574\t }\n80\t\n81\t    /// Get the color for this event type.\n82\t    pub fn color(&self) -> Color {\n83\t        match self {\n84\t            ActivityEventType::WorkerSpawn => Color::Green,\n85\t }\n147\t    }\n148\t}\n149\t\n150\t/// Activity log data with ring buffer.\n151\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n152\tpub struct ActivityLogData {\n153\t    /// Ring buffer of entries\n154\t    entries: Self::default()\n189\t    }\n190\t\n191\t    /// Push a new entry to the log.\n192\t    pub fn push(&mut self, entry: ActivityEntry) {\n193\t        // If at capacity, remove oldest\n194\t        if self.entries.len() >= self.capacity {\n195\t            self.entries.pop_front();\n196\t        }\n197\t        self.entries.push_back(entry);\n198\t        self.total_added += 1;\n199\t if self.auto_scroll_paused && self.scroll_offset > 0 {\n204\t            self.scroll_offset += 1;\n205\t        }\n206\t    }\n207\t\n208\t    /// Push multiple entries.\n209\t    pub fn self.entries.is_empty()\n223\t    }\n224\t\n225\t    /// Get the capacity.\n226\t    pub fn capacity(&self) -> usize {\n227\t        self.capacity\n228\t    }\n229\t\n230\t    /// Get total entries {\n237\t        let max_scroll = self.entries.len().saturating_sub(1);\n238\t        self.scroll_offset = (self.scroll_offset + n).min(max_scroll);\n239\t        if self.scroll_offset > 0 {\n240\t            self.auto_scroll_paused = true;\n241\t        }\n242\t    }\n243\t\n244\t    /// Scroll down by N lines.\n245\t    pub fn scroll_down(&mut self, n: usize) {\n246\t        self.scroll_offset = self.scroll_offset.saturating_sub(n);\n247\t        if self.scroll_offset == 0 {\n248\t            self.auto_scroll_paused = false;\n249\t        }\n250\t    }\n251\t\n252\t    /// Scroll to the to the top.\n259\t    pub fn scroll_to_top(&mut self) {\n260\t        self.scroll_offset = self.entries.len().saturating_sub(1);\n261\t        self.auto_scroll_paused = true;\n262\t    }\n263\t\n264\t    /// Check if auto-scroll is paused.\n265\t    pub fn is_auto_scroll_paused(&self) -> bool {\n266\t        self.auto_scroll_paused\n267\t    }\n268\t\n269\t    /// Get current scroll offset.\n270\t    pub fn scroll_offset(&self) -> usize {\n271\t        self.scroll_offset\n272\t    }\n273\t\n274\t    /// Get visible entries for rendering.\n275\t    ///\n276\t    /// Returns entries in reverse order (newest first) with scroll offset applied.\n277\t    pub fn visible_entries(&self, max_lines: usize) -> Vec<&ActivityEntry> {\n278\t        let total = self.entries.len();\n279\t        if total == 0 {\n280\t            return Vec::new();\n281\t        }\n282\t\n283\t        // Calculate start index based on scroll offset\n284\t        // scroll_offset = 0 means show newest entries (bottom)\n285\t        // scroll_offset > 0 means scroll back from newest\n286\t        let start_idx = if self.scroll_offset >= total {\n287\t            0\n288\t        } else {\n289\t            total.saturating_sub(self.scroll_offset + max_lines)\n290\t        };\n291\t\n292\t        // Get entries from newest to oldest, bounded by max_lines\n293\t        let end_idx = total.saturating_sub(self.scroll_offset);\n294\t\n295\t        self.entries\n296\t            .iter()\n297\t            .skip(start_idx)\n298\t            .take(end_idx.saturating_sub(start_idx).min(max_lines))\n299\t            .collect()\n300\t    }\n301\t\n302\t    /// Get all entries (oldest first).\n303\t    pub fn iter(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &ActivityEntry> {\n304\t        self.entries.iter()\n305\t    }\n306\t\n307\t    /// Clear all entries.\n308\t    pub fn clear(&mut self) {\n309\t        self.entries.clear();\n310\t        self.scroll_offset = 0;\n311\t        self.auto_scroll_paused = false;\n312\t    }\n313\t\n314\t    /// Get the last entry.\n315\t    pub fn last(&self) -> Option<&ActivityEntry> {\n316\t        self.entries.back()\n317\t    }\n318\t\n319\t    /// Check if we have any entries.\n320\t    pub fn has_entries(&self) -> bool {\n321\t        !self.entries.is_empty()\n322\t    }\n323\t\n324\t    /// entry.event_type.icon();\n355\t        let color = entry.event_type.color();\n356\t\n357\t        let mut spans = Vec::new();\n358\t\n359\t        // Timestamp (dim)\n360\t        spans.push(Span::styled(\n361\t            format!(\"{} \", _time),\n362\t            Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),\n363\t        ));\n364\t\n365\t        // Icon with event color\n366\t present) in cyan\n372\t        if let Some(ref source) = entry.source {\n373\t            spans.push(Span::styled(\n374\t                format!(\"[{}] \", source),\n375\t                Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan),\n376\t            ));\n377\t        }\n378\t\n379\t        // Message in appropriate color based on event type\n380\t        let msg_color = match entry.event_type {\n381\t            ActivityEventType::Error => Color::Red,\n382\t            ActivityEventType::Warning => Color::Yellow,\n383\t            _ => Color::White,\n384\t        };\n385\t        spans.push(Span::styled(entry.message.clone(), Style::default().fg(msg_color)));\n386\t\n387\t        Line::from(spans)\n388\t    }\n389\t}\n390\t\n391\timpl Widget for ActivityPanel<'_> {\n392\t    fn render(self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n393\t        // Enhanced focus indicator: \"▶\" arrow for focused (clearer than diamond), \"▪\" for unfocused\n394\t        let focus_icon = if self.focused { \"▶\" } else { \"▪\" };\n395\t\n396\t        // Border type: Double border for focused (highly visible), Normal for unfocused\n397\t        let border_type = if self.focused {\n398\t            BorderType::Double\n399\t        } else {\n400\t            BorderType::Plain\n401\t        };\n402\t\n403\t        // Border style: bright cyan with bold for focused, dim for unfocused\n404\t        let border_style = if self.focused {\n405\t            Style::default()\n406\t                .fg(Color::Cyan)\n407\t                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)\n408\t        } else {\n409\t            Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)\n410\t        };\n411\t\n412\t        // Title style: bold + underlined for focused, dim for unfocused\n413\t        let title_style = if self.focused {\n414\t            Style::default()\n415\t                .fg(Color::Cyan)\n416\t                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD | Modifier::UNDERLINED)\n417\t        } else {\n418\t            Style::default().fg(Color::Rgb(80, 80, 80))\n419\t        };\n420\t\n421\t        // Build title with status indicators and focus icon\n422\t        let title = if self.data.is_auto_scroll_paused() {\n423\t            format!(\n424\t                \" {} Activity Log [paused - {}/{}] \",\n425\t                focus_icon,\n426\t                self.data.scroll_offset(),\n427\t                self.data.len()\n428\t            )\n429\t        } else if self.data.has_entries() {\n430\t            format!(\" {} Activity Log [{}] \", focus_icon, self.data.len())\n431\t        } else {\n432\t            format!(\" {} Activity Log \", focus_icon)\n433\t        };\n434\t\n435\t        let block = Block::default()\n436\t            .borders(Borders::ALL)\n437\t            .border_type(border_type)\n438\t            .border_style(border_style)\n439\t            .title(Span::styled(title, title_style));\n440\t\n441\t        let inner = block.inner(area);\n442\t        block.render(area, buf);\n443\t\n444\t        // Handle empty state\n445\t        if !self.data.has_entries() {\n446\t            let empty_msg = Paragraph::new(\n447\t                \"No recent activity.\\n\\n\\\n448\t                 Activity will appear here as workers\\n\\\n449\t                 start, complete tasks, and update status.\\n\\n\\\n450\t                 [↑/↓] Scroll  [G] Top  [Shift+G] Bottom\",\n451\t            )\n452\t            .style(Style::default().fg(Color::Gray));\n453\t            empty_msg.render(inner, buf);\n454\t            return;\n455\t        }\n456\t\n457\t        // Calculate how many lines we can display\n458\t        let max_lines = inner.height as usize;\n459\t\n460\t        // Get visible entries (in reverse order for display)\n461\t        let visible = self.data.visible_entries(max_lines);\n462\t\n463\t        // Build lines from entries (newest at bottom)\n464\t        let lines: Vec<Line> = visible.into_iter().rev().map(|e| self.format_entry_line(e)).collect();\n465\t\n466\t        let paragraph = Paragraph::new(lines);\n467\t        paragraph.render(inner, buf);\n468\t    }\n469\t}\n470\t\n471\t/// Compact activity fn render(self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n485\t        if !self.data.has_entries() {\n486\t            let no_activity =\n487\t                Paragraph::new(\"No activity\").style(Style::default().fg(Color::Gray));\n488\t            no_activity.render(area, buf);\n489\t            return;\n490\t        }\n491\t\n492\t        // Show last 3 entries in compact form\n493\t        let entries: Vec<_> = self.data.entries.iter().rev().take(3).collect();\n494\t\n495\t        let mut lines = Vec::new();\n496\t        for entry in entries {\n497\t            let time = entry.timestamp.with_timezone(&Local).format(\"%H:%M\");\n498\t            let icon = entry.event_type.icon();\n499\t            let color = entry.event_type.color();\n500\t\n501\t            // Truncate message if needed\n502\t            let max_msg_len = (area.width as usize).saturating_sub(15);\n503\t            let msg = if entry.message.len() > max_msg_len {\n504\t                format!(\"{}…\", &entry.message[..max_msg_len.saturating_sub(1)])\n505\t            } else {\n506\t                entry.message.clone()\n507\t            };\n508\t\n509\t            lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n510\t                Span::styled(time.to_string(), Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),\n511\t                Span::raw(\" \"),\n512\t                Span::styled(icon.to_string(), Style::default().fg(color)),\n513\t                Span::raw(\" \"),\n514\t                Span::styled(msg, Style::default().fg(Color::White)),\n515\t            ]));\n516\t        }\n517\t\n518\t        let paragraph = Paragraph::new(lines);\n519\t        paragraph.render(area, buf);\n520\t    }\n521\t}\n522\t\n523\t#[cfg(test)]\n524\tmod tests {\n525\t
