{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_b835f469a8044d76bcc5cef1","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Log streaming and buffer management for the FORGE TUI.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module provides real-time log streaming from worker log files\n4\t//! using async file tailing and a ring buffer to prevent unbounded memory growth.\n5\t//!\n6\t//! ## Architecture (per ADR 0008)\n7\t//!\n8\t//! - **Async tail**: 100ms polling interval per log file\n9\t//! - **Ring buffer**: VecDeque<LogEntry> with configurable capacity (default: 1000)\n10\t//! - **Batch updates**: Flush N entries at once to UI\n11\t//! - **Log rotation handling**: Detect inode changes, reopen file\n12\t//!\n13\t//! ## Example\n14\t//!\n15\t//! ```no_run\n16\t//! use forge_tui::log::{LogBuffer, LogEntry, LogLevel};\n17\t//!\n18\t//! let mut buffer = LogBuffer::new(100);\n19\t//! buffer.push(LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Info, \"Worker started\".to_string()));\n20\t//! assert_eq!(buffer.len(), 1);\n21\t//! ```\n22\t\n23\tuse std::collections::VecDeque;\n24\tuse std::path::PathBuf;\n25\tuse std::sync::mpsc::Sender;\n26\t\n27\tuse chrono::{DateTime, Local, Utc};\n28\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n29\tuse thiserror::Error;\n30\tuse tracing::{debug, warn};\n31\t\n32\t/// Default ring buffer capacity (1000 log entries per ADR 0008).\n33\tpub const DEFAULT_BUFFER_CAPACITY: usize = 1000;\n34\t\n35\t/// Default polling interval in milliseconds (100ms per ADR 0008).\n36\tpub const DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS: u64 = 100;\n37\t\n38\t/// Default batch size for UI updates (10 entries per ADR 0008).\n39\tpub const DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE: usize = 10;\n40\t\n41\t/// Errors that can occur during log streaming.\n42\t#[derive(Error, Debug)]\n43\tpub enum LogError {\n44\t    /// Failed to open log file\n45\t    #[error(\"Failed to open log file {path}: {source}\")]\n46\t    OpenFile {\n47\t        path: PathBuf,\n48\t        #[source]\n49\t        source: std::io::Error,\n50\t    },\n51\t\n52\t    /// Failed to read log file\n53\t    #[error(\"Failed to read log file {path}: {source}\")]\n54\t    ReadFile {\n55\t        path: PathBuf,\n56\t        #[source]\n57\t        source: std::io::Error,\n58\t    },\n59\t\n60\t    /// Failed to parse log entry\n61\t    #[error(\"Failed to parse log entry: {message}\")]\n62\t    ParseError { message: String },\n63\t\n64\t    /// Log file was rotated (inode changed)\n65\t    #[error(\"Log file rotated: {path}\")]\n66\t    FileRotated { path: PathBuf },\n67\t}\n68\t\n69\t/// Result type for log operations.\n70\tpub type LogResult<T> = Result<T, LogError>;\n71\t\n72\t/// Log severity levels.\n73\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]\n74\t#[serde(rename_all = \"lowercase\")]\n75\tpub enum LogLevel {\n76\t    /// Trace level (most verbose)\n77\t    Trace,\n78\t    /// Debug level\n79\t    Debug,\n80\t    /// Informational level\n81\t    #[default]\n82\t    Info,\n83\t    /// Warning level\n84\t    Warn,\n85\t    /// Error level\n86\t    Error,\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\timpl LogLevel {\n90\t    /// Parse a log level from a string.\n91\t    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Self {\n92\t        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {\n93\t            \"trace\" => LogLevel::Trace,\n94\t            \"debug\" => LogLevel::Debug,\n95\t            \"info\" => LogLevel::Info,\n96\t            \"warn\" | \"warning\" => LogLevel::Warn,\n97\t            \"error\" | \"err\" => LogLevel::Error,\n98\t            _ => LogLevel::Info,\n99\t        }\n100\t    }\n101\t\n102\t    /// Get the display symbol for this level.\n103\t    pub fn symbol(&self) -> &'static str {\n104\t        match self {\n105\t            LogLevel::Trace => \"→\",\n106\t            LogLevel::Debug => \"○\",\n107\t            LogLevel::Info => \"●\",\n108\t            LogLevel::Warn => \"⚠\",\n109\t            LogLevel::Error => \"✖\",\n110\t        }\n111\t    }\n112\t\n113\t    /// Get the ANSI color code for this level.\n114\t    pub fn color(&self) -> &'static str {\n115\t        match self {\n116\t            LogLevel::Trace => \"gray\",\n117\t            LogLevel::Debug => \"blue\",\n118\t            LogLevel::Info => \"green\",\n119\t            LogLevel::Warn => \"yellow\",\n120\t            LogLevel::Error => \"red\",\n121\t        }\n122\t    }\n123\t}\n124\t\n125\timpl std::fmt::Display for LogLevel {\n126\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n127\t        match self {\n128\t            LogLevel::Trace => write!(f, \"TRACE\"),\n129\t            LogLevel::Debug => write!(f, \"DEBUG\"),\n130\t            LogLevel::Info => write!(f, \"INFO\"),\n131\t            LogLevel::Warn => write!(f, \"WARN\"),\n132\t            LogLevel::Error => write!(f, \"ERROR\"),\n133\t        }\n134\t    }\n135\t}\n136\t\n137\t/// A parsed log entry.\n138\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n139\tpub struct LogEntry {\n140\t    /// Timestamp of the log entry\n141\t    #[serde(default = \"default_timestamp\")]\n142\t    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,\n143\t\n144\t    /// Log severity level\n145\t    #[serde(default)]\n146\t    pub level: LogLevel,\n147\t\n148\t    /// Log message content\n149\t    pub message: String,\n150\t\n151\t    /// Source of the log (worker ID, component, etc.)\n152\t    #[serde(default)]\n153\t    pub source: Option<String>,\n154\t\n155\t    /// Target/module that generated the log\n156\t    #[serde(default)]\n157\t    pub target: Option<String>,\n158\t\n159\t    /// Additional structured fields\n160\t    #[serde(default, flatten)]\n161\t    pub fields: std::collections::HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n162\t}\n163\t\n164\tfn default_timestamp() -> DateTime<Utc> {\n165\t    Utc::now()\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\timpl LogEntry {\n169\t    /// Create a new log entry with the current timestamp.\n170\t    pub fn new(level: LogLevel, message: String) -> Self {\n171\t        Self {\n172\t            timestamp: Utc::now(),\n173\t            level,\n174\t            message,\n175\t            source: None,\n176\t            target: None,\n177\t            fields: std::collections::HashMap::new(),\n178\t        }\n179\t    }\n180\t\n181\t    /// Create a log entry with a specific source.\n182\t    pub fn with_source(mut self, source: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n183\t        self.source = Some(source.into());\n184\t        self\n185\t    }\n186\t\n187\t    /// Create a log entry with a specific target.\n188\t    pub fn with_target(mut self, target: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n189\t        self.target = Some(target.into());\n190\t        self\n191\t    }\n192\t\n193\t    /// Parse a log entry from a JSON line.\n194\t    pub fn from_json(line: &str) -> LogResult<Self> {\n195\t        serde_json::from_str(line).map_err(|e| LogError::ParseError {\n196\t            message: format!(\"Invalid JSON: {}\", e),\n197\t        })\n198\t    }\n199\t\n200\t    /// Parse a log entry from a simple text format.\n201\t    ///\n202\t    /// Expected format: `TIMESTAMP [LEVEL] MESSAGE`\n203\t    /// Example: `2026-02-08T14:23:45Z [INFO] Worker started`\n204\t    pub fn from_text(line: &str) -> LogResult<Self> {\n205\t        let line = line.trim();\n206\t        if line.is_empty() {\n207\t            return Err(LogError::ParseError {\n208\t                message: \"Empty line\".to_string(),\n209\t            });\n210\t        }\n211\t\n212\t        // Try to parse timestamp at the beginning\n213\t        let (timestamp, rest) =\n214\t            if line.len() > 20 && line.chars().take(4).all(|c| c.is_ascii_digit()) {\n215\t                // Looks like ISO timestamp\n216\t                if let Some(space_idx) = line.find(' ') {\n217\t                    if let Ok(ts) = DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&line[..space_idx]) {\n218\t                        (ts.with_timezone(&Utc), &line[space_idx + 1..])\n219\t                    } else {\n220\t                        (Utc::now(), line)\n221\t                    }\n222\t                } else {\n223\t                    (Utc::now(), line)\n224\t                }\n225\t            } else {\n226\t                (Utc::now(), line)\n227\t            };\n228\t\n229\t        // Try to parse level in brackets\n230\t        let (level, message) = if rest.starts_with('[') {\n231\t            if let Some(close_idx) = rest.find(']') {\n232\t                let level_str = &rest[1..close_idx];\n233\t                let msg = rest[close_idx + 1..].trim();\n234\t                (LogLevel::from_str(level_str), msg.to_string())\n235\t            } else {\n236\t                (LogLevel::Info, rest.to_string())\n237\t            }\n238\t        } else {\n239\t            (LogLevel::Info, rest.to_string())\n240\t        };\n241\t\n242\t        Ok(Self {\n243\t            timestamp,\n244\t            level,\n245\t            message,\n246\t            source: None,\n247\t            target: None,\n248\t            fields: std::collections::HashMap::new(),\n249\t        })\n250\t    }\n251\t\n252\t    /// Try to parse a log entry from a line, falling back gracefully.\n253\t    ///\n254\t    /// First tries JSON parsing, then text format, then creates a raw entry.\n255\t    pub fn parse(line: &str) -> Self {\n256\t        let line = line.trim();\n257\t        if line.is_empty() {\n258\t            return Self::new(LogLevel::Info, String::new());\n259\t        }\n260\t\n261\t        // Try JSON first\n262\t        if line.starts_with('{') {\n263\t            if let Ok(entry) = Self::from_json(line) {\n264\t                return entry;\n265\t            }\n266\t        }\n267\t\n268\t        // Try text format\n269\t        if let Ok(entry) = Self::from_text(line) {\n270\t            return entry;\n271\t        }\n272\t\n273\t        // Fallback: treat entire line as message\n274\t        Self::new(LogLevel::Info, line.to_string())\n275\t    }\n276\t\n277\t    /// Format the entry for display.\n278\t    pub fn format_display(&self) -> String {\n279\t        let ts = self.timestamp.with_timezone(&Local).format(\"%H:%M:%S\");\n280\t        let source = self.source.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\");\n281\t        let source_prefix = if source.is_empty() {\n282\t            String::new()\n283\t        } else {\n284\t            format!(\"[{}] \", source)\n285\t        };\n286\t\n287\t        format!(\n288\t            \"{} {} {}{}\",\n289\t            ts,\n290\t            self.level.symbol(),\n291\t            source_prefix,\n292\t            self.message\n293\t        )\n294\t    }\n295\t}\n296\t\n297\t/// A ring buffer for log entries.\n298\t///\n299\t/// Uses VecDeque with a maximum capacity. When the buffer is full,\n300\t/// new entries push out the oldest entries.\n301\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n302\tpub struct LogBuffer {\n303\t    /// The underlying buffer\n304\t    entries: VecDeque<LogEntry>,\n305\t    /// Maximum capacity\n306\t    capacity: usize,\n307\t    /// Total entries ever added (for stats)\n308\t    total_added: usize,\n309\t    /// Entries dropped due to capacity limits\n310\t    dropped_count: usize,\n311\t}\n312\t\n313\timpl LogBuffer {\n314\t    /// Create a new log buffer with the specified capacity.\n315\t    pub fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self {\n316\t        Self {\n317\t            entries: VecDeque::with_capacity(capacity.min(10000)), // Cap allocation\n318\t            capacity,\n319\t            total_added: 0,\n320\t            dropped_count: 0,\n321\t        }\n322\t    }\n323\t\n324\t    /// Create a new log buffer with default capacity (1000 entries).\n325\t    pub fn default_capacity() -> Self {\n326\t        Self::new(DEFAULT_BUFFER_CAPACITY)\n327\t    }\n328\t\n329\t    /// Push a log entry to the buffer.\n330\t    ///\n331\t    /// If the buffer is at capacity, the oldest entry is removed.\n332\t    pub fn push(&mut self, entry: LogEntry) {\n333\t        if self.entries.len() >= self.capacity {\n334\t            self.entries.pop_front();\n335\t            self.dropped_count += 1;\n336\t        }\n337\t        self.entries.push_back(entry);\n338\t        self.total_added += 1;\n339\t    }\n340\t\n341\t    /// Push multiple entries to the buffer.\n342\t    pub fn push_batch(&mut self, entries: impl IntoIterator<Item = LogEntry>) {\n343\t        for entry in entries {\n344\t            self.push(entry);\n345\t        }\n346\t    }\n347\t\n348\t    /// Get the number of entries in the buffer.\n349\t    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {\n350\t        self.entries.len()\n351\t    }\n352\t\n353\t    /// Check if the buffer is empty.\n354\t    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n355\t        self.entries.is_empty()\n356\t    }\n357\t\n358\t    /// Get the buffer capacity.\n359\t    pub fn capacity(&self) -> usize {\n360\t        self.capacity\n361\t    }\n362\t\n363\t    /// Get the total number of entries ever added.\n364\t    pub fn total_added(&self) -> usize {\n365\t        self.total_added\n366\t    }\n367\t\n368\t    /// Get the number of entries dropped due to capacity limits.\n369\t    pub fn dropped_count(&self) -> usize {\n370\t        self.dropped_count\n371\t    }\n372\t\n373\t    /// Check if the buffer is at capacity.\n374\t    pub fn is_full(&self) -> bool {\n375\t        self.entries.len() >= self.capacity\n376\t    }\n377\t\n378\t    /// Get an iterator over the entries (oldest first).\n379\t    pub fn iter(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &LogEntry> {\n380\t        self.entries.iter()\n381\t    }\n382\t\n383\t    /// Get the last N entries (most recent).\n384\t    pub fn last_n(&self, n: usize) -> impl Iterator<Item = &LogEntry> {\n385\t        let skip = self.entries.len().saturating_sub(n);\n386\t        self.entries.iter().skip(skip)\n387\t    }\n388\t\n389\t    /// Get entries filtered by log level.\n390\t    pub fn filter_level(&self, min_level: LogLevel) -> Vec<&LogEntry> {\n391\t        let min_ord = match min_level {\n392\t            LogLevel::Trace => 0,\n393\t            LogLevel::Debug => 1,\n394\t            LogLevel::Info => 2,\n395\t            LogLevel::Warn => 3,\n396\t            LogLevel::Error => 4,\n397\t        };\n398\t\n399\t        self.entries\n400\t            .iter()\n401\t            .filter(|e| {\n402\t                let e_ord = match e.level {\n403\t                    LogLevel::Trace => 0,\n404\t                    LogLevel::Debug => 1,\n405\t                    LogLevel::Info => 2,\n406\t                    LogLevel::Warn => 3,\n407\t                    LogLevel::Error => 4,\n408\t                };\n409\t                e_ord >= min_ord\n410\t            })\n411\t            .collect()\n412\t    }\n413\t\n414\t    /// Get entries filtered by source.\n415\t    pub fn filter_source(&self, source: &str) -> Vec<&LogEntry> {\n416\t        self.entries\n417\t            .iter()\n418\t            .filter(|e| e.source.as_deref() == Some(source))\n419\t            .collect()\n420\t    }\n421\t\n422\t    /// Clear all entries from the buffer.\n423\t    pub fn clear(&mut self) {\n424\t        self.entries.clear();\n425\t        // Don't reset total_added or dropped_count for stats continuity\n426\t    }\n427\t\n428\t    /// Get the oldest entry.\n429\t    pub fn oldest(&self) -> Option<&LogEntry> {\n430\t        self.entries.front()\n431\t    }\n432\t\n433\t    /// Get the newest entry.\n434\t    pub fn newest(&self) -> Option<&LogEntry> {\n435\t        self.entries.back()\n436\t    }\n437\t\n438\t    /// Convert to a Vec for serialization or display.\n439\t    pub fn to_vec(&self) -> Vec<LogEntry> {\n440\t        self.entries.iter().cloned().collect()\n441\t    }\n442\t}\n443\t\n444\timpl Default for LogBuffer {\n445\t    fn default() -> Self {\n446\t        Self::default_capacity()\n447\t    }\n448\t}\n449\t\n450\t/// Events emitted by the log tailer.\n451\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n452\tpub enum LogEvent {\n453\t    /// New log entries received\n454\t    NewEntries(Vec<LogEntry>),\n455\t\n456\t    /// Log file was rotated\n457\t    FileRotated { path: PathBuf },\n458\t\n459\t    /// Error reading log file\n460\t    Error { path: PathBuf, error: String },\n461\t\n462\t    /// Tailer started\n463\t    Started { path: PathBuf },\n464\t\n465\t    /// Tailer stopped\n466\t    Stopped { path: PathBuf },\n467\t}\n468\t\n469\t/// Configuration for the log tailer.\n470\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n471\tpub struct LogTailerConfig {\n472\t    /// Path to the log file\n473\t    pub path: PathBuf,\n474\t\n475\t    /// Polling interval in milliseconds\n476\t    pub poll_interval_ms: u64,\n477\t\n478\t    /// Batch size for sending entries\n479\t    pub batch_size: usize,\n480\t\n481\t    /// Whether to start from end of file (tail -f behavior)\n482\t    pub start_from_end: bool,\n483\t\n484\t    /// Source identifier for log entries\n485\t    pub source: Option<String>,\n486\t}\n487\t\n488\timpl Default for LogTailerConfig {\n489\t    fn default() -> Self {\n490\t        Self {\n491\t            path: PathBuf::new(),\n492\t            poll_interval_ms: DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS,\n493\t            batch_size: DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE,\n494\t            start_from_end: true,\n495\t            source: None,\n496\t        }\n497\t    }\n498\t}\n499\t\n500\timpl LogTailerConfig {\n501\t    /// Create a new config for the given path.\n502\t    pub fn new(path: impl Into<PathBuf>) -> Self {\n503\t        Self {\n504\t            path: path.into(),\n505\t            ..Default::default()\n506\t        }\n507\t    }\n508\t\n509\t    /// Set the polling interval.\n510\t    pub fn with_poll_interval_ms(mut self, ms: u64) -> Self {\n511\t        self.poll_interval_ms = ms;\n512\t        self\n513\t    }\n514\t\n515\t    /// Set the batch size.\n516\t    pub fn with_batch_size(mut self, size: usize) -> Self {\n517\t        self.batch_size = size;\n518\t        self\n519\t    }\n520\t\n521\t    /// Set whether to start from end of file.\n522\t    pub fn with_start_from_end(mut self, start_from_end: bool) -> Self {\n523\t        self.start_from_end = start_from_end;\n524\t        self\n525\t    }\n526\t\n527\t    /// Set the source identifier.\n528\t    pub fn with_source(mut self, source: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n529\t        self.source = Some(source.into());\n530\t        self\n531\t    }\n532\t}\n533\t\n534\t/// Synchronous log tailer for testing and simple use cases.\n535\t///\n536\t/// For production use, consider using an async implementation with tokio.\n537\tpub struct LogTailer {\n538\t    /// Configuration\n539\t    config: LogTailerConfig,\n540\t\n541\t    /// Current file position\n542\t    position: u64,\n543\t\n544\t    /// Sender for log events\n545\t    event_tx: Option<Sender<LogEvent>>,\n546\t\n547\t    /// Current inode (for rotation detection)\n548\t    #[cfg(unix)]\n549\t    current_inode: Option<u64>,\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\timpl LogTailer {\n553\t    /// Create a new log tailer with the given configuration.\n554\t    pub fn new(config: LogTailerConfig) -> Self {\n555\t        Self {\n556\t            config,\n557\t            position: 0,\n558\t            event_tx: None,\n559\t            #[cfg(unix)]\n560\t            current_inode: None,\n561\t        }\n562\t    }\n563\t\n564\t    /// Create a log tailer for a specific file path.\n565\t    pub fn for_file(path: impl Into<PathBuf>) -> Self {\n566\t        Self::new(LogTailerConfig::new(path))\n567\t    }\n568\t\n569\t    /// Set the event sender.\n570\t    pub fn with_event_sender(mut self, tx: Sender<LogEvent>) -> Self {\n571\t        self.event_tx = Some(tx);\n572\t        self\n573\t    }\n574\t\n575\t    /// Read new lines from the file and parse them.\n576\t    ///\n577\t    /// Returns parsed log entries or an empty vec if no new content.\n578\t    pub fn read_new_lines(&mut self) -> LogResult<Vec<LogEntry>> {\n579\t        use std::fs::File;\n580\t        use std::io::{BufRead, BufReader, Seek, SeekFrom};\n581\t\n582\t        let path = &self.config.path;\n583\t\n584\t        // Open file\n585\t        let file = File::open(path).map_err(|e| LogError::OpenFile {\n586\t            path: path.clone(),\n587\t            source: e,\n588\t        })?;\n589\t\n590\t        // Check for rotation (inode change)\n591\t        #[cfg(unix)]\n592\t        {\n593\t            use std::os::unix::fs::MetadataExt;\n594\t            let metadata = file.metadata().map_err(|e| LogError::ReadFile {\n595\t                path: path.clone(),\n596\t                source: e,\n597\t            })?;\n598\t            let inode = metadata.ino();\n599\t\n600\t            if let Some(prev_inode) = self.current_inode {\n601\t                if inode != prev_inode {\n602\t                    // File rotated - reset position\n603\t                    debug!(path = ?path, old_inode = prev_inode, new_inode = inode, \"Log file rotated\");\n604\t                    self.position = 0;\n605\t                    self.current_inode = Some(inode);\n606\t\n607\t                    if let Some(tx) = &self.event_tx {\n608\t                        let _ = tx.send(LogEvent::FileRotated { path: path.clone() });\n609\t                    }\n610\t                }\n611\t            } else {\n612\t                self.current_inode = Some(inode);\n613\t            }\n614\t        }\n615\t\n616\t        let mut reader = BufReader::new(file);\n617\t\n618\t        // Seek to last position (or end if starting fresh)\n619\t        if self.position == 0 && self.config.start_from_end {\n620\t            reader\n621\t                .seek(SeekFrom::End(0))\n622\t                .map_err(|e| LogError::ReadFile {\n623\t                    path: path.clone(),\n624\t                    source: e,\n625\t                })?;\n626\t            self.position = reader.stream_position().map_err(|e| LogError::ReadFile {\n627\t                path: path.clone(),\n628\t                source: e,\n629\t            })?;\n630\t            return Ok(Vec::new());\n631\t        }\n632\t\n633\t        reader\n634\t            .seek(SeekFrom::Start(self.position))\n635\t            .map_err(|e| LogError::ReadFile {\n636\t                path: path.clone(),\n637\t                source: e,\n638\t            })?;\n639\t\n640\t        // Read new lines\n641\t        let mut entries = Vec::new();\n642\t        let mut line = String::new();\n643\t\n644\t        loop {\n645\t            line.clear();\n646\t            match reader.read_line(&mut line) {\n647\t                Ok(0) => break, // EOF\n648\t                Ok(_) => {\n649\t                    let mut entry = LogEntry::parse(&line);\n650\t\n651\t                    // Add source from config if not already set\n652\t                    if entry.source.is_none() {\n653\t                        entry.source = self.config.source.clone();\n654\t                    }\n655\t\n656\t                    if !entry.message.is_empty() {\n657\t                        entries.push(entry);\n658\t                    }\n659\t                }\n660\t                Err(e) => {\n661\t                    warn!(path = ?path, error = %e, \"Error reading log line\");\n662\t                    break;\n663\t                }\n664\t            }\n665\t        }\n666\t\n667\t        // Update position\n668\t        self.position = reader.stream_position().map_err(|e| LogError::ReadFile {\n669\t            path: path.clone(),\n670\t            source: e,\n671\t        })?;\n672\t\n673\t        // Send event if we have entries\n674\t        if !entries.is_empty() {\n675\t            if let Some(tx) = &self.event_tx {\n676\t                let _ = tx.send(LogEvent::NewEntries(entries.clone()));\n677\t            }\n678\t        }\n679\t\n680\t        Ok(entries)\n681\t    }\n682\t\n683\t    /// Get the current file position.\n684\t    pub fn position(&self) -> u64 {\n685\t        self.position\n686\t    }\n687\t\n688\t    /// Reset position to start of file.\n689\t    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n690\t        self.position = 0;\n691\t        #[cfg(unix)]\n692\t        {\n693\t            self.current_inode = None;\n694\t        }\n695\t    }\n696\t}\n697\t\n698\t/// Aggregate log buffer that combines logs from multiple sources.\n699\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n700\tpub struct AggregateLogBuffer {\n701\t    /// Combined buffer of all logs\n702\t    buffer: LogBuffer,\n703\t\n704\t    /// Per-source buffers for filtering\n705\t    source_buffers: std::collections::HashMap<String, LogBuffer>,\n706\t}\n707\t\n708\timpl AggregateLogBuffer {\n709\t    /// Create a new aggregate buffer with the given capacity.\n710\t    pub fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self {\n711\t        Self {\n712\t            buffer: LogBuffer::new(capacity),\n713\t            source_buffers: std::collections::HashMap::new(),\n714\t        }\n715\t    }\n716\t\n717\t    /// Push an entry to the aggregate buffer.\n718\t    pub fn push(&mut self, entry: LogEntry) {\n719\t        // Add to combined buffer\n720\t        self.buffer.push(entry.clone());\n721\t\n722\t        // Add to source-specific buffer if source is set\n723\t        if let Some(source) = &entry.source {\n724\t            self.source_buffers\n725\t                .entry(source.clone())\n726\t                .or_insert_with(|| LogBuffer::new(100))\n727\t                .push(entry);\n728\t        }\n729\t    }\n730\t\n731\t    /// Get the combined buffer.\n732\t    pub fn all(&self) -> &LogBuffer {\n733\t        &self.buffer\n734\t    }\n735\t\n736\t    /// Get logs for a specific source.\n737\t    pub fn for_source(&self, source: &str) -> Option<&LogBuffer> {\n738\t        self.source_buffers.get(source)\n739\t    }\n740\t\n741\t    /// Get list of sources.\n742\t    pub fn sources(&self) -> Vec<&String> {\n743\t        self.source_buffers.keys().collect()\n744\t    }\n745\t\n746\t    /// Get total entry count.\n747\t    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {\n748\t        self.buffer.len()\n749\t    }\n750\t\n751\t    /// Check if empty.\n752\t    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n753\t        self.buffer.is_empty()\n754\t    }\n755\t\n756\t    /// Clear all buffers.\n757\t    pub fn clear(&mut self) {\n758\t        self.buffer.clear();\n759\t        for buffer in self.source_buffers.values_mut() {\n760\t            buffer.clear();\n761\t        }\n762\t    }\n763\t}\n764\t\n765\t#[cfg(test)]\n766\tmod tests {\n767\t    use super::*;\n768\t    use std::fs;\n769\t    use std::io::Write;\n770\t    use std::sync::mpsc;\n771\t    use tempfile::TempDir;\n772\t\n773\t    // ============================================================\n774\t    // LogLevel Tests\n775\t    // ============================================================\n776\t\n777\t    #[test]\n778\t    fn test_log_level_from_str() {\n779\t        assert_eq!(LogLevel::from_str(\"trace\"), LogLevel::Trace);\n780\t        assert_eq!(LogLevel::from_str(\"DEBUG\"), LogLevel::Debug);\n781\t        assert_eq!(LogLevel::from_str(\"Info\"), LogLevel::Info);\n782\t        assert_eq!(LogLevel::from_str(\"warn\"), LogLevel::Warn);\n783\t        assert_eq!(LogLevel::from_str(\"warning\"), LogLevel::Warn);\n784\t        assert_eq!(LogLevel::from_str(\"error\"), }\n788\t\n789\t    #[test]\n790\t    fn test_log_level_display() {\n791\t        assert_eq!(LogLevel::Info.to_string(), \"INFO\");\n792\t        assert_eq!(LogLevel::Error.to_string(), LogLevel::Debug);\n857\t\n858\t        // Text with brackets\n859\t        let entry2 = LogEntry::parse(\"[ERROR] Failed\");\n860\t        assert_eq!(entry2.level, LogLevel::Error);\n861\t\n862\t assert!(display.contains(\"[worker-1]\"));\n875\t        assert!(display.contains(\"Test message\"));\n876\t    }\n877\t\n878\t    // ============================================================\n879\t    // LogBuffer (Ring Buffer) Tests\n880\t    // ============================================================\n881\t\n882\t    #[test]\n883\t    fn test_log_buffer_new() {\n884\t        let buffer = LogBuffer::new(100);\n885\t        assert_eq!(buffer.capacity(), 100);\n886\t        assert_eq!(buffer.len(), 0);\n887\t        assert!(buffer.is_empty());\n888\t    }\n889\t\n890\t    #[test]\n891\t    fn test_log_buffer_push() {\n892\t        let mut buffer = LogBuffer::new(10);\n893\t        buffer.push(LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Info, \"Message 1\".to_string()));\n894\t        buffer.push(LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Info, \"Message 2\".to_string()));\n895\t\n896\t        assert_eq!(buffer.len(), 2);\n897\t        assert_eq!(buffer.total_added(), 2);\n898\t        assert_eq!(buffer.dropped_count(), 0);\n899\t    }\n900\t\n901\t    #[test]\n902\t    fn test_log_buffer_capacity_limit() {\n903\t        let mut buffer = LogBuffer::new(3);\n904\t\n905\t        buffer.push(LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Info, \"1\".to_string()));\n906\t        buffer.push(LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Info, \"2\".to_string()));\n907\t        buffer.push(LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Info, \"3\".to_string()));\n908\t\n909\t        assert_eq!(buffer.len(), 3);\n910\t        assert!(buffer.is_full());\n911\t        assert_eq!(buffer.dropped_count(), 0);\n912\t\n913\t        // Push one more - should drop oldest\n914\t        buffer.push(LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Info, \"4\".to_string()));\n915\t\n916\t        assert_eq!(buffer.len(), 3);\n917\t        assert_eq!(buffer.total_added(), 4);\n918\t        assert_eq!(buffer.dropped_count(), 1);\n919\t\n920\t        // Verify oldest entry is gone\n921\t        let entries: Vec<_> = buffer.iter().collect();\n922\t        assert_eq!(entries[0].message, \"2\");\n923\t        assert_eq!(entries[2].message, \"4\");\n924\t    }\n925\t\n926\t    #[test]\n927\t    fn test_log_buffer_ring_behavior() {\n928\t        let mut buffer = LogBuffer::new(5);\n929\t\n930\t        // Add 10 entries - first 5 should be dropped\n931\t        for i in 1..=10 {\n932\t            buffer.push(LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Info, format!(\"Entry {}\", i)));\n933\t        }\n934\t\n935\t        assert_eq!(buffer.len(), 5);\n936\t        assert_eq!(buffer.total_added(), 10);\n937\t        assert_eq!(buffer.dropped_count(), 5);\n938\t\n939\t        // Should have entries 6-10\n940\t        let messages: Vec<_> = buffer.iter().map(|e| e.message.as_str()).collect();\n941\t        assert_eq!(\n942\t            messages,\n943\t            vec![\"Entry 6\", \"Entry 7\", \"Entry 8\", \"Entry 9\", \"Entry 10\"]\n944\t        );\n945\t    }\n946\t\n947\t    #[test]\n948\t    fn test_log_buffer_oldest_newest() {\n949\t        let mut buffer = LogBuffer::new(10);\n950\t\n951\t        buffer.push(LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Info, \"First\".to_string()));\n952\t        buffer.push(LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Info, \"Last\".to_string()));\n953\t\n954\t        assert_eq!(buffer.oldest().unwrap().message, \"First\");\n955\t        assert_eq!(buffer.newest().unwrap().message, \"Last\");\n956\t    }\n957\t\n958\t    #[test]\n959\t    fn test_log_buffer_last_n() {\n960\t        let mut buffer = LogBuffer::new(10);\n961\t\n962\t        for i in 1..=5 {\n963\t            buffer.push(LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Info, format!(\"{}\", i)));\n964\t        }\n965\t\n966\t        let last_2: Vec<_> = buffer.last_n(2).map(|e| e.message.as_str()).collect();\n967\t        assert_eq!(last_2, vec![\"4\", \"5\"]);\n968\t\n969\t        // Request more than available\n970\t        let last_10: Vec<_> = buffer.last_n(10).map(|e| e.message.as_str()).collect();\n971\t        assert_eq!(last_10.len(), 5);\n972\t    }\n973\t\n974\t    #[test]\n975\t    fn test_log_buffer_filter_level() {\n976\t        let mut buffer = LogBuffer::new(10);\n977\t\n978\t        buffer.push(LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Debug, \"Debug\".to_string()));\n979\t        buffer.push(LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Info, \"Info\".to_string()));\n980\t        buffer.push(LogEntry::new(LogLevel::Warn, \"Warn\".to_string()));\n981\t config.model,\n73\t            config.workspace.display()\n74\t        );\n75\t\n76\t        // Check if session already exists and kill it\n77\t        let session_name = format!(\"{}{}\", self.session_prefix, config.session_name);\n78\t        if tmux::session_exists(&session_name).await? {\n79\t            warn!(\"Session {} already exists, killing it\", session_name);\n80\t `/home/coding/FORGE/crates/forge-tui/src/activity_panel.rs`\n  - **Purpose**: Real-time activity log panel with ring buffer\n  - **Key Components**:\n    - `ActivityLogData`: Ring buffer (default 100 entries) for activity events\n    - `ActivityEntry`: Individual log entries with timestamp, event type, source, message\n    - `ActivityEventType`: 14 event types (WorkerSpawn, WorkerStop, TaskPickup, ApiCall, etc.)\n    - `ActivityPanel`: TUI widget for rendering\n    - Auto-scroll with manual pause on user navigation\n    - Color-coded by event type and severity\n\n- **File**: `/home/coding/FORGE/crates/forge-tui/src/log.rs`\n  - **Purpose**: Log streaming and buffer management for worker log files\n  - **Key Components**:\n    - `LogBuffer`: Ring buffer (default 1000 entries) for parsed log entries\n    - `LogEntry`: Structured log entries with timestamp, level, message, source\n    - `LogLevel`: Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error\n    - `LogTailer`: Async file tailing with rotation detection\n    - `AggregateLogBuffer`: Multi-source log aggregation\n\n### 2. **Existing Event Types in forge-core**\n\n#### **Audit Event Types**  // API call event\n    HealthCheck,      // Health check result\n    ConfigReload,     // Config hot-reloaded\n    Info,             // General info\n    Warning,          // Warning event\n    Error,            // Error event\n}\n```\n\n### 3. **Worker Actions (Spawn/Kill/Pause/Resume) Handling**\n\n#### **Worker Launcher** (`/home/coding/FORGE/crates/forge-worker/src/launcher.rs`)\n- - `idx_audit_logs_actor`\n  - `idx_audit_logs_entity` (composite on entity_type, entity_id)\n  - `idx_audit_logs_severity`\n\n- **Key Methods**:\n  - `open()`: Open or create audit database\n  - `log()`: Insert audit event\n  - `query()`: Query with filters (AuditFilter)\n  - `count()`: Count matching events\n  - `get_entity_history()`: Get history for specific entity\n  - `apply_retention()`: Delete old logs per retention policy\n  - `export_json()`, `export_csv()`: Export capabilities\n  - `stats()`: Get audit log statistics\n  - `vacuum()`: Reclaim space\n\n### 5. pub workspaces: MultiWorkspaceConfig,\n163\t}\n164\t\n165\timpl ForgeConfig {\n166\t    /// Load configuration from the default path (~/.forge/config.yaml).\n167\t    ///\n168\t    /// Returns default fallback.\n184\t    ///\n185\t    /// This method attempts to load and parse the config file. If the file\n186\t    /// doesn't exist, is unreadable, or contains invalid YAML, it returns\n187\t    /// the default configuration rather than failing.\n188\t    ///\n189\t    /// Errors are logged but don't prevent the application from starting.\n190\t    pub fn load_from(path: &PathBuf) -> Option<Self> {\n191\t        if !path.exists() {\n192\t            tracing::debug!(\"Config file does not exist: {:?} - using defaults\", path);\n193\t            return None;\n194\t        }\n195\t\n196\t        // Try to read the file\n197\t        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n198\t            Ok(c) => c,\n199\t            Err(e) => {\n200\t                tracing::warn!(\n201\t                    path = ?path,\n202\t                    error = %e,\n203\t                    \"Failed to read config file - using defaults\"\n204\t                );\n205\t                return None;\n206\t            }\n207\t        };\n208\t\n209\t        // Try to parse with fallback to partial parsing\n210\t        Self::parse_with_fallback(&content)\n211\t    }\n212\t\n213\t    /// Load configuration from a specific path with detailed error reporting.\n214\t    ///\n215\t    /// Returns a Result with detailed error information for display to users.\n216\t    pub fn load_from_with_error(path: &PathBuf) -> Result<Self, ConfigLoadError> {\n217\t        if !path.exists() {\n218\t            return Err(ConfigLoadError::NotFound(path.clone()));\n219\t        }\n220\t\n221\t        // Try to read the file\n222\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n223\t            .map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ReadError {\n224\t                path: path.clone(),\n225\t                error: e,\n226\t            })?;\n227\t\n228\t        // Try to parse as full YAML\n229\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<ForgeConfig>(&content) {\n230\t            Ok(config) => {\n231\t                // Validate and return errors if invalid\n232\t                config.validate()?;\n233\t                Ok(config)\n234\t            }\n235\t            Err(e) => Err(ConfigLoadError::ParseError {\n236\t                path: path.clone(),\n237\t                error: e,\n238\t            }),\n239\t        }\n240\t    }\n241\t\n242\t    /// Parse configuration from YAML string.\n243\t    ///\n244\t    /// Returns None if parsing fails completely.\n245\t    pub fn parse(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n246\t        Self::parse_with_fallback(content)\n247\t    }\n248\t\n249\t    /// Parse configuration with fallback for partial/invalid configs.\n250\t    ///\n251\t    /// This method:\n252\t    /// 1. Tries to parse the full config\n253\t    /// 2. Falls back to partial parsing if sections are invalid\n254\t    /// 3. Returns default for completely invalid YAML\n255\t    fn parse_with_fallback(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n256\t        // First try to parse as full YAML\n257\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<ForgeConfig>(content) {\n258\t            Ok(config) => {\n259\t                // Validate and warn about issues, but still return the config\n260\t                if let Err(e) = config.validate() {\n261\t                    tracing::warn!(\n262\t                        error = %e,\n263\t                        \"Config validation warning - some settings may be ignored\"\n264\t                    );\n265\t                }\n266\t                tracing::debug!(\"Successfully parsed forge config\");\n267\t                Some(config)\n268\t            }\n269\t            Err(e) => {\n270\t                // Format detailed error message with line/column information\n271\t                let error_msg = format_yaml_error(&e);\n272\t                tracing::warn!(\n273\t                    error = %error_msg,\n274\t                    \"Failed to parse config YAML - attempting partial parse\"\n275\t                );\n276\t\n277\t                // Try to parse individual sections as a fallback\n278\t                Self::parse_partial(content)\n279\t            }\n280\t        }\n281\t    }\n282\t\n283\t    /// Attempt to parse individual sections of a malformed config.\n284\t    ///\n285\t    /// This allows partial configs to work even if one section has errors.\n286\t    fn parse_partial(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n287\t        // Try to parse as generic YAML first\n288\t        let yaml: serde_yaml::Value = match serde_yaml::from_str(content) {\n289\t            Ok(y) => y,\n290\t            Err(e) => {\n291\t                tracing::warn!(\n292\t                    error = %e,\n293\t                    \"Config is not valid YAML - using defaults\"\n294\t                );\n295\t                return None;\n296\t            }\n297\t        };\n298\t\n299\t        // Try to extract individual sections\n300\t        let dashboard = yaml\n301\t            .get(\"dashboard\")\n302\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n303\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n304\t\n305\t        let theme = yaml\n306\t            .get(\"theme\")\n307\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n308\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n309\t\n310\t        let cost_tracking = yaml\n311\t            .get(\"cost_tracking\")\n312\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n313\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n314\t\n315\t        let auto_recovery = yaml\n316\t            .get(\"auto_recovery\")\n317\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n318\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n319\t\n320\t        let workers = yaml\n321\t            .get(\"workers\")\n322\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n323\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n324\t\n325\t        let notifications = yaml\n326\t            .get(\"notifications\")\n327\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n328\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n329\t\n330\t        let workspaces = yaml\n331\t            .get(\"workspaces\")\n332\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n333\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n334\t\n335\t        tracing::info!(\"Loaded partial config - some sections may use defaults\");\n336\t\n337\t        Some(Self {\n338\t            dashboard,\n339\t            theme,\n340\t            cost_tracking,\n341\t            auto_recovery,\n342\t            workers,\n343\t            notifications,\n344\t            workspaces,\n345\t        })\n346\t    }\n347\t\n348\t    /// Validate the configuration.\n349\t    ///\n350\t    /// Returns Ok(()) if valid, Err with message if invalid.\n351\t    /// Warnings are logged but don't cause validation failure.\n352\t    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n353\t        let mut warnings = Vec::new();\n354\t\n355\t        // Validate refresh interval (must be >= 100ms)\n356\t        if self.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms < 100 {\n357\t            warnings.push(format!(\n358\t                \"refresh_interval_ms {} is too low, using minimum of 100ms\",\n359\t                self.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms\n360\t            ));\n361\t        }\n362\t\n363\t        // Validate max_fps (must be 1-120)\n364\t        if self.dashboard.max_fps == 0 || self.dashboard.max_fps > 120 {\n365\t            warnings.push(format!(\n366\t                \"max_fps {} is invalid, must be between 1 and 120\",\n367\t                self.dashboard.max_fps\n368\t            ));\n369\t        }\n370\t\n371\t        // Validate budget thresholds (must be 0-100)\n372\t        if self.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold > 100 {\n373\t            warnings.push(format!(\n374\t                \"budget_warning_threshold {} exceeds 100%\",\n375\t                self.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold\n376\t            ));\n377\t        }\n378\t\n379\t        if self.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold > 100 {\n380\t            warnings.push(format!(\n381\t                \"budget_critical_threshold {} exceeds 100%\",\n382\t                self.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold\n383\t            ));\n384\t        }\n385\t\n386\t        // Validate theme name if specified\n387\t        if let Some(ref theme_name) = self.theme.name {\n388\t            let valid_themes = [\"default\", \"dark\", \"light\", \"cyberpunk\"];\n389\t            if !valid_themes.contains(&theme_name.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n390\t                warnings.push(format!(\n391\t                    \"Invalid theme '{}', valid themes: {:?}\",\n392\t                    theme_name, valid_themes\n393\t                ));\n394\t            }\n395\t        }\n396\t\n397\t        // Validate max workers\n398\t        if self.workers.max_workers == 0 {\n399\t            warnings.push(\"max_workers must be at least 1\".to_string());\n400\t        }\n401\t\n402\t        // Validate default model\n403\t        let valid_models = [\"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\", \"glm\"];\n404\t        if !valid_models.contains(&self.workers.default_model.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n405\t            warnings.push(format!(\n406\t                \"Invalid default_model '{}', valid models: {:?}\",\n407\t                self.workers.default_model, valid_models\n408\t            ));\n409\t        }\n410\t\n411\t        // Validate sonnet costs (must be non-negative)\n412\t        if self.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input < 0.0 {\n413\t            warnings.push(\"sonnet_cost_per_1k_input must be non-negative\".to_string());\n414\t        }\n415\t        if self.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output < 0.0 {\n416\t            warnings.push(\"sonnet_cost_per_1k_output must be non-negative\".to_string());\n417\t        }\n418\t\n419\t        if warnings.is_empty() {\n420\t            Ok(())\n421\t        } else {\n422\t            // Return the first warning as the error message\n423\t            Err(warnings.join(\"; \"))\n424\t        }\n425\t    }\n426\t\n427\t    /// Sanitize the configuration by fixing invalid values.\n428\t    ///\n429\t    /// Returns a new config with all invalid values replaced by defaults.\n430\t    pub fn sanitized(&self) -> Self {\n431\t        let mut config = self.clone();\n432\t\n433\t        // Sanitize refresh interval\n434\t        if config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms < 100 {\n435\t            tracing::warn!(\n436\t                original = config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms,\n437\t                \"Sanitizing refresh_interval_ms to minimum 100ms\"\n438\t            );\n439\t            config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms = 100;\n440\t        }\n441\t\n442\t        // Sanitize max_fps\n443\t        if config.dashboard.max_fps == 0 || config.dashboard.max_fps > 120 {\n444\t            tracing::warn!(\n445\t                original = config.dashboard.max_fps,\n446\t                \"Sanitizing max_fps to default 60\"\n447\t            );\n448\t            config.dashboard.max_fps = 60;\n449\t        }\n450\t\n451\t        // Sanitize budget thresholds\n452\t        if config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold > 100 {\n453\t            tracing::warn!(\n454\t                original = config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold,\n455\t                \"Sanitizing budget_warning_threshold to 100\"\n456\t            );\n457\t            config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold = 100;\n458\t        }\n459\t\n460\t        if config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold > 100 {\n461\t            tracing::warn!(\n462\t                original = config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold,\n463\t                \"Sanitizing budget_critical_threshold to 100\"\n464\t            );\n465\t            config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold = 100;\n466\t        }\n467\t\n468\t        // Sanitize theme name\n469\t        if let Some(ref theme_name) = config.theme.name {\n470\t            let valid_themes = [\"default\", \"dark\", \"light\", \"cyberpunk\"];\n471\t            if !valid_themes.contains(&theme_name.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n472\t                tracing::warn!(\n473\t                    original = theme_name,\n474\t                    \"Sanitizing invalid theme name to default\"\n475\t                );\n476\t                config.theme.name = None;\n477\t            }\n478\t        }\n479\t\n480\t        // Sanitize max workers\n481\t        if config.workers.max_workers == 0 {\n482\t            tracing::warn!(\n483\t                original = config.workers.max_workers,\n484\t                \"Sanitizing max_workers to minimum 1\"\n485\t            );\n486\t            config.workers.max_workers = 1;\n487\t        }\n488\t\n489\t        // Sanitize default model\n490\t        let valid_models = [\"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\", \"glm\"];\n491\t        if !valid_models.contains(&config.workers.default_model.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n492\t            tracing::warn!(\n493\t                original = config.workers.default_model,\n494\t                \"Sanitizing invalid default_model to sonnet\"\n495\t            );\n496\t            config.workers.default_model = \"sonnet\".to_string();\n497\t        }\n498\t\n499\t        // Sanitize sonnet costs\n500\t        if config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input < 0.0 {\n501\t            config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input = 0.003;\n502\t        }\n503\t        if config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output < 0.0 {\n504\t            config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output = 0.015;\n505\t        }\n506\t\n507\t        config\n508\t    }\n509\t\n510\t    /// Save configuration to a file.\n511\t    ///\n512\t    /// Writes the configuration as YAML to the specified path.\n513\t    /// Creates parent directories if they don't exist.\n514\t    pub fn save_to(&self, path: &PathBuf) -> Result<(), ConfigLoadError> {\n515\t        // Create parent directory if needed\n516\t        if let Some(parent) = path.parent()\n517\t            && !parent.exists() {\n518\t                std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(\n519\t                    format!(\"Failed to create config directory: {}\", e)\n520\t                ))?;\n521\t        }\n522\t\n523\t        // Serialize to          ))?;\n528\t\n529\t        // Write to file\n530\t        std::fs::write(path, yaml)\n531\t            .map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(\n532\t                format!(\"Failed to write config file: {}\", e)\n533\t            ))?;\n534\t\n535\t        tracing::info!(\"Saved configuration to {:?}\", path);\n536\t        Ok(())\n537\t    }\n538\t\n539\t    /// Save configuration to the default path (~/.forge/config.yaml).\n540\t    pub fn save(&self) -> Result<(), ConfigLoadError> {\n541\t        let path = config_path().ok_or(ConfigLoadError::NoHomePath)?;\n542\t        self.save_to(&path)\n543\t    }\n544\t}\n545\t\n546\t/// Dashboard configuration.\n547\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\n548\tpub struct DashboardConfig {\n549\t    /// Configuration management for FORGE.\n//!\n//! This crate handles loading, validating, and managing FORGE configuration\n//! from `~/.forge/config.yaml`.\n\nuse std::io;\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse thiserror::Error;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Default config file path (~/.forge/config.yaml).\npub fn config_path() -> Option<PathBuf> {\n    dirs::home_dir().map(|h| h.join(\".forge/config.yaml\"))\n}\n\n/// Format a YAML parse error with line/column information if available.\nfn format_yaml_error(error: &serde_yaml::Error) -> String {\n    if let Some(location) = error.location() {\n        format!(\n            \"YAML parse error at line {}, column {}: {}\",\n            location.line(),\n            location.column(),\n            error\n        )\n    } else {\n        format!(\"YAML parse error: {}\", error)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Errors that can occur when loading configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Error)]\npub enum ConfigLoadError {\n    /// Could not determine home directory\n    #[error(\"Could not determine home directory\")]\n    NoHomePath,\n\n    /// Config file not found\n    #[error(\"Config file not found: {0}\")]\n    NotFound(PathBuf),\n\n    /// Error reading config file\n    #[error(\"Failed to read config file {path}: {error}\")]\n    ReadError {\n        path: PathBuf,\n        #[source]\n        error: io::Error,\n    },\n\n    /// Error parsing YAML\n    #[error(\"Failed to parse config YAML in {path}: {}\", format_yaml_error(error))]\n    ParseError {\n        path: PathBuf,\n        error: serde_yaml::Error,\n    },\n\n    /// Config validation failed\n    #[error(\"Config validation failed: {0}\")]\n    ValidationError(String),\n}\n\nimpl From<String> for ConfigLoadError {\n    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n        ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(s)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ConfigLoadError {\n    /// Get the line number where the error occurred (if available).\n    pub fn line_number(&self) -> Option<usize> {\n        match self {\n            ConfigLoadError::ParseError { error, .. } => {\n                error.location().map(|loc| loc.line())\n            }\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the column number where the error occurred (if available).\n    pub fn column_number(&self) -> Option<usize> {\n        match self {\n            ConfigLoadError::ParseError { error, .. } => {\n                error.location().map(|loc| loc.column())\n            }\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the config file path (if applicable).\n    pub fn path(&self) -> Option<&PathBuf> {\n        match self {\n            ConfigLoadError::NotFound(path)\n            | ConfigLoadError::ReadError { path, .. }\n            | ConfigLoadError::ParseError { path, .. } => Some(path),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Multi-workspace configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct MultiWorkspaceConfig {\n    /// Enable multi-workspace mode\n    #[serde(default = \"default_multi_workspace_enabled\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n\n    /// List of workspace paths to monitor\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub workspace_paths: HashMap<String, String>,\n\n    /// Path to workspace registry file\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub registry_path: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for MultiWorkspaceConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: default_multi_workspace_enabled(),\n            workspace_paths: HashMap::new(),\n            registry_path: None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nfn default_multi_workspace_enabled() -> bool {\n    false\n}\n\n/// Forge configuration structure.\n///\n/// This represents the subset of config.yaml that can be hot-reloaded.\n/// Changes to these fields will take effect immediately.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct ForgeConfig {\n    /// Dashboard configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub dashboard: DashboardConfig,\n\n    /// Theme configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub theme: ThemeConfig,\n\n    /// Cost tracking configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub cost_tracking: CostTrackingConfig,\n\n    /// Auto-recovery configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub auto_recovery: AutoRecoveryConfig,\n\n    /// Worker defaults configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub workers: WorkerConfig,\n\n    /// Notification configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub notifications: NotificationsConfig,\n\n    /// Multi-workspace configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub workspaces: MultiWorkspaceConfig,\n}\n\nimpl ForgeConfig {\n    /// Load configuration from the default path (~/.forge/config.yaml).\n    ///\n    /// Returns default configuration if the file doesn't exist or is invalid.\n    /// Invalid configs are logged as warnings but don't prevent startup.\n    pub fn load() -> Option<Self> {\n        let path = config_path()?;\n        Self::load_from(&path)\n    }\n\n    /// Load configuration from the default path with detailed error reporting.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a Result with detailed error information for display to users.\n    pub fn load_with_error() -> Result<Self, ConfigLoadError> {\n        let path = config_path().ok_or(ConfigLoadError::NoHomePath)?;\n        Self::load_from_with_error(&path)\n    }\n\n    /// Load configuration from a specific path with graceful fallback.\n    ///\n    /// This method attempts to load and parse the config file. If the file\n    /// doesn't exist, is unreadable, or contains invalid YAML, it returns\n    /// the default configuration rather than failing.\n    ///\n    /// Errors are logged but don't prevent the application from starting.\n    pub fn load_from(path: &PathBuf) -> Option<Self> {\n        if !path.exists() {\n            tracing::debug!(\"Config file does not exist: {:?} - using defaults\", path);\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        // Try to read the file\n        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(c) => c,\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    path = ?path,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"Failed to read config file - using defaults\"\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Try to parse with fallback to partial parsing\n        Self::parse_with_fallback(&content)\n    }\n\n    /// Load configuration from a specific path with detailed error reporting.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a Result with detailed error information for display to users.\n    pub fn load_from_with_error(path: &PathBuf) -> Result<Self, ConfigLoadError> {\n        if !path.exists() {\n            return Err(ConfigLoadError::NotFound(path.clone()));\n        }\n\n        // Try to read the file\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n            .map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ReadError {\n                path: path.clone(),\n                error: e,\n            })?;\n\n        // Try to parse as full YAML\n        match serde_yaml::from_str::<ForgeConfig>(&content) {\n            Ok(config) => {\n                // Validate and return errors if invalid\n                config.validate()?;\n                Ok(config)\n            }\n            Err(e) => Err(ConfigLoadError::ParseError {\n                path: path.clone(),\n                error: e,\n            }),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Parse configuration from YAML string.\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if parsing fails completely.\n    pub fn parse(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        Self::parse_with_fallback(content)\n    }\n\n    /// Parse configuration with fallback for partial/invalid configs.\n    ///\n    /// This method:\n    /// 1. Tries to parse the full config\n    /// 2. Falls back to partial parsing if sections are invalid\n    /// 3. Returns default for completely invalid YAML\n    fn parse_with_fallback(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        // First try to parse as full YAML\n        match serde_yaml::from_str::<ForgeConfig>(content) {\n            Ok(config) => {\n                // Validate and warn about issues, but still return the config\n                if let Err(e) = config.validate() {\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"Config validation warning - some settings may be ignored\"\n                    );\n                }\n                tracing::debug!(\"Successfully parsed forge config\");\n                Some(config)\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                // Format detailed error message with line/column information\n                let error_msg = format_yaml_error(&e);\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %error_msg,\n                    \"Failed to parse config YAML - attempting partial parse\"\n                );\n\n                // Try to parse individual sections as a fallback\n                Self::parse_partial(content)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Attempt to parse individual sections of a malformed config.\n    ///\n    /// This allows partial configs to work even if one section has errors.\n    fn parse_partial(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        // Try to parse as generic YAML first\n        let yaml: serde_yaml::Value = match serde_yaml::from_str(content) {\n            Ok(y) => y,\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"Config is not valid YAML - using defaults\"\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Try to extract individual sections\n        let dashboard = yaml\n            .get(\"dashboard\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let theme = yaml\n            .get(\"theme\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let cost_tracking = yaml\n            .get(\"cost_tracking\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let auto_recovery = yaml\n            .get(\"auto_recovery\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let workers = yaml\n            .get(\"workers\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let notifications = yaml\n            .get(\"notifications\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let workspaces = yaml\n            .get(\"workspaces\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        tracing::info!(\"Loaded partial config - some sections may use defaults\");\n\n        Some(Self {\n            dashboard,\n            theme,\n            cost_tracking,\n            auto_recovery,\n            workers,\n            notifications,\n            workspaces,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Validate the configuration.\n    ///\n    /// Returns Ok(()) if valid, Err with message if invalid.\n    /// Warnings are logged but don't cause validation failure.\n    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n        let mut warnings = Vec::new();\n\n        // Validate refresh interval (must be >= 100ms)\n        if self.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms < 100 {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"refresh_interval_ms {} is too low, using minimum of 100ms\",\n                self.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Validate max_fps (must be 1-120)\n        if self.dashboard.max_fps == 0 || self.dashboard.max_fps > 120 {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"max_fps {} is invalid, must be between 1 and 120\",\n                self.dashboard.max_fps\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Validate budget thresholds (must be 0-100)\n        if self.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold > 100 {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"budget_warning_threshold {} exceeds 100%\",\n                self.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if self.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold > 100 {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"budget_critical_threshold {} exceeds 100%\",\n                self.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Validate theme name if specified\n        if let Some(ref theme_name) = self.theme.name {\n            let valid_themes = [\"default\", \"dark\", \"light\", \"cyberpunk\"];\n            if !valid_themes.contains(&theme_name.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n                warnings.push(format!(\n                    \"Invalid theme '{}', valid themes: {:?}\",\n                    theme_name, valid_themes\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Validate max workers\n        if self.workers.max_workers == 0 {\n            warnings.push(\"max_workers must be at least 1\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        // Validate default model\n        let valid_models = [\"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\", \"glm\"];\n        if !valid_models.contains(&self.workers.default_model.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"Invalid default_model '{}', valid models: {:?}\",\n                self.workers.default_model, valid_models\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Validate sonnet costs (must be non-negative)\n        if self.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input < 0.0 {\n            warnings.push(\"sonnet_cost_per_1k_input must be non-negative\".to_string());\n        }\n        if self.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output < 0.0 {\n            warnings.push(\"sonnet_cost_per_1k_output must be non-negative\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        if warnings.is_empty() {\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            // Return the first warning as the error message\n            Err(warnings.join(\"; \"))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Sanitize the configuration by fixing invalid values.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a new config with all invalid values replaced by defaults.\n    pub fn sanitized(&self) -> Self {\n        let mut config = self.clone();\n\n        // Sanitize refresh interval\n        if config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms < 100 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms,\n                \"Sanitizing refresh_interval_ms to minimum 100ms\"\n            );\n            config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms = 100;\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize max_fps\n        if config.dashboard.max_fps == 0 || config.dashboard.max_fps > 120 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.dashboard.max_fps,\n                \"Sanitizing max_fps to default 60\"\n            );\n            config.dashboard.max_fps = 60;\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize budget thresholds\n        if config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold > 100 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold,\n                \"Sanitizing budget_warning_threshold to 100\"\n            );\n            config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold = 100;\n        }\n\n        if config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold > 100 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold,\n                \"Sanitizing budget_critical_threshold to 100\"\n            );\n            config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold = 100;\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize theme name\n        if let Some(ref theme_name) = config.theme.name {\n            let valid_themes = [\"default\", \"dark\", \"light\", \"cyberpunk\"];\n            if !valid_themes.contains(&theme_name.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    original = theme_name,\n                    \"Sanitizing invalid theme name to default\"\n                );\n                config.theme.name = None;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize max workers\n        if config.workers.max_workers == 0 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.workers.max_workers,\n                \"Sanitizing max_workers to minimum 1\"\n            );\n            config.workers.max_workers = 1;\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize default model\n        let valid_models = [\"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\", \"glm\"];\n        if !valid_models.contains(&config.workers.default_model.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.workers.default_model,\n                \"Sanitizing invalid default_model to sonnet\"\n            );\n            config.workers.default_model = \"sonnet\".to_string();\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize sonnet costs\n        if config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input < 0.0 {\n            config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input = 0.003;\n        }\n        if config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output < 0.0 {\n            config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output = 0.015;\n        }\n\n        config\n    }\n\n    /// Save configuration to a file.\n    ///\n    /// Writes the configuration as YAML to the specified path.\n    /// Creates parent directories if they don't exist.\n    pub fn save_to(&self, path: &PathBuf) -> Result<(), ConfigLoadError> {\n        // Create parent directory if needed\n        if let Some(parent) = path.parent()\n            && !parent.exists() {\n                std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(\n                    format!(\"Failed to create config directory: {}\", e)\n                ))?;\n        }\n\n        // Serialize to YAML\n        let yaml = serde_yaml::to_string(self)\n            .map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(\n                format!(\"Failed to serialize config: {}\", e)\n            ))?;\n\n        // Write to file\n        std::fs::write(path, yaml)\n            .map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(\n                format!(\"Failed to write config file: {}\", e)\n            ))?;\n\n        tracing::info!(\"Saved configuration to {:?}\", path);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Save configuration to the default path (~/.forge/config.yaml).\n    pub fn save(&self) -> Result<(), ConfigLoadError> {\n        let path = config_path().ok_or(ConfigLoadError::NoHomePath)?;\n        self.save_to(&path)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Dashboard configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct DashboardConfig {\n    /// Refresh interval in milliseconds.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_refresh_interval\")]\n    pub refresh_interval_ms: u64,\n\n    /// Maximum frames per second.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_fps\")]\n    pub max_fps: u64,\n\n    /// Default layout mode.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_layout\")]\n    pub default_layout: String,\n\n    /// Log retention period in days (0 = forever).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_log_retention_days\")]\n    pub log_retention_days: u64,\n}\n\nimpl Default for DashboardConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            refresh_interval_ms: default_refresh_interval(),\n            max_fps: default_max_fps(),\n            default_layout: default_layout(),\n            log_retention_days: default_log_retention_days(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nfn default_refresh_interval() -> u64 {\n    1000\n}\n\nfn default_max_fps() -> u64 {\n    60\n}\n\nfn default_layout() -> String {\n    \"overview\".to_string()\n}\n\nfn default_log_retention_days() -> u64 {\n    7\n}\n\n/// Theme configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct ThemeConfig {\n    /// Theme name (default, dark, light, cyberpunk).\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub name: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Worker configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct WorkerConfig {\n    /// Maximum concurrent workers.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_workers\")]\n    pub max_workers: u64,\n\n    /// Default model for new workers.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_model\")]\n    pub default_model: String,\n}\n\nimpl Default for WorkerConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            max_workers:
