Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n359\t        let mut params: Vec<Box<dyn rusqlite::ToSql>> = Vec::new();\n360\t\n361\t        if let Some(start) = &self.start_time {\n362\t            conditions.push(\"timestamp >= ?\".to_string());\n363\t            params.push(Box::new(start.to_rfc3339()));\n364\t        }\n365\t        if let Some(end) = &self.end_time {\n366\t            conditions.push(\"timestamp <= ?\".to_string());\n367\t            params.push(Box::new(end.to_rfc3339()));\n368\t        }\n369\t        if let Some(types) = &self.event_types {\n370\t            let placeholders: Vec<String> = (0..types.len()).map(|_| \"?\".to_string()).collect();\n371\t            conditions.push(format!(\"event_type IN ({})\", placeholders.join(\", \")));\n372\t            for t in types {\n373\t                params.push(Box::new(t.to_string()));\n374\t            }\n375\t        }\n376\t        if let Some(actor) = &self.actor {\n377\t            conditions.push(\"actor = ?\".to_string());\n378\t            params.push(Box::new(actor.clone()));\n379\t        }\n380\t        if let Some(entity_type) = &self.entity_type {\n381\t            conditions.push(\"entity_type = ?\".to_string());\n382\t            params.push(Box::new(entity_type.clone()));\n383\t        }\n384\t        if let Some(entity_id) = &self.entity_id {\n385\t            conditions.push(\"entity_id = ?\".to_string());\n386\t            params.push(Box::new(entity_id.clone()));\n387\t        }\n388\t        if let Some(severity) = &self.severity {\n389\t            conditions.push(\"severity = ?\".to_string());\n390\t            params.push(Box::new(severity.to_string()));\n391\t        }\n392\t\n393\t        let where_clause = if conditions.is_empty() {\n394\t            String::new()\n395\t        } else {\n396\t            format!(\"WHERE {}\", conditions.join(\" AND \"))\n397\t        };\n398\t\n399\t        (where_clause, params)\n400\t    }\n401\t}\n402\t\n403\t/// Retention policy for audit logs.\n404\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n405\tpub struct RetentionPolicy {\n406\t    /// Number of days to retain logs\n407\t    pub days: i64,\n408\t    /// Whether to apply retention on startup\n409\t    pub apply_on_startup: bool,\n410\t}\n411\t\n412\timpl Default for RetentionPolicy {\n413\t    fn default() -> Self {\n414\t        Self {\n415\t            days: DEFAULT_RETENTION_DAYS,\n416\t            apply_on_startup: true,\n417\t        }\n418\t    }\n419\t}\n420\t\n421\timpl RetentionPolicy {\n422\t    /// Create a new retention policy.\n423\t    pub fn new(days: i64, apply_on_startup: bool) -> Self {\n424\t        Self {\n425\t            days: days.max(1), // At least 1 day\n426\t            apply_on_startup,\n427\t        }\n428\t    }\n429\t\n430\t    /// Get the cutoff timestamp for this policy.\n431\t    pub fn cutoff(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {\n432\t        Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::days(self.days)\n433\t    }\n434\t}\n435\t\n436\t/// SQLite-backed audit logger.\n437\t#[derive(Clone)]\n438\tpub struct AuditLogger {\n439\t    conn: Arc<Mutex<Connection>>,\n440\t    db_path: PathBuf,\n441\t    retention: RetentionPolicy,\n442\t}\n443\t\n444\timpl AuditLogger {\n445\t    /// Open or create an audit database at the given path.\n446\t    pub fn open<P: AsRef<Path>>(path: P) -> Result<Self> {\n447\t        let db_path = path.as_ref().to_path_buf();\n448\t\n449\t        // Ensure parent directory exists\n450\t        if let Some(parent) = db_path.parent() {\n451\t            fs::create_dir_all(parent)\n452\t                .map_err(|e| ForgeError::io(\"create_dir_all\", parent, e))?;\n453\t        }\n454\t\n455\t        let conn = Connection::open(&db_path)\n456\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::Audit { message: format!(\"failed to open database: {}\", e) })?;\n457\t\n458\t        // Enable WAL mode for better concurrent access\n459\t        // Returns the new mode, so we need to use query_row\n460\t        conn.query_row(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\", [], |row| {\n461\t            row.get::<_, String>(0)\n462\t        })\n463\t        .map_err(|e| ForgeError::Audit { message: format!(\"failed to enable WAL: {}\", e) })?;\n464\t\n465\t        // Set a busy timeout for concurrent access\n466\t        conn.query_row(\"PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000\", [], |row| {\n467\t            row.get::<_, i64>(0)\n468\t        })\n469\t        .map_err(|e| ForgeError::Audit { message: format!(\"failed to set busy timeout: {}\", e) })?;\n470\t\n471\t        let mut logger = Self {\n472\t            conn: Arc::new(Mutex::new(conn)),\n473\t            db_path,\n474\t            retention: RetentionPolicy::default(),\n475\t        };\n476\t\n477\t        logger.migrate()?;\n478\t\n479\t        // Apply retention if configured\n480\t        if logger.retention.apply_on_startup {\n481\t            logger.apply_retention()?;\n482\t        }\n483\t\n484\t        Ok(logger)\n485\t    }\n486\t\n487\t    /// Open with a custom retention policy.\n488\t    pub fn open_with_retention<P: AsRef<Path>>(\n489\t        path: P,\n490\t        retention: RetentionPolicy,\n491\t    ) -> Result<Self> {\n492\t        let mut logger = Self::open(path)?;\n493\t        logger.retention = retention.clone();\n494\t        if retention.apply_on_startup {\n495\t            logger.apply_retention()?;\n496\t        }\n497\t        Ok(logger)\n498\t    }\n499\t\n500\t    /// Get the database path.\n501\t    pub fn path(&self) -> &Path {\n502\t        &self.db_path\n503\t    }\n504\t\n505\t    /// Get the current retention policy.\n506\t    pub fn retention(&self) -> &RetentionPolicy {\n507\t        &self.retention\n508\t    }\n509\t\n510\t    /// Update the retention policy.\n511\t    pub fn set_retention(&mut self, retention: RetentionPolicy) -> Result<()> {\n512\t        self.retention = retention.clone();\n513\t        self.apply_retention().map(|_| ())\n514\t    }\n515\t\n516\t    /// Run database migrations.\n517\t    fn migrate(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n518\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n519\t            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n520\t        })?;\n521\t\n522\t        // Create schema version table\n523\t        conn.execute(\n524\t            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_schema_version (\n525\t                version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY\n526\t            )\",\n527\t            [],\n528\t        ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create schema table: {}\", e)))?;\n529\t\n530\t        // Get current version\n531\t        let current_version: i32 = conn\n532\t            .query_row(\n533\t                \"SELECT COALESCE(MAX(version), 0) FROM audit_schema_version\",\n534\t                [],\n535\t                |row| row.get(0),\n536\t            )\n537\t            .unwrap_or(0);\n538\t\n539\t        if current_version < AUDIT_SCHEMA_VERSION {\n540\t            info!(\n541\t                current = current_version,\n542\t                target = AUDIT_SCHEMA_VERSION,\n543\t                \"Running audit database migrations\"\n544\t            );\n545\t\n546\t            // Create audit logs table\n547\t            conn.execute(\n548\t                \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (\n549\t                    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n550\t                    timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,\n551\t                    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n552\t                    actor TEXT NOT NULL,\n553\t                    entity_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n554\t                    entity_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n555\t                    old_value TEXT,\n556\t                    new_value TEXT,\n557\t                    metadata TEXT,\n558\t                    severity TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'info'\n559\t                )\",\n560\t                [],\n561\t            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create audit_logs table: {}\", e)))?;\n562\t\n563\t            // Create indexes for efficient queries\n564\t            conn.execute(\n565\t                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_timestamp\n566\t                 ON audit_logs(timestamp DESC)\",\n567\t                [],\n568\t            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create timestamp index: {}\", e)))?;\n569\t\n570\t            conn.execute(\n571\t                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_event_type\n572\t                 ON audit_logs(event_type)\",\n573\t                [],\n574\t            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create event_type index: {}\", e)))?;\n575\t\n576\t            conn.execute(\n577\t                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_actor\n578\t                 ON audit_logs(actor)\",\n579\t                [],\n580\t            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create actor index: {}\", e)))?;\n581\t\n582\t            conn.execute(\n583\t                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_entity\n584\t                 ON audit_logs(entity_type, entity_id)\",\n585\t                [],\n586\t            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create entity index: {}\", e)))?;\n587\t\n588\t            conn.execute(\n589\t                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_severity\n590\t                 ON audit_logs(severity)\",\n591\t                [],\n592\t            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create severity index: {}\", e)))?;\n593\t\n594\t            // Record schema version\n595\t            conn.execute(\n596\t                \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO audit_schema_version (version) VALUES (?)\",\n597\t                params![AUDIT_SCHEMA_VERSION],\n598\t            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to record schema version: {}\", e)))?;\n599\t\n600\t            info!(\"Audit database initialized at schema version {}\", AUDIT_SCHEMA_VERSION);\n601\t        }\n602\t\n603\t        Ok(())\n604\t    }\n605\t\n606\t    /// Log an audit event.\n607\t    pub fn log(&self, event: AuditEvent) -> Result<i64> {\n608\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n609\t            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n610\t        })?;\n611\t\n612\t        debug!(\n613\t            event_type = %event.event_type,\n614\t            actor = %event.actor,\n615\t            entity = %format!(\"{}:{}\", event.entity_type, event.entity_id),\n616\t            \"Logging audit event\"\n617\t        );\n618\t\n619\t        conn.execute(\n620\t            \"INSERT INTO audit_logs (\n621\t                timestamp, event_type, actor, entity_type, entity_id,\n622\t                old_value, new_value, metadata, severity\n623\t            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\",\n624\t            params![\n625\t                event.timestamp.to_rfc3339(),\n626\t                event.event_type.to_string(),\n627\t                event.actor,\n628\t                event.entity_type,\n629\t                event.entity_id,\n630\t                event.old_value,\n631\t                event.new_value,\n632\t                event.metadata,\n633\t                event.severity.to_string(),\n634\t            ],\n635\t        )\n636\t        .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to insert audit log: {}\", e)))?;\n637\t\n638\t        Ok(conn.last_insert_rowid())\n639\t    }\n640\t\n641\t    /// Query audit logs with optional filters.\n642\t    pub fn query(&self, filter: &AuditFilter) -> Result<Vec<AuditEvent>> {\n643\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n644\t            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n645\t        })?;\n646\t\n647\t        let (where_clause, params) = filter.build_where_clause();\n648\t        let limit = filter.limit.unwrap_or(MAX_QUERY_RESULTS);\n649\t        let offset = filter.offset.unwrap_or(0);\n650\t\n651\t        let sql = format!(\n652\t            \"SELECT timestamp, event_type, actor, entity_type, entity_id,\n653\t                    old_value, new_value, metadata, severity\n654\t             FROM audit_logs\n655\t             {}\n656\t             ORDER BY timestamp DESC\n657\t             LIMIT {} OFFSET {}\",\n658\t            where_clause, limit, offset\n659\t        );\n660\t\n661\t        let mut stmt = conn\n662\t            .prepare(&sql)\n663\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to prepare query: {}\", e)))?;\n664\t\n665\t        let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = params.iter().map(|p| p.as_ref()).collect();\n666\t\n667\t        // Helper to parse timestamp from string\n668\t        let parse_timestamp = |s: String| -> std::result::Result<DateTime<Utc>, String> {\n669\t            DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&s)\n670\t                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc))\n671\t                .map_err(|e| format!(\"invalid timestamp: {}\", e))\n672\t        };\n673\t\n674\t        // Helper to parse event type from string\n675\t        let parse_event_type = |s: String| -> std::result::Result<EventType, String> {\n676\t            s.parse().map_err(|e| format!(\"invalid event type: {}\", e))\n677\t        };\n678\t\n679\t        // Helper to parse severity from string\n680\t        let parse_severity = |s: String| -> std::result::Result<Severity, String> {\n681\t            s.parse().map_err(|e| format!(\"invalid severity: {}\", e))\n682\t        };\n683\t\n684\t        let events = stmt\n685\t            .query_map(param_refs.as_slice(), |row| {\n686\t                let ts_str: String = row.get(0)?;\n687\t                let event_type_str: String = row.get(1)?;\n688\t                let severity_str: String = row.get(8)?;\n689\t\n690\t                Ok(AuditEvent {\n691\t                    timestamp: parse_timestamp(ts_str).map_err(|e| {\n692\t                        rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(\n693\t                            Box::new(ParseError(e))\n694\t                        )\n695\t                    })?,\n696\t                    event_type: parse_event_type(event_type_str).map_err(|e| {\n697\t                        rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(\n698\t                            Box::new(ParseError(e))\n699\t                        )\n700\t                    })?,\n701\t                    actor: row.get(2)?,\n702\t                    entity_type: row.get(3)?,\n703\t                    entity_id: row.get(4)?,\n704\t                    old_value: row.get(5)?,\n705\t                    new_value: row.get(6)?,\n706\t                    metadata: row.get(7)?,\n707\t                    severity: parse_severity(severity_str).map_err(|e| {\n708\t                        rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(\n709\t                            Box::new(ParseError(e))\n710\t                        )\n711\t                    })?,\n712\t                })\n713\t            })\n714\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to execute query: {}\", e)))?\n715\t            .collect::<std::result::Result<Vec<_>, _>>()\n716\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to parse results: {}\", e)))?;\n717\t\n718\t        Ok(events)\n719\t    }\n720\t\n721\t    /// Get a count of audit logs matching the filter.\n722\t    pub fn count(&self, filter: &AuditFilter) -> Result<i64> {\n723\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n724\t            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n725\t        })?;\n726\t\n727\t        let (where_clause, params) = filter.build_where_clause();\n728\t\n729\t        let sql = format!(\n730\t            \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_logs {}\",\n731\t            where_clause\n732\t        );\n733\t\n734\t        let mut stmt = conn\n735\t            .prepare(&sql)\n736\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to prepare count query: {}\", e)))?;\n737\t\n738\t        let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = params.iter().map(|p| p.as_ref()).collect();\n739\t\n740\t        let count: i64 = stmt\n741\t            .query_row(param_refs.as_slice(), |row| row.get(0))\n742\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to execute count query: {}\", e)))?;\n743\t\n744\t        Ok(count)\n745\t    }\n746\t\n747\t    /// Get audit logs for a specific entity.\n748\t    pub fn get_entity_history(\n749\t        &self,\n750\t        entity_type: &str,\n751\t        entity_id: &str,\n752\t        limit: Option<i64>,\n753\t    ) -> Result<Vec<AuditEvent>> {\n754\t        let }\n760\t\n761\t    /// Apply retention policy - delete old logs.\n762\t    pub fn apply_retention(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n763\t        let cutoff = self.retention.cutoff();\n764\t        let cutoff_str = cutoff.to_rfc3339();\n765\t\n766\t        info!(\n767\t            cutoff = %cutoff_str,\n768\t            days = self.retention.days,\n769\t            \"Applying audit log retention\"\n770\t        );\n771\t\n772\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n773\t            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n774\t        })?;\n775\t\n776\t        let affected = conn\n777\t            .execute(\n778\t                \"DELETE FROM audit_logs WHERE timestamp < ?\",\n779\t                params![cutoff_str],\n780\t            )\n781\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to apply retention: {}\", e)))?;\n782\t\n783\t        if affected > 0 {\n784\t            info!(\n785\t                deleted = affected,\n786\t                \"Deleted audit logs past retention period\"\n787\t            );\n788\t        }\n789\t\n790\t        Ok(affected)\n791\t    }\n792\t\n793\t    /// Export audit logs to JSON.\n794\t    pub fn export_json(&self, filter: &AuditFilter, output: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n795\t        let events = self.query(filter)?;\n796\t\n797\t        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&events)\n798\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to serialize JSON: {}\", e)))?;\n799\t\n800\t        fs::write(output, json)\n801\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to write export: {}\", e)))?;\n802\t\n803\t        info!(\n804\t            count = events.len(),\n805\t            path = %output.display(),\n806\t            \"Exported audit logs to JSON\"\n807\t        );\n808\t\n809\t        Ok(())\n810\t    }\n811\t\n812\t    /// Export audit logs to CSV.\n813\t    pub fn export_csv(&self, filter: &AuditFilter, output: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n814\t        let events = self.query(filter)?;\n815\t\n816\t        let mut wtr = csv::Writer::from_path(output)\n817\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create CSV writer: {}\", e)))?;\n818\t\n819\t        // Write header\n820\t        wtr.write_record(&[\n821\t            \"timestamp\", \"event_type\", \"actor\", \"entity_type\", \"entity_id\",\n822\t            \"old_value\", \"new_value\", \"metadata\", \"severity\",\n823\t        ])\n824\t        .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to write CSV header: {}\", e)))?;\n825\t\n826\t        // Write records\n827\t        for event in &events {\n828\t            wtr.write_record(&[\n829\t                event.timestamp.to_rfc3339().as_str(),\n830\t                &event.event_type.to_string(),\n831\t                &event.actor,\n832\t                &event.entity_type,\n833\t                &event.entity_id,\n834\t                event.old_value.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n835\t                event.new_value.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n836\t                event.metadata.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n837\t                &event.severity.to_string(),\n838\t            ])\n839\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to write CSV record: {}\", e)))?;\n840\t        }\n841\t\n842\t        wtr.flush()\n843\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to flush CSV: {}\", e)))?;\n844\t\n845\t        info!(\n846\t            count = events.len(),\n847\t            path = %output.display(),\n848\t            \"Exported audit logs to CSV\"\n849\t        );\n850\t\n851\t        Ok(())\n852\t    }\n853\t\n854\t    /// Get statistics about the audit log.\n855\t    pub fn stats(&self) -> Result<AuditStats> {\n856\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n857\t            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n858\t        })?;\n859\t\n860\t        let total_events: i64 = conn\n861\t            .query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_logs\", [], |row| row.get(0))\n862\t            .unwrap_or(0);\n863\t\n864\t        let oldest_event: Option<String> = conn\n865\t            .query_row(\n866\t                \"SELECT MIN(timestamp) FROM audit_logs\",\n867\t                [],\n868\t                |row| row.get(0),\n869\t            )\n870\t            .unwrap_or(None);\n871\t\n872\t        let newest_event: Option<String> = conn\n873\t            .query_row(\n874\t                \"SELECT MAX(timestamp) FROM audit_logs\",\n875\t                [],\n876\t                |row| row.get(0),\n877\t            )\n878\t            .unwrap_or(None);\n879\t\n880\t        // Count by event type\n881\t        let mut type_counts = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n882\t        let mut stmt = conn\n883\t            .prepare(\n884\t                \"SELECT event_type, COUNT(*) as count FROM audit_logs GROUP BY event_type\"\n885\t            )\n886\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to prepare stats query: {}\", e)))?;\n887\t\n888\t        let rows = stmt\n889\t            .query_map([], |row| {\n890\t                Ok((\n891\t                    row.get::<_, String>(0)?,\n892\t                    row.get::<_, i64>(1)?\n893\t                ))\n894\t            })\n895\t            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to execute stats query: {}\", e)))?;\n896\t\n897\t        for row in rows {\n898\t            if let Ok((event_type, count)) = row {\n899\t                type_counts.insert(event_type, count);\n900\t            }\n901\t        }\n902\t\n903\t        Ok(AuditStats {\n904\t            total_events,\n905\t            oldest_timestamp: oldest_event.and_then(|s| DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&s).ok())\n906\t                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc)),\n907\t            newest_timestamp: newest_event.and_then(|s| DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&s).ok())\n908\t                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc)),\n909\t            event_type_counts: type_counts,\n910\t        })\n911\t    }\n912\t\n913\t    /// Vacuum the database to reclaim space.\n914\t    pub fn vacuum(&self) -> Result<()> {\n915\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n916\t            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n917\t        })?;\n918\t\n919\t        info!(\"Vacuuming audit database\");\n920\t\n921\t        conn.execute(\"VACUUM\", [])\n922\t            .map_err(|e| Audit logging for compliance and security tracking.\n//!\n//! This module provides structured, append-only audit trails for all mutating\n//! operations in FORGE, including worker lifecycle, bead status changes, and\n//! configuration modifications.\n//!\n//! ## Features\n//!\n//! - Immutable append-only log stored in SQLite\n//! - Comprehensive event tracking for compliance\n//! - Queryable by time range, entity, actor, and event type\n//! - Export to JSON/CSV formats\n//! - Configurable retention policy\n//!\n//! ## Example\n//!\n//! ```no_run\n//! use forge_core::audit::{AuditLogger, AuditEvent, EventType};\n//!\n//! # fn main() -> forge_core::Result<()> {\n//! let logger = AuditLogger::open(\"~/.forge/audit.db\")?;\n//!\n//! logger.log(AuditEvent {\n//!     event_type: EventType::WorkerSpawn,\n//!     actor: \"user\".to_string(),\n//!     entity_type: \"worker\".to_string(),\n//!     entity_id: \"worker-1\".to_string(),\n//!     old_value: None,\n//!     new_value: Some(r#\"{\"status\":\"active\"}\"#.to_string()),\n//!     metadata: None,\n//!     severity: Severity::Info,\n//! })?;\n//! # Ok(())\n//! # }\n//! ```\n\nuse crate::{ForgeError, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse rusqlite::{Connection, params};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::fmt;\nuse std::fs;\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse tracing::{debug, info};\n\n/// Error type for parse failures that implements std::error::Error.\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct ParseError(String);\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ParseError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"{}\", self.0)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for ParseError {}\n\n/// Current audit schema version.\nconst AUDIT_SCHEMA_VERSION: i32 = 1;\n\n/// Default retention period for audit logs (90 days).\nconst DEFAULT_RETENTION_DAYS: i64 = 90;\n\n/// Maximum number of records to return in a single query.\nconst MAX_QUERY_RESULTS: i64 = 10000;\n\n/// Types of auditable events.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum EventType {\n    /// Worker spawned/created\n    WorkerSpawn,\n    /// Worker killed/stopped\n    WorkerKill,\n    /// Worker paused\n    WorkerPause,\n    /// Worker resumed\n    WorkerResume,\n    /// Bead status changed\n    BeadStatusChange,\n    /// Configuration value changed\n    ConfigChange,\n    /// Database schema migrated\n    SchemaMigration,\n    /// API call recorded (cost tracking)\n    ApiCallRecorded,\n    /// Cost aggregation performed\n    CostAggregation,\n    /// Subscription created/updated\n    SubscriptionChange,\n    /// Task event recorded\n    TaskEvent,\n    /// Manual user action\n    UserAction,\n    /// System automated action\n    SystemAction,\n    /// Error/failure event\n    Error,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for EventType {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::WorkerSpawn => write!(f, \"worker_spawn\"),\n            Self::WorkerKill => write!(f, \"worker_kill\"),\n            Self::WorkerPause => write!(f, \"worker_pause\"),\n            Self::WorkerResume => write!(f, \"worker_resume\"),\n            Self::BeadStatusChange => write!(f, \"bead_status_change\"),\n            Self::ConfigChange => write!(f, \"config_change\"),\n            Self::SchemaMigration => write!(f, \"schema_migration\"),\n            Self::ApiCallRecorded => write!(f, \"api_call_recorded\"),\n            Self::CostAggregation => write!(f, \"cost_aggregation\"),\n            Self::SubscriptionChange => write!(f, \"subscription_change\"),\n            Self::TaskEvent => write!(f, \"task_event\"),\n            Self::UserAction => write!(f, \"user_action\"),\n            Self::SystemAction => write!(f, \"system_action\"),\n            Self::Error => write!(f, \"error\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::str::FromStr for EventType {\n    type Err = String;\n\n    fn from_str(s: &str) -> std::result::Result<Self, Self::Err> {\n        match s {\n            \"worker_spawn\" => Ok(Self::WorkerSpawn),\n            \"worker_kill\" => Ok(Self::WorkerKill),\n            \"worker_pause\" => Ok(Self::WorkerPause),\n            \"worker_resume\" => Ok(Self::WorkerResume),\n            \"bead_status_change\" => Ok(Self::BeadStatusChange),\n            \"config_change\" => Ok(Self::ConfigChange),\n            \"schema_migration\" => Ok(Self::SchemaMigration),\n            \"api_call_recorded\" => Ok(Self::ApiCallRecorded),\n            \"cost_aggregation\" => Ok(Self::CostAggregation),\n            \"subscription_change\" => Ok(Self::SubscriptionChange),\n            \"task_event\" => Ok(Self::TaskEvent),\n            \"user_action\" => Ok(Self::UserAction),\n            \"system_action\" => Ok(Self::SystemAction),\n            \"error\" => Ok(Self::Error),\n            _ => Err(format!(\"unknown event type: {}\", s)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Severity level for audit events.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum Severity {\n    /// Informational event\n    Info,\n    /// Warning condition\n    Warning,\n    /// Error condition\n    Error,\n    /// Critical failure\n    Critical,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for Severity {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Info => write!(f, \"info\"),\n            Self::Warning => write!(f, \"warning\"),\n            Self::Error => write!(f, \"error\"),\n            Self::Critical => write!(f, \"critical\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::str::FromStr for Severity {\n    type Err = String;\n\n    fn from_str(s: &str) -> std::result::Result<Self, Self::Err> {\n        match s {\n            \"info\" => Ok(Self::Info),\n            \"warning\" => Ok(Self::Warning),\n            \"error\" => Ok(Self::Error),\n            \"critical\" => Ok(Self::Critical),\n            _ => Err(format!(\"unknown severity: {}\", s)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A single audit event record.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct AuditEvent {\n    /// When the event occurred\n    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// Type of event\n    pub event_type: EventType,\n    /// Who performed the action (user ID, \"system\", worker_id, etc.)\n    pub actor: String,\n    /// Type of entity affected (worker, bead, config, etc.)\n    pub entity_type: String,\n    /// ID of the specific entity\n    pub entity_id: String,\n    /// Previous state (JSON encoded)\n    pub old_value: Option<String>,\n    /// New state (JSON encoded)\n    pub new_value: Option<String>,\n    /// Additional context (JSON encoded)\n    pub metadata: Option<String>,\n    /// Event severity\n    pub severity: Severity,\n}\n\nimpl AuditEvent {\n    /// Create a new audit event with the current timestamp.\n    pub fn new(\n        event_type: EventType,\n        actor: impl Into<String>,\n        entity_type: impl Into<String>,\n        entity_id: impl Into<String>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            timestamp: Utc::now(),\n            event_type,\n            actor: actor.into(),\n            entity_type: entity_type.into(),\n            entity_id: entity_id.into(),\n            old_value: None,\n            new_value: None,\n            metadata: None,\n            severity: Severity::Info,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the old value (previous state).\n    pub fn with_old_value(mut self, value: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.old_value = Some(value.into());\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the new value (new state).\n    pub fn with_new_value(mut self, value: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.new_value = Some(value.into());\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the metadata (additional context).\n    pub fn with_metadata(mut self, value: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.metadata = Some(value.into());\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the severity level.\n    pub fn with_severity(mut self, severity: Severity) -> Self {\n        self.severity = severity;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Create a worker lifecycle event.\n    pub fn worker_lifecycle(\n        event_type: EventType,\n        worker_id: impl Into<String>,\n        actor: impl Into<String>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self::new(event_type, actor, \"worker\", worker_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Create a bead status change event.\n    pub fn bead_status_change(\n        bead_id: impl Into<String>,\n        old_status: impl Into<String>,\n        new_status: impl Into<String>,\n        actor: impl Into<String>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self::new(EventType::BeadStatusChange, actor, \"bead\", bead_id)\n            .with_old_value(old_status.into())\n            .with_new_value(new_status.into())\n    }\n\n    /// Create a config change event.\n    pub fn config_change(\n        key: impl Into<String>,\n        old_value: impl Into<String>,\n        new_value: impl Into<String>,\n        actor: impl Into<String>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self::new(EventType::ConfigChange, actor, \"config\", key)\n            .with_old_value(old_value.into())\n            .with_new_value(new_value.into())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Filter criteria for querying audit logs.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct AuditFilter {\n    /// Start of time range (inclusive)\n    pub start_time: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    /// End of time range (inclusive)\n    pub end_time: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    /// Filter by event types\n    pub event_types: Option<Vec<EventType>>,\n    /// Filter by actor\n    pub actor: Option<String>,\n    /// Filter by entity type\n    pub entity_type: Option<String>,\n    /// Filter by entity ID\n    pub entity_id: Option<String>,\n    /// Filter by severity\n    pub severity: Option<Severity>,\n    /// Maximum number of results\n    pub limit: Option<i64>,\n    /// Offset for pagination\n    pub offset: Option<i64>,\n}\n\nimpl AuditFilter {\n    /// Create a new empty filter.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Set time range.\n    pub fn with_time_range(mut self, start: DateTime<Utc>, end: DateTime<Utc>) -> Self {\n        self.start_time = Some(start);\n        self.end_time = Some(end);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set event types filter.\n    pub fn with_event_types(mut self, types: Vec<EventType>) -> Self {\n        self.event_types = Some(types);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set actor filter.\n    pub fn with_actor(mut self, actor: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.actor = Some(actor.into());\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set entity filter.\n    pub fn with_entity(mut self, entity_type: impl Into<String>, entity_id: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.entity_type = Some(entity_type.into());\n        self.entity_id = Some(entity_id.into());\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set severity filter.\n    pub fn with_severity(mut self, severity: Severity) -> Self {\n        self.severity = Some(severity);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set pagination.\n    pub fn with_pagination(mut self, limit: i64, offset: i64) -> Self {\n        self.limit = Some(limit);\n        self.offset = Some(offset);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Build SQL WHERE clause and parameters from this filter.\n    fn build_where_clause(&self) -> (String, Vec<Box<dyn rusqlite::ToSql>>) {\n        let mut conditions: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n        let mut params: Vec<Box<dyn rusqlite::ToSql>> = Vec::new();\n\n        if let Some(start) = &self.start_time {\n            conditions.push(\"timestamp >= ?\".to_string());\n            params.push(Box::new(start.to_rfc3339()));\n        }\n        if let Some(end) = &self.end_time {\n            conditions.push(\"timestamp <= ?\".to_string());\n            params.push(Box::new(end.to_rfc3339()));\n        }\n        if let Some(types) = &self.event_types {\n            let placeholders: Vec<String> = (0..types.len()).map(|_| \"?\".to_string()).collect();\n            conditions.push(format!(\"event_type IN ({})\", placeholders.join(\", \")));\n            for t in types {\n                params.push(Box::new(t.to_string()));\n            }\n        }\n        if let Some(actor) = &self.actor {\n            conditions.push(\"actor = ?\".to_string());\n            params.push(Box::new(actor.clone()));\n        }\n        if let Some(entity_type) = &self.entity_type {\n            conditions.push(\"entity_type = ?\".to_string());\n            params.push(Box::new(entity_type.clone()));\n        }\n        if let Some(entity_id) = &self.entity_id {\n            conditions.push(\"entity_id = ?\".to_string());\n            params.push(Box::new(entity_id.clone()));\n        }\n        if let Some(severity) = &self.severity {\n            conditions.push(\"severity = ?\".to_string());\n            params.push(Box::new(severity.to_string()));\n        }\n\n        let where_clause = if conditions.is_empty() {\n            String::new()\n        } else {\n            format!(\"WHERE {}\", conditions.join(\" AND \"))\n        };\n\n        (where_clause, params)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Retention policy for audit logs.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct RetentionPolicy {\n    /// Number of days to retain logs\n    pub days: i64,\n    /// Whether to apply retention on startup\n    pub apply_on_startup: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for RetentionPolicy {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            days: DEFAULT_RETENTION_DAYS,\n            apply_on_startup: true,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl RetentionPolicy {\n    /// Create a new retention policy.\n    pub fn new(days: i64, apply_on_startup: bool) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            days: days.max(1), // At least 1 day\n            apply_on_startup,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the cutoff timestamp for this policy.\n    pub fn cutoff(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {\n        Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::days(self.days)\n    }\n}\n\n/// SQLite-backed audit logger.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AuditLogger {\n    conn: Arc<Mutex<Connection>>,\n    db_path: PathBuf,\n    retention: RetentionPolicy,\n}\n\nimpl AuditLogger {\n    /// Open or create an audit database at the given path.\n    pub fn open<P: AsRef<Path>>(path: P) -> Result<Self> {\n        let db_path = path.as_ref().to_path_buf();\n\n        // Ensure parent directory exists\n        if let Some(parent) = db_path.parent() {\n            fs::create_dir_all(parent)\n                .map_err(|e| ForgeError::io(\"create_dir_all\", parent, e))?;\n        }\n\n        let conn = Connection::open(&db_path)\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::Audit { message: format!(\"failed to open database: {}\", e) })?;\n\n        // Enable WAL mode for better concurrent access\n        // Returns the new mode, so we need to use query_row\n        conn.query_row(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\", [], |row| {\n            row.get::<_, String>(0)\n        })\n        .map_err(|e| ForgeError::Audit { message: format!(\"failed to enable WAL: {}\", e) })?;\n\n        // Set a busy timeout for concurrent access\n        conn.query_row(\"PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000\", [], |row| {\n            row.get::<_, i64>(0)\n        })\n        .map_err(|e| ForgeError::Audit { message: format!(\"failed to set busy timeout: {}\", e) })?;\n\n        let mut logger = Self {\n            conn: Arc::new(Mutex::new(conn)),\n            db_path,\n            retention: RetentionPolicy::default(),\n        };\n\n        logger.migrate()?;\n\n        // Apply retention if configured\n        if logger.retention.apply_on_startup {\n            logger.apply_retention()?;\n        }\n\n        Ok(logger)\n    }\n\n    /// Open with a custom retention policy.\n    pub fn open_with_retention<P: AsRef<Path>>(\n        path: P,\n        retention: RetentionPolicy,\n    ) -> Result<Self> {\n        let mut logger = Self::open(path)?;\n        logger.retention = retention.clone();\n        if retention.apply_on_startup {\n            logger.apply_retention()?;\n        }\n        Ok(logger)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the database path.\n    pub fn path(&self) -> &Path {\n        &self.db_path\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current retention policy.\n    pub fn retention(&self) -> &RetentionPolicy {\n        &self.retention\n    }\n\n    /// Update the retention policy.\n    pub fn set_retention(&mut self, retention: RetentionPolicy) -> Result<()> {\n        self.retention = retention.clone();\n        self.apply_retention().map(|_| ())\n    }\n\n    /// Run database migrations.\n    fn migrate(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        // Create schema version table\n        conn.execute(\n            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_schema_version (\n                version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY\n            )\",\n            [],\n        ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create schema table: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Get current version\n        let current_version: i32 = conn\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT COALESCE(MAX(version), 0) FROM audit_schema_version\",\n                [],\n                |row| row.get(0),\n            )\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        if current_version < AUDIT_SCHEMA_VERSION {\n            info!(\n                current = current_version,\n                target = AUDIT_SCHEMA_VERSION,\n                \"Running audit database migrations\"\n            );\n\n            // Create audit logs table\n            conn.execute(\n                \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (\n                    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                    timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    actor TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    entity_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    entity_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    old_value TEXT,\n                    new_value TEXT,\n                    metadata TEXT,\n                    severity TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'info'\n                )\",\n                [],\n            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create audit_logs table: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            // Create indexes for efficient queries\n            conn.execute(\n                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_timestamp\n                 ON audit_logs(timestamp DESC)\",\n                [],\n            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create timestamp index: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            conn.execute(\n                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_event_type\n                 ON audit_logs(event_type)\",\n                [],\n            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create event_type index: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            conn.execute(\n                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_actor\n                 ON audit_logs(actor)\",\n                [],\n            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create actor index: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            conn.execute(\n                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_entity\n                 ON audit_logs(entity_type, entity_id)\",\n                [],\n            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create entity index: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            conn.execute(\n                \"CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_logs_severity\n                 ON audit_logs(severity)\",\n                [],\n            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create severity index: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            // Record schema version\n            conn.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO audit_schema_version (version) VALUES (?)\",\n                params![AUDIT_SCHEMA_VERSION],\n            ).map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to record schema version: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            info!(\"Audit database initialized at schema version {}\", AUDIT_SCHEMA_VERSION);\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Log an audit event.\n    pub fn log(&self, event: AuditEvent) -> Result<i64> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        debug!(\n            event_type = %event.event_type,\n            actor = %event.actor,\n            entity = %format!(\"{}:{}\", event.entity_type, event.entity_id),\n            \"Logging audit event\"\n        );\n\n        conn.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO audit_logs (\n                timestamp, event_type, actor, entity_type, entity_id,\n                old_value, new_value, metadata, severity\n            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\",\n            params![\n                event.timestamp.to_rfc3339(),\n                event.event_type.to_string(),\n                event.actor,\n                event.entity_type,\n                event.entity_id,\n                event.old_value,\n                event.new_value,\n                event.metadata,\n                event.severity.to_string(),\n            ],\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to insert audit log: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        Ok(conn.last_insert_rowid())\n    }\n\n    /// Query audit logs with optional filters.\n    pub fn query(&self, filter: &AuditFilter) -> Result<Vec<AuditEvent>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let (where_clause, params) = filter.build_where_clause();\n        let limit = filter.limit.unwrap_or(MAX_QUERY_RESULTS);\n        let offset = filter.offset.unwrap_or(0);\n\n        let sql = format!(\n            \"SELECT timestamp, event_type, actor, entity_type, entity_id,\n                    old_value, new_value, metadata, severity\n             FROM audit_logs\n             {}\n             ORDER BY timestamp DESC\n             LIMIT {} OFFSET {}\",\n            where_clause, limit, offset\n        );\n\n        let mut stmt = conn\n            .prepare(&sql)\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to prepare query: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = params.iter().map(|p| p.as_ref()).collect();\n\n        // Helper to parse timestamp from string\n        let parse_timestamp = |s: String| -> std::result::Result<DateTime<Utc>, String> {\n            DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&s)\n                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc))\n                .map_err(|e| format!(\"invalid timestamp: {}\", e))\n        };\n\n        // Helper to parse event type from string\n        let parse_event_type = |s: String| -> std::result::Result<EventType, String> {\n            s.parse().map_err(|e| format!(\"invalid event type: {}\", e))\n        };\n\n        // Helper to parse severity from string\n        let parse_severity = |s: String| -> std::result::Result<Severity, String> {\n            s.parse().map_err(|e| format!(\"invalid severity: {}\", e))\n        };\n\n        let events = stmt\n            .query_map(param_refs.as_slice(), |row| {\n                let ts_str: String = row.get(0)?;\n                let event_type_str: String = row.get(1)?;\n                let severity_str: String = row.get(8)?;\n\n                Ok(AuditEvent {\n                    timestamp: parse_timestamp(ts_str).map_err(|e| {\n                        rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(\n                            Box::new(ParseError(e))\n                        )\n                    })?,\n                    event_type: parse_event_type(event_type_str).map_err(|e| {\n                        rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(\n                            Box::new(ParseError(e))\n                        )\n                    })?,\n                    actor: row.get(2)?,\n                    entity_type: row.get(3)?,\n                    entity_id: row.get(4)?,\n                    old_value: row.get(5)?,\n                    new_value: row.get(6)?,\n                    metadata: row.get(7)?,\n                    severity: parse_severity(severity_str).map_err(|e| {\n                        rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(\n                            Box::new(ParseError(e))\n                        )\n                    })?,\n                })\n            })\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to execute query: {}\", e)))?\n            .collect::<std::result::Result<Vec<_>, _>>()\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to parse results: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        Ok(events)\n    }\n\n    /// Get a count of audit logs matching the filter.\n    pub fn count(&self, filter: &AuditFilter) -> Result<i64> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let (where_clause, params) = filter.build_where_clause();\n\n        let sql = format!(\n            \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_logs {}\",\n            where_clause\n        );\n\n        let mut stmt = conn\n            .prepare(&sql)\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to prepare count query: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = params.iter().map(|p| p.as_ref()).collect();\n\n        let count: i64 = stmt\n            .query_row(param_refs.as_slice(), |row| row.get(0))\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to execute count query: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        Ok(count)\n    }\n\n    /// Get audit logs for a specific entity.\n    pub fn get_entity_history(\n        &self,\n        entity_type: &str,\n        entity_id: &str,\n        limit: Option<i64>,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<AuditEvent>> {\n        let filter = AuditFilter::new()\n            .with_entity(entity_type, entity_id)\n            .with_pagination(limit.unwrap_or(1000), 0);\n\n        self.query(&filter)\n    }\n\n    /// Apply retention policy - delete old logs.\n    pub fn apply_retention(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n        let cutoff = self.retention.cutoff();\n        let cutoff_str = cutoff.to_rfc3339();\n\n        info!(\n            cutoff = %cutoff_str,\n            days = self.retention.days,\n            \"Applying audit log retention\"\n        );\n\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let affected = conn\n            .execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM audit_logs WHERE timestamp < ?\",\n                params![cutoff_str],\n            )\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to apply retention: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        if affected > 0 {\n            info!(\n                deleted = affected,\n                \"Deleted audit logs past retention period\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(affected)\n    }\n\n    /// Export audit logs to JSON.\n    pub fn export_json(&self, filter: &AuditFilter, output: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n        let events = self.query(filter)?;\n\n        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&events)\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to serialize JSON: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        fs::write(output, json)\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to write export: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        info!(\n            count = events.len(),\n            path = %output.display(),\n            \"Exported audit logs to JSON\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Export audit logs to CSV.\n    pub fn export_csv(&self, filter: &AuditFilter, output: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n        let events = self.query(filter)?;\n\n        let mut wtr = csv::Writer::from_path(output)\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to create CSV writer: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Write header\n        wtr.write_record(&[\n            \"timestamp\", \"event_type\", \"actor\", \"entity_type\", \"entity_id\",\n            \"old_value\", \"new_value\", \"metadata\", \"severity\",\n        ])\n        .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to write CSV header: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Write records\n        for event in &events {\n            wtr.write_record(&[\n                event.timestamp.to_rfc3339().as_str(),\n                &event.event_type.to_string(),\n                &event.actor,\n                &event.entity_type,\n                &event.entity_id,\n                event.old_value.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n                event.new_value.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n                event.metadata.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n                &event.severity.to_string(),\n            ])\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to write CSV record: {}\", e)))?;\n        }\n\n        wtr.flush()\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to flush CSV: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        info!(\n            count = events.len(),\n            path = %output.display(),\n            \"Exported audit logs to CSV\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get statistics about the audit log.\n    pub fn stats(&self) -> Result<AuditStats> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let total_events: i64 = conn\n            .query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_logs\", [], |row| row.get(0))\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        let oldest_event: Option<String> = conn\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT MIN(timestamp) FROM audit_logs\",\n                [],\n                |row| row.get(0),\n            )\n            .unwrap_or(None);\n\n        let newest_event: Option<String> = conn\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT MAX(timestamp) FROM audit_logs\",\n                [],\n                |row| row.get(0),\n            )\n            .unwrap_or(None);\n\n        // Count by event type\n        let mut type_counts = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n        let mut stmt = conn\n            .prepare(\n                \"SELECT event_type, COUNT(*) as count FROM audit_logs GROUP BY event_type\"\n            )\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to prepare stats query: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let rows = stmt\n            .query_map([], |row| {\n                Ok((\n                    row.get::<_, String>(0)?,\n                    row.get::<_, i64>(1)?\n                ))\n            })\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to execute stats query: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        for row in rows {\n            if let Ok((event_type, count)) = row {\n                type_counts.insert(event_type, count);\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(AuditStats {\n            total_events,\n            oldest_timestamp: oldest_event.and_then(|s| DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&s).ok())\n                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc)),\n            newest_timestamp: newest_event.and_then(|s| DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&s).ok())\n                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc)),\n            event_type_counts: type_counts,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Vacuum the database to reclaim space.\n    pub fn vacuum(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        info!(\"Vacuuming audit database\");\n\n        conn.execute(\"VACUUM\", [])\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::audit_error(format!(\"failed to vacuum: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Statistics about the audit log.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct AuditStats {\n    /// Total number of events\n    pub total_events: i64,\n    /// Oldest event timestamp\n    pub oldest_timestamp: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    /// Newest event timestamp\n    pub newest_timestamp: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    /// Count of events by type\n    pub event_type_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, i64>,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use tempfile::tempdir;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_audit_event_creation() {\n        let event = AuditEvent::worker_lifecycle(\n            EventType::WorkerSpawn,\n            \"worker-1\",\n            \"user\"\n        )\n        .with_new_value(r#\"{\"status\":\"active\"}\"#)\n        .with_severity(Severity::Info);\n\n \"worker-1\");\n        assert_eq!(event.severity, Severity::Info);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_audit_logger() {\n        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();\n        let db_path = EventType::WorkerSpawn,\n            \"worker-test\",\n            \"test\"\n        );\n        let id = logger.log(event).unwrap();\n        assert!(id > 0);\n\n        // Query it back\n        let assert_eq!(results[0].event_type, EventType::WorkerSpawn);\n        assert_eq!(results[0].entity_id, \"worker-test\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_filter_building() {\n        let filter = AuditFilter::new()\n            .with_time_range(\n                DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(\"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\").unwrap().with_timezone(&Utc),\n                DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(\"2024-12-31T23:59:59Z\").unwrap().with_timezone(&Utc),\n            )\n            .with_actor(\"user\")\n            .with_entity(\"worker\", \"worker-1\");\n\n        let (where_clause, params) = filter.build_where_clause();\n\n        assert!(where_clause.contains(\"timestamp >=\"));\n        assert!(where_clause.contains(\"timestamp <=\"));\n        assert!(where_clause.contains(\"actor =\"));\n        assert!(where_clause.contains(\"entity_type =\"));\n        assert!(where_clause.contains(\"entity_id =\"));\n        assert_eq!(params.len(), 5);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_event_type_from_str() {\n        assert_eq!(\n            \"worker_spawn\".parse::<EventType>().unwrap(),\n            EventType::WorkerSpawn\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            \"config_change\".parse::<EventType>().unwrap(),\n            EventType::ConfigChange\n        );\n        assert!(\"unknown\".parse::<EventType>().is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_severity_from_str() {\n        assert_eq!(\"info\".parse::<Severity>().unwrap(), Severity::Info);\n        assert_eq!(\"critical\".parse::<Severity>().unwrap(), Severity::Critical);\n        assert!(\"unknown\".parse::<Severity>().is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_retention_policy() {\n        let policy = RetentionPolicy::new(30, false);\n        assert_eq!(policy.days, 30);\n        assert!(!policy.apply_on_startup);\n\n        let cutoff = policy.cutoff();\n        let now = Utc::now();\n        let days_diff = (now - cutoff).num_days();\n        assert!((days_diff - 30).abs() <= 1); // Allow 1 day tolerance\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_stats() {\n        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();\n        let db_path = \"w1\", \"u1\")).unwrap();\n        logger.log(AuditEvent::worker_lifecycle(EventType::WorkerSpawn, \"w2\", \"u1\")).unwrap();\n        logger.log(AuditEvent::worker_lifecycle(EventType::WorkerKill, {\n332\t        // Ultra-wide mode requirements\n333\t        assert!(LayoutMode::UltraWide.meets_requirements(199, 38));\n334\t        assert!(LayoutMode::UltraWide.meets_requirements(250, 50));\n335\t        assert!(!LayoutMode::UltraWide.meets_requirements(199, 37)); // Height too short\n336\t        assert!(!LayoutMode::UltraWide.meets_requirements(198, 38)); // Width too narrow\n337\t\n338\t        // Wide mode requirements\n339\t        assert!(LayoutMode::Wide.meets_requirements(150, 30));\n340\t        assert!(LayoutMode::Wide.meets_requirements(120, 35));\n341\t        assert!(!LayoutMode::Wide.meets_requirements(150, 29)); // Height too short\n342\t        assert!(!LayoutMode::Wide.meets_requirements(199, 30)); // Width triggers UltraWide\n343\t\n344\t        // Narrow mode requirements\n345\t        assert!(LayoutMode::Narrow.meets_requirements(80, 20));\n346\t        assert!(LayoutMode::Narrow.meets_requirements(100, 25));\n347\t        assert!(!LayoutMode::Narrow.meets_requirements(80, 19)); // Height too short\n348\t        assert!(!LayoutMode::Narrow.meets_requirements(120, 20)); // Width triggers Wide\n349\t    }\n350\t\n351\t    #[test]\n352\t    fn test_layout_mode_boundary_conditions() {\n353\t        // Test exact boundary values\n354\t        assert_eq!(LayoutMode::from_width(198), LayoutMode::Wide);\n355\t        assert_eq!(LayoutMode::from_width(199), LayoutMode::UltraWide);\n356\t\n357\t        assert_eq!(LayoutMode::from_width(119), LayoutMode::Narrow);\n358\t        assert_eq!(LayoutMode::from_width(120), LayoutMode::Wide);\n359\t View types and navigation for the FORGE TUI.\n//!\n//! Views represent the different screens/modes available in the dashboard.\n\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Available views in the FORGE dashboard.\n///\n/// Each view represents a distinct screen with its own content and interactions.\n/// Views can be switched using hotkeys or the Tab key to cycle.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]\npub enum View {\n    /// Overview/dashboard showing summary of all components\n    #[default]\n    Overview,\n    /// Worker pool management and status\n    Workers,\n    /// Task queue and bead management\n    Tasks,\n    /// Cost analytics and optimization\n    Costs,\n    /// Performance metrics and statistics\n    Metrics,\n    /// FORGE internal performance (event loop, render time, memory)\n    Perf,\n    /// Activity log viewer\n    Logs,\n    /// Subscription tracking and utilization\n    Subscriptions,\n    /// Worker health alerts and notifications\n    Alerts,\n    /// Model routing analytics and complexity scoring\n    Routing,\n    /// Conversational chat interface (activated with `:`)\n    Chat,\n    /// Multi-workspace management and switching\n    Workspaces,\n    /// Audit log viewer for compliance tracking\n    Audit,\n}\n\nimpl View {\n    /// Returns the hotkey character for this view.\n    pub fn hotkey(&self) -> char {\n        match self {\n            View::Overview => 'o',\n            View::Workers => 'w',\n            View::Tasks => 't',\n            View::Costs => 'c',\n            View::Metrics => 'm',\n            View::Perf => 'p',\n            View::Logs => 'l',\n            View::Subscriptions => 'u',\n            View::Alerts => 'a',\n            View::Routing => 'r',\n            View::Chat => ':',\n            View::Workspaces => 'W',\n            View::Audit => 'Z',\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the display title for this view.\n    pub fn title(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            View::Overview => \"Overview\",\n            View::Workers => \"Workers\",\n            View::Tasks => \"Tasks\",\n            View::Costs => \"Costs\",\n            View::Metrics => \"Metrics\",\n            View::Perf => \"Perf\",\n            View::Logs => \"Logs\",\n            View::Subscriptions => \"Subscriptions\",\n            View::Alerts => \"Alerts\",\n            View::Routing => \"Routing\",\n            View::Chat => \"Chat\",\n            View::Workspaces => \"Workspaces\",\n            View::Audit => \"Audit\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the hotkey hint for status bar display.\n    pub fn hotkey_hint(&self) -> String {\n        format!(\"[{}] {}\", self.hotkey(), self.title())\n    }\n\n    /// All views in display order (for Tab cycling).\n    pub const ALL: [View; 13] = [\n        View::Overview,\n        View::Workers,\n        View::Tasks,\n        View::Costs,\n        View::Metrics,\n        View::Perf,\n        View::Logs,\n        View::Subscriptions,\n        View::Alerts,\n        View::Routing,\n        View::Chat,\n        View::Workspaces,\n        View::Audit,\n    ];\n\n    /// Returns the next view in the cycle (for Tab navigation).\n    pub fn next(&self) -> View {\n        let idx = Self::ALL.iter().position(|v| v == self).unwrap_or(0);\n        Self::ALL[(idx + 1) % Self::ALL.len()]\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the previous view in the cycle (for Shift+Tab navigation).\n    pub fn prev(&self) -> View {\n        let idx = Self::ALL.iter().position(|v| v == self).unwrap_or(0);\n        if idx == 0 {\n            Self::ALL[Self::ALL.len() - 1]\n        } else {\n            Self::ALL[idx - 1]\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Try to parse a view from a hotkey character.\n    pub fn from_hotkey(key: char) -> Option<View> {\n        match key {\n            'o' | 'O' => Some(View::Overview),\n            'w' => Some(View::Workers), // lowercase only\n            'W' => Some(View::Workspaces), // uppercase only\n            't' | 'T' => Some(View::Tasks),\n            'c' | 'C' => Some(View::Costs),\n            'm' | 'M' => Some(View::Metrics),\n            'p' | 'P' => Some(View::Perf),\n            'l' | 'L' => Some(View::Logs),\n            'u' | 'U' => Some(View::Subscriptions),\n            'a' | 'A' => Some(View::Alerts),\n            'r' | 'R' => Some(View::Routing),\n            ':' => Some(View::Chat),\n            'Z' => Some(View::Audit),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for View {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"{}\", self.title())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Focus state for panels within a view.\n///\n/// Tracks which panel has keyboard focus for navigation and highlighting.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]\npub enum FocusPanel {\n    /// No specific panel focused (view-level focus)\n    #[default]\n    None,\n    /// Worker pool panel\n    WorkerPool,\n    /// Subscription/usage panel\n    Subscriptions,\n    /// Task queue panel\n    TaskQueue,\n    /// Activity log panel\n    ActivityLog,\n    /// Cost breakdown panel\n    CostBreakdown,\n    /// Metrics charts panel\n    MetricsCharts,\n    /// Perf metrics panel\n    PerfCharts,\n    /// Chat input panel\n    ChatInput,\n    /// Alert list panel\n    AlertList,\n}\n\nimpl FocusPanel {\n    /// Returns whether this panel should show a highlight border.\n    pub fn is_highlighted(&self) -> bool {\n        !matches!(self, FocusPanel::None)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Layout mode based on terminal dimensions.\n///\n/// The TUI adapts its layout based on available screen real estate.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum LayoutMode {\n    /// Ultra-wide layout (199+ cols): 3-column with all 6 panels visible.\n    /// Left (66 cols): Workers + Subscriptions\n    /// Middle (66 cols): Tasks + Activity\n    /// Right (65 cols): Costs + Actions\n    UltraWide,\n    /// Wide layout (120-198 cols): 2-column with 4 panels visible.\n    Wide,\n    /// Narrow layout (<120 cols): single-view mode.\n    Narrow,\n}\n\nimpl LayoutMode {\n    /// Determine the layout mode based on terminal width.\n    pub fn from_width(width: u16) -> Self {\n        if width >= 199 {\n            LayoutMode::UltraWide\n        } else if width >= 120 {\n            LayoutMode::Wide\n        } else {\n            LayoutMode::Narrow\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the minimum terminal height for this layout mode.\n    pub fn min_height(&self) -> u16 {\n        match self {\n            LayoutMode::UltraWide => 38,\n            LayoutMode::Wide => 30,\n            LayoutMode::Narrow => 20,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the terminal dimensions meet the layout requirements.\n    pub fn meets_requirements(&self, width: u16, height: u16) -> bool {\n        let required_mode = Self::from_width(width);\n        let height_ok = height >= self.min_height();\n\n        // Mode is valid if it matches width requirement and height is sufficient\n        *self == required_mode && height_ok\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_view_hotkeys() {\n        assert_eq!(View::Overview.hotkey(), 'o');\n        assert_eq!(View::Workers.hotkey(), 'w');\n        assert_eq!(View::Tasks.hotkey(), 't');\n        assert_eq!(View::Costs.hotkey(), 'c');\n        assert_eq!(View::Metrics.hotkey(), 'm');\n        assert_eq!(View::Logs.hotkey(), 'l');\n        assert_eq!(View::Subscriptions.hotkey(), 'u');\n        assert_eq!(View::Alerts.hotkey(), 'a');\n        assert_eq!(View::Routing.hotkey(), 'r');\n        assert_eq!(View::Chat.hotkey(), ':');\n        assert_eq!(View::Workspaces.hotkey(), 'W');\n        assert_eq!(View::Audit.hotkey(), 'Z');\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_view_from_hotkey() {\n        assert_eq!(View::from_hotkey('o'), Some(View::Overview));\n        assert_eq!(View::from_hotkey('w'), Some(View::Workers)); // lowercase for Workers\n        assert_eq!(View::from_hotkey('W'), Some(View::Workspaces)); // uppercase for Workspaces\n        assert_eq!(View::from_hotkey('u'), Some(View::Subscriptions));\n        assert_eq!(View::from_hotkey('a'), Some(View::Alerts));\n        assert_eq!(View::from_hotkey('r'), Some(View::Routing));\n        assert_eq!(View::from_hotkey(':'), Some(View::Chat));\n        assert_eq!(View::from_hotkey('Z'), Some(View::Audit));\n        assert_eq!(View::from_hotkey('x'), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_view_cycling() {\n        assert_eq!(View::Overview.next(), View::Workers);\n        assert_eq!(View::Workspaces.next(), View::Audit); // Audit is after Workspaces\n        assert_eq!(View::Audit.next(), View::Overview); // wraps around\n        assert_eq!(View::Overview.prev(), View::Audit); // wraps around\n        assert_eq!(View::Audit.prev(), View::Workspaces);\n        assert_eq!(View::Workers.prev(), View::Overview);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_view_titles() {\n        assert_eq!(View::Overview.title(), \"Overview\");\n        assert_eq!(View::Workers.title(), \"Workers\");\n        assert_eq!(View::Alerts.title(), \"Alerts\");\n        assert_eq!(View::Routing.title(), \"Routing\");\n        assert_eq!(View::Chat.title(), \"Chat\");\n        assert_eq!(View::Workspaces.title(), \"Workspaces\");\n        assert_eq!(View::Audit.title(), \"Audit\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_hotkey_hint() {\n        assert_eq!(View::Overview.hotkey_hint(), \"[o] Overview\");\n assert_eq!(View::Workspaces.hotkey_hint(), \"[W] Workspaces\");\n        assert_eq!(View::Audit.hotkey_hint(), \"[Z] Audit\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_default_view() {\n // Height too short\n        assert!(!LayoutMode::UltraWide.meets_requirements(198, 38)); // Width too narrow\n\n        // Wide mode requirements\n Ok(())\n//! }\n//! ```\n\npub mod activity_monitor;\npub mod audit;\npub mod deps;\npub mod error;\npub mod logging;\npub mod recovery;\n#[cfg(feature = \"self-update\")]\npub mod self_update;\npub friendly_error_message, retry_with_backoff, retry_with_backoff_async, RecoveryAction,\n    RetryConfig, RetryResult, Retryable,\n};\npub use status::{StatusReader, StatusWriter, WorkerStatusInfo};\npub use watcher::{StatusEvent, StatusWatcher, WatcherConfig};\n\n// Re-export dependency checking utilities\npub use deps::{check_and_report as check_dependencies, check_dependencies as dependency_check, DependencyCheck};\n\n// Re-export self_update types when feature is enabled\n#[cfg(feature = \"self-update\")]\npub use self_update::{\n    check_and_perform_self_install, check_and_rollback, check_for_update,\n    did_previous_startup_crash, mark_startup_in_progress, mark_startup_successful,\n    perform_update, read_last_version,
