Configuration management for FORGE.\n//!\n//! This crate handles loading, validating, and managing FORGE configuration\n//! from `~/.forge/config.yaml`.\n\nuse std::io;\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse thiserror::Error;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Default config file path (~/.forge/config.yaml).\npub fn config_path() -> Option<PathBuf> {\n    dirs::home_dir().map(|h| h.join(\".forge/config.yaml\"))\n}\n\n/// Format a YAML parse error with line/column information if available.\nfn format_yaml_error(error: &serde_yaml::Error) -> String {\n    if let Some(location) = error.location() {\n        format!(\n            \"YAML parse error at line {}, column {}: {}\",\n            location.line(),\n            location.column(),\n            error\n        )\n    } else {\n        format!(\"YAML parse error: {}\", error)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Errors that can occur when loading configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Error)]\npub enum ConfigLoadError {\n    /// Could not determine home directory\n    #[error(\"Could not determine home directory\")]\n    NoHomePath,\n\n    /// Config file not found\n    #[error(\"Config file not found: {0}\")]\n    NotFound(PathBuf),\n\n    /// Error reading config file\n    #[error(\"Failed to read config file {path}: {error}\")]\n    ReadError {\n        path: PathBuf,\n        #[source]\n        error: io::Error,\n    },\n\n    /// Error parsing YAML\n    #[error(\"Failed to parse config YAML in {path}: {}\", format_yaml_error(error))]\n    ParseError {\n        path: PathBuf,\n        error: serde_yaml::Error,\n    },\n\n    /// Config validation failed\n    #[error(\"Config validation failed: {0}\")]\n    ValidationError(String),\n}\n\nimpl From<String> for ConfigLoadError {\n    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n        ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(s)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ConfigLoadError {\n    /// Get the line number where the error occurred (if available).\n    pub fn line_number(&self) -> Option<usize> {\n        match self {\n            ConfigLoadError::ParseError { error, .. } => {\n                error.location().map(|loc| loc.line())\n            }\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the column number where the error occurred (if available).\n    pub fn column_number(&self) -> Option<usize> {\n        match self {\n            ConfigLoadError::ParseError { error, .. } => {\n                error.location().map(|loc| loc.column())\n            }\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the config file path (if applicable).\n    pub fn path(&self) -> Option<&PathBuf> {\n        match self {\n            ConfigLoadError::NotFound(path)\n            | ConfigLoadError::ReadError { path, .. }\n            | ConfigLoadError::ParseError { path, .. } => Some(path),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Multi-workspace configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct MultiWorkspaceConfig {\n    /// Enable multi-workspace mode\n    #[serde(default = \"default_multi_workspace_enabled\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n\n    /// List of workspace paths to monitor\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub workspace_paths: HashMap<String, String>,\n\n    /// Path to workspace registry file\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub registry_path: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for MultiWorkspaceConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: default_multi_workspace_enabled(),\n            workspace_paths: HashMap::new(),\n            registry_path: None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nfn default_multi_workspace_enabled() -> bool {\n    false\n}\n\n/// Forge configuration structure.\n///\n/// This represents the subset of config.yaml that can be hot-reloaded.\n/// Changes to these fields will take effect immediately.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct ForgeConfig {\n    /// Dashboard configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub dashboard: DashboardConfig,\n\n    /// Theme configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub theme: ThemeConfig,\n\n    /// Cost tracking configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub cost_tracking: CostTrackingConfig,\n\n    /// Auto-recovery configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub auto_recovery: AutoRecoveryConfig,\n\n    /// Worker defaults configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub workers: WorkerConfig,\n\n    /// Notification configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub notifications: NotificationsConfig,\n\n    /// Multi-workspace configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub workspaces: MultiWorkspaceConfig,\n\n    /// Audit logging configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub audit: AuditConfig,\n}\n\nimpl ForgeConfig {\n    /// Load configuration from the default path (~/.forge/config.yaml).\n    ///\n    /// Returns default configuration if the file doesn't exist or is invalid.\n    /// Invalid configs are logged as warnings but don't prevent startup.\n    pub fn load() -> Option<Self> {\n        let path = config_path()?;\n        Self::load_from(&path)\n    }\n\n    /// Load configuration from the default path with detailed error reporting.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a Result with detailed error information for display to users.\n    pub fn load_with_error() -> Result<Self, ConfigLoadError> {\n        let path = config_path().ok_or(ConfigLoadError::NoHomePath)?;\n        Self::load_from_with_error(&path)\n    }\n\n    /// Load configuration from a specific path with graceful fallback.\n    ///\n    /// This method attempts to load and parse the config file. If the file\n    /// doesn't exist, is unreadable, or contains invalid YAML, it returns\n    /// the default configuration rather than failing.\n    ///\n    /// Errors are logged but don't prevent the application from starting.\n    pub fn load_from(path: &PathBuf) -> Option<Self> {\n        if !path.exists() {\n            tracing::debug!(\"Config file does not exist: {:?} - using defaults\", path);\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        // Try to read the file\n        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(c) => c,\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    path = ?path,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"Failed to read config file - using defaults\"\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Try to parse with fallback to partial parsing\n        Self::parse_with_fallback(&content)\n    }\n\n    /// Load configuration from a specific path with detailed error reporting.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a Result with detailed error information for display to users.\n    pub fn load_from_with_error(path: &PathBuf) -> Result<Self, ConfigLoadError> {\n        if !path.exists() {\n            return Err(ConfigLoadError::NotFound(path.clone()));\n        }\n\n        // Try to read the file\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n            .map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ReadError {\n                path: path.clone(),\n                error: e,\n            })?;\n\n        // Try to parse as full YAML\n        match serde_yaml::from_str::<ForgeConfig>(&content) {\n            Ok(config) => {\n                // Validate and return errors if invalid\n                config.validate()?;\n                Ok(config)\n            }\n            Err(e) => Err(ConfigLoadError::ParseError {\n                path: path.clone(),\n                error: e,\n            }),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Parse configuration from YAML string.\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if parsing fails completely.\n    pub fn parse(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        Self::parse_with_fallback(content)\n    }\n\n    /// Parse configuration with fallback for partial/invalid configs.\n    ///\n    /// This method:\n    /// 1. Tries to parse the full config\n    /// 2. Falls back to partial parsing if sections are invalid\n    /// 3. Returns default for completely invalid YAML\n    fn parse_with_fallback(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        // First try to parse as full YAML\n        match serde_yaml::from_str::<ForgeConfig>(content) {\n            Ok(config) => {\n                // Validate and warn about issues, but still return the config\n                if let Err(e) = config.validate() {\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"Config validation warning - some settings may be ignored\"\n                    );\n                }\n                tracing::debug!(\"Successfully parsed forge config\");\n                Some(config)\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                // Format detailed error message with line/column information\n                let error_msg = format_yaml_error(&e);\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %error_msg,\n                    \"Failed to parse config YAML - attempting partial parse\"\n                );\n\n                // Try to parse individual sections as a fallback\n                Self::parse_partial(content)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Attempt to parse individual sections of a malformed config.\n    ///\n    /// This allows partial configs to work even if one section has errors.\n    fn parse_partial(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        // Try to parse as generic YAML first\n        let yaml: serde_yaml::Value = match serde_yaml::from_str(content) {\n            Ok(y) => y,\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"Config is not valid YAML - using defaults\"\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Try to extract individual sections\n        let dashboard = yaml\n            .get(\"dashboard\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let theme = yaml\n            .get(\"theme\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let cost_tracking = yaml\n            .get(\"cost_tracking\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let auto_recovery = yaml\n            .get(\"auto_recovery\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let workers = yaml\n            .get(\"workers\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let notifications = yaml\n            .get(\"notifications\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let workspaces = yaml\n            .get(\"workspaces\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        tracing::info!(\"Loaded partial config - some sections may use defaults\");\n\n        Some(Self {\n            dashboard,\n            theme,\n            cost_tracking,\n            auto_recovery,\n            workers,\n            notifications,\n            workspaces,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Validate the configuration.\n    ///\n    /// Returns Ok(()) if valid, Err with message if invalid.\n    /// Warnings are logged but don't cause validation failure.\n    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n        let mut warnings = Vec::new();\n\n        // Validate refresh interval (must be >= 100ms)\n        if self.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms < 100 {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"refresh_interval_ms {} is too low, using minimum of 100ms\",\n                self.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Validate max_fps (must be 1-120)\n        if self.dashboard.max_fps == 0 || self.dashboard.max_fps > 120 {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"max_fps {} is invalid, must be between 1 and 120\",\n                self.dashboard.max_fps\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Validate budget thresholds (must be 0-100)\n        if self.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold > 100 {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"budget_warning_threshold {} exceeds 100%\",\n                self.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if self.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold > 100 {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"budget_critical_threshold {} exceeds 100%\",\n                self.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Validate theme name if specified\n        if let Some(ref theme_name) = self.theme.name {\n            let valid_themes = [\"default\", \"dark\", \"light\", \"cyberpunk\"];\n            if !valid_themes.contains(&theme_name.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n                warnings.push(format!(\n                    \"Invalid theme '{}', valid themes: {:?}\",\n                    theme_name, valid_themes\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Validate max workers\n        if self.workers.max_workers == 0 {\n            warnings.push(\"max_workers must be at least 1\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        // Validate default model\n        let valid_models = [\"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\", \"glm\"];\n        if !valid_models.contains(&self.workers.default_model.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"Invalid default_model '{}', valid models: {:?}\",\n                self.workers.default_model, valid_models\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Validate sonnet costs (must be non-negative)\n        if self.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input < 0.0 {\n            warnings.push(\"sonnet_cost_per_1k_input must be non-negative\".to_string());\n        }\n        if self.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output < 0.0 {\n            warnings.push(\"sonnet_cost_per_1k_output must be non-negative\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        if warnings.is_empty() {\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            // Return the first warning as the error message\n            Err(warnings.join(\"; \"))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Sanitize the configuration by fixing invalid values.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a new config with all invalid values replaced by defaults.\n    pub fn sanitized(&self) -> Self {\n        let mut config = self.clone();\n\n        // Sanitize refresh interval\n        if config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms < 100 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms,\n                \"Sanitizing refresh_interval_ms to minimum 100ms\"\n            );\n            config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms = 100;\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize max_fps\n        if config.dashboard.max_fps == 0 || config.dashboard.max_fps > 120 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.dashboard.max_fps,\n                \"Sanitizing max_fps to default 60\"\n            );\n            config.dashboard.max_fps = 60;\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize budget thresholds\n        if config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold > 100 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold,\n                \"Sanitizing budget_warning_threshold to 100\"\n            );\n            config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold = 100;\n        }\n\n        if config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold > 100 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold,\n                \"Sanitizing budget_critical_threshold to 100\"\n            );\n            config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold = 100;\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize theme name\n        if let Some(ref theme_name) = config.theme.name {\n            let valid_themes = [\"default\", \"dark\", \"light\", \"cyberpunk\"];\n            if !valid_themes.contains(&theme_name.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    original = theme_name,\n                    \"Sanitizing invalid theme name to default\"\n                );\n                config.theme.name = None;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize max workers\n        if config.workers.max_workers == 0 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.workers.max_workers,\n                \"Sanitizing max_workers to minimum 1\"\n            );\n            config.workers.max_workers = 1;\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize default model\n        let valid_models = [\"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\", \"glm\"];\n        if !valid_models.contains(&config.workers.default_model.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.workers.default_model,\n                \"Sanitizing invalid default_model to sonnet\"\n            );\n            config.workers.default_model = \"sonnet\".to_string();\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize sonnet costs\n        if config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input < 0.0 {\n            config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input = 0.003;\n        }\n        if config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output < 0.0 {\n            config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output = 0.015;\n        }\n\n        config\n    }\n\n    /// Save configuration to a file.\n    ///\n    /// Writes the configuration as YAML to the specified path.\n    /// Creates parent directories if they don't exist.\n    pub fn save_to(&self, path: &PathBuf) -> Result<(), ConfigLoadError> {\n        // Create parent directory if needed\n        if let Some(parent) = path.parent()\n            && !parent.exists() {\n                std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(\n                    format!(\"Failed to create config directory: {}\", e)\n                ))?;\n        }\n\n        // Serialize to YAML\n        let yaml = serde_yaml::to_string(self)\n            .map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(\n                format!(\"Failed to serialize config: {}\", e)\n            ))?;\n\n        // Write to file\n        std::fs::write(path, yaml)\n            .map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(\n                format!(\"Failed to write config file: {}\", e)\n            ))?;\n\n        tracing::info!(\"Saved configuration to {:?}\", path);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Save configuration to the default path (~/.forge/config.yaml).\n    pub fn save(&self) -> Result<(), ConfigLoadError> {\n        let path = config_path().ok_or(ConfigLoadError::NoHomePath)?;\n        self.save_to(&path)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Dashboard configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct DashboardConfig {\n    /// Refresh interval in milliseconds.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_refresh_interval\")]\n    pub refresh_interval_ms: u64,\n\n    /// Maximum frames per second.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_fps\")]\n    pub max_fps: u64,\n\n    /// Default layout mode.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_layout\")]\n    pub default_layout: String,\n\n    /// Log retention period in days (0 = forever).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_log_retention_days\")]\n    pub log_retention_days: u64,\n}\n\nimpl Default for DashboardConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            refresh_interval_ms: default_refresh_interval(),\n            max_fps: default_max_fps(),\n            default_layout: default_layout(),\n            log_retention_days: default_log_retention_days(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nfn default_refresh_interval() -> u64 {\n    1000\n}\n\nfn default_max_fps() -> u64 {\n    60\n}\n\nfn default_layout() -> String {\n    \"overview\".to_string()\n}\n\nfn default_log_retention_days() -> u64 {\n    7\n}\n\n/// Theme configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct ThemeConfig {\n    /// Theme name (default, dark, light, cyberpunk).\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub name: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Worker configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct WorkerConfig {\n    /// Maximum concurrent workers.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_workers\")]\n    pub max_workers: u64,\n\n    /// Default model for new workers.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_model\")]\n    pub default_model: String,\n}\n\nimpl Default for WorkerConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            max_workers: default_max_workers(),\n            default_model: default_model(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nfn default_max_workers() -> u64 {\n    10\n}\n\nfn default_model() -> String {\n    \"sonnet\".to_string()\n}\n\n/// Cost tracking configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct CostTrackingConfig {\n    /// Whether cost tracking is enabled.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_cost_enabled\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n\n    /// Budget warning threshold (percentage).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_warning_threshold\")]\n    pub budget_warning_threshold: u8,\n\n    /// Budget critical threshold (percentage).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_critical_threshold\")]\n    pub budget_critical_threshold: u8,\n\n    /// Monthly budget in USD.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub monthly_budget_usd: Option<f64>,\n\n    /// Cost per 1K input tokens for Sonnet (USD).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_sonnet_input_cost\")]\n    pub sonnet_cost_per_1k_input: f64,\n\n    /// Cost per 1K output tokens for Sonnet (USD).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_sonnet_output_cost\")]\n    pub sonnet_cost_per_1k_output: f64,\n}\n\nimpl Default for CostTrackingConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: default_cost_enabled(),\n            budget_warning_threshold: default_warning_threshold(),\n            budget_critical_threshold: default_critical_threshold(),\n            monthly_budget_usd: None,\n            sonnet_cost_per_1k_input: default_sonnet_input_cost(),\n            sonnet_cost_per_1k_output: default_sonnet_output_cost(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Auto-recovery configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct AutoRecoveryConfig {\n    /// Enable/disable auto-recovery.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n\n    /// How often to run recovery checks (in seconds).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_recovery_check_interval\")]\n    pub check_interval_secs: u64,\n\n    /// Policy for dead worker restart: \"disabled\", \"notify\", or \"auto\".\n    #[serde(default = \"default_recovery_policy\")]\n    pub dead_worker_policy: String,\n\n    /// Maximum restart attempts for dead workers.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_restart_attempts\")]\n    pub max_restart_attempts: u8,\n\n    /// Policy for memory leak: \"disabled\", \"notify\", or \"auto\".\n    #[serde(default = \"default_recovery_policy\")]\n    pub memory_leak_policy: String,\n\n    /// Memory threshold in MB before considering it a leak.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_memory_threshold\")]\n    pub memory_threshold_mb: u64,\n\n    /// Policy for stuck tasks: \"disabled\", \"notify\", or \"auto\".\n    #[serde(default = \"default_recovery_policy\")]\n    pub stuck_task_policy: String,\n\n    /// Time in minutes before a task is considered stuck.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_stuck_timeout\")]\n    pub stuck_task_timeout_mins: i64,\n\n    /// Policy for stale assignees: \"disabled\", \"notify\", or \"auto\".\n    #[serde(default = \"default_stale_assignee_policy\")]\n    pub stale_assignee_policy: String,\n\n    /// Time in minutes before an assignee is considered stale.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_stale_timeout\")]\n    pub stale_assignee_timeout_mins: i64,\n}\n\nimpl Default for AutoRecoveryConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: false,\n            check_interval_secs: default_recovery_check_interval(),\n            dead_worker_policy: default_recovery_policy(),\n            max_restart_attempts: default_max_restart_attempts(),\n            memory_leak_policy: Notification configuration for alerts.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct NotificationsConfig {\n    /// Enable terminal bell for critical alerts.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_bell_enabled\")]\n    pub bell_on_critical: bool,\n\n    /// Enable terminal bell for warning alerts.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub bell_on_warning: bool,\n\n    /// Minimum interval between bells (in seconds) to avoid spam.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_bell_interval\")]\n    pub bell_interval_secs: u64,\n\n    /// Enable visual flash for critical alerts.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_visual_flash\")]\n    pub visual_flash_on_critical: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for NotificationsConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            bell_on_critical: default_bell_enabled(),\n            bell_on_warning: false,\n            bell_interval_secs: default_bell_interval(),\n            visual_flash_on_critical: default_visual_flash(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Audit logging configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct AuditConfig {\n    /// Enable audit logging\n    #[serde(default = \"default_audit_enabled\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n\n    /// Retention period in days (0 = forever)\n    #[serde(default = \"default_audit_retention_days\")]\n    pub retention_days: i64,\n\n    /// Apply retention policy on startup\n    #[serde(default = \"default_audit_apply_on_startup\")]\n    pub apply_on_startup: bool,\n\n    /// Export directory for audit logs\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub export_path: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for AuditConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: default_audit_enabled(),\n -> bool {\n    true\n}\n\nfn default_bell_interval() -> u64 {\n    30 // Minimum 30 seconds between bells\n}\n\nfn default_visual_flash() -> bool {\n    true\n}\n\nfn default_recovery_check_interval() -> u64 {\n    30\n}\n\nfn default_recovery_policy() -> String {\n    \"notify\".to_string()\n}\n\nfn default_stale_assignee_policy() -> String {\n    \"auto\".to_string()\n}\n\nfn default_max_restart_attempts() -> u8 {\n    3\n}\n\nfn default_memory_threshold() -> u64 {\n    2048\n}\n\nfn default_stuck_timeout() -> i64 {\n    30\n}\n\nfn default_stale_timeout() -> i64 {\n    60\n}\n\nfn default_cost_enabled() -> bool {\n    true\n}\n\nfn default_warning_threshold() -> u8 {\n    70\n}\n\nfn default_critical_threshold() -> u8 {\n    90\n}\n\nfn default_sonnet_input_cost()  budget_warning_threshold: 80\n  budget_critical_threshold: 95\n  monthly_budget_usd: 100.0\n\"#;\n        let config = ForgeConfig::parse(yaml).expect(\"Failed to parse config\");\n The `App` struct manages overall application state, view switching,\n//! and coordinates between different components.\n//!\n//! # Chat Backend Integration Architecture\n//!\n//! The chat functionality integrates an async `ChatBackend` into the synchronous\n//! TUI event loop using the following design:\n//!\n//! ## Async-to-Sync Bridge\n//!\n//! 1. **Tokio Runtime**: A single-threaded tokio runtime is created during App::new()\n//!    and lives for the duration of the application. This runtime handles all async\n//!    chat operations.\n//!\n//! 2. **Message Passing**: An `mpsc` channel connects async chat tasks to the sync UI:\n//!    - `chat_tx`: Sender held by App, used to submit chat requests\n//!    - `chat_rx`: Receiver held by a background task, processes requests\n//!    - `response_rx`: Receiver held by App, receives responses\n//!\n//! 3. **Non-Blocking Execution**: When user submits chat input:\n//!    - Send request to background task via `chat_tx`\n//!    - Set `chat_pending` flag and display loading indicator\n//!    - Continue UI event loop without blocking\n//!    - Poll `response_rx` each frame to check for responses\n//!\n//! ## State Management\n//!\n//! - `chat_pending: bool` - Whether a request is in flight\n//! - `chat_history: Vec<ChatExchange>` - Last 10 exchanges (user query + response)\n//! - `chat_backend: Option<ChatBackend>` - Initialized from config.yaml\n//!\n//! ## Error Handling\n//!\n//! - Backend initialization failures: App runs without chat (display error in UI)\n//! - Request timeout: Background task implements 30s timeout\n//! - API errors: Captured in response, displayed in chat history as error messages\n//!\n//! ## Performance\n//!\n//! - Background task prevents UI blocking\n//! - Response polling adds <1ms per frame\n//! - Chat history limited to 10 exchanges (~1KB memory)\n\nuse std::io;\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::sync::mpsc::{self, Receiver, Sender};\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n\nuse crossterm::event::{self, Event, KeyEvent};\nuse forge_config::ForgeConfig;\nuse forge_chat::{ChatBackend, ChatConfig, ChatResponse, StreamingChatChunk};\nuse forge_core::types::WorkerTier;\nuse forge_worker::{\n    LaunchConfig, SpawnRequest, WorkerLauncher,\n    discovery::DiscoveredWorker,\n};\nuse ratatui::{\n    Frame, Terminal,\n    backend::CrosstermBackend,\n    layout::{Constraint, Direction, Layout, Rect},\n    style::{Color, for chat exchange data during streaming display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\nstruct PendingChatExchange {\n    query: String,\n    response_text: String,\n    timestamp: String,\n    tool_calls: pub side_effects: Vec<SideEffectInfo>,\n    /// Confirmation prompt if action requires approval\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub confirmation: #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub error_guidance: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Information about a tool call for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct ToolCallInfo {\n    /// Tool name (e.g., \"spawn_worker\", \"get_worker_status\")\n    pub name: String,\n    /// Whether the tool execution succeeded\n    pub success: bool,\n    /// Human-readable result message\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\n/// Information about a side effect for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize, description: String,\n}\n\n/// Confirmation prompt information for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct ConfirmationInfo {\n    /// Title of the confirmation\n    pub title: String,\n    /// Description of what will happen\n    pub description: String,\n    /// Warning level (info, warning, danger)\n    pub level: ConfirmationLevel,\n    /// Estimated cost impact\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub cost_impact: Option<f64>,\n    /// Items that will be affected\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub affected_items: Vec<String>,\n    /// Whether this action is reversible\n    pub reversible: bool,\n}\n\n/// Warning level for confirmation prompts.\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub enum ConfirmationLevel {\n    /// Informational (no danger)\n    Info,\n    /// Warning (some risk)\n    Warning,\n    /// Danger (high risk, destructive)\n    Danger,\n}\n\n/// Response metadata for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct ResponseMetadata {\n    /// Duration in milliseconds\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub duration_ms: u64,\n    /// Estimated cost in USD\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub cost_usd: Option<f64>,\n    /// Provider name\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub provider: String,\n}\n\n/// Generate actionable guidance for common errors.\nfn get_error_guidance(error_message: &str) -> Option<String> {\n    let error_lower = error_message.to_lowercase();\n\n    // Config not found errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"config\") && (error_lower.contains(\"not found\") || error_lower.contains(\"missing\")) {\n        return Some(\"Run 'forge init' to create a configuration file, or check that ~/.forge/config.yaml exists.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // API/authentication errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"api key\") || error_lower.contains(\"authentication\") || error_lower.contains(\"unauthorized\") {\n        return Some(\"Check your API key in ~/.forge/config.yaml. Ensure the key is valid and not expired.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Rate limit errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"rate limit\") || error_lower.contains(\"too many requests\") {\n        return Some(\"Wait a moment before sending more commands. Rate limits reset automatically.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Network unreachable (no internet)\n    if error_lower.contains(\"network unreachable\") || error_lower.contains(\"no route to host\") {\n        return Some(\"⚠️  Network unreachable. Check your internet connection and try again when online. Last known state is cached.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // DNS resolution failures\n    if error_lower.contains(\"dns\") || error_lower.contains(\"failed to lookup address\") || error_lower.contains(\"name or service not known\") {\n        return Some(\"⚠️  DNS resolution failed. Check DNS settings or try using 8.8.8.8 as your DNS server. Click 'r' to retry.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Connection timeout\n    if error_lower.contains(\"timeout\") {\n        return Some(\"⏱️  Request timed out. The server may be slow or unreachable. Click 'r' to retry or check status.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Generic connection/network errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"connection\") || error_lower.contains(\"network\") {\n        return Some(\"⚠️  Connection failed. Check your internet connection. The API server may be temporarily unavailable. Click 'r' to retry.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Provider errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"provider\") && error_lower.contains(\"not found\") {\n        return Some(\"Check that the provider is installed and available in your PATH. Run 'forge --help' for setup instructions.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Tool execution errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"tool\") && error_lower.contains(\"failed\") {\n        return Some(\"The requested operation could not be completed. Check the tool output above for details.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Worker errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"worker\") && (error_lower.contains(\"spawn\") || error_lower.contains(\"launch\")) {\n        return Some(\"Ensure you have sufficient system resources and the worker binary is available.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Context gathering errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"context\") && error_lower.contains(\"failed\") {\n        return Some(\"The dashboard context could not be loaded. Try restarting the application.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Permission/access errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"permission\") || error_lower.contains(\"access denied\") || error_lower.contains(\"forbidden\") {\n        return Some(\"You don't have permission for this action. Check your configuration and credentials.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Resource not found errors (general)\n    if error_lower.contains(\"not found\") || error_lower.contains(\"does not exist\") {\n        return Some(\"The requested resource was not found. Verify the name and try again.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // JSON parsing errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"json\") || error_lower.contains(\"parse\") || error_lower.contains(\"deserialize\") {\n        return Some(\"There was a problem processing the response. The server may have returned unexpected data.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // IO errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"io error\") || error_lower.contains(\"file\") && error_lower.contains(\"error\") {\n        return Some(\"A file system error occurred. Check file permissions and available disk space.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    None\n}\n\n/// Result of an update operation.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub enum UpdateResult {\n    /// Update completed successfully with version info\n    Success {\n        /// Previous version\n        old_version: String,\n        /// New version\n        new_version: String,\n    },\n    /// Update failed with an error message\n    Failed(String),\n    /// Already up to date\n    AlreadyUpToDate,\n}\n\n/// Progress information for update downloads.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default)]\npub struct UpdateProgress {\n    /// Current status message\n    pub status: String,\n    /// Download progress percentage (0-100)\n    pub percent: u8,\n    /// Whether the update is in progress\n    pub in_progress: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for App {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl App {\n    /// Create a new app instance with default state.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n      // Time ThemeManager initialization\n        let theme_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Loading ThemeManager...\");\n        let theme_manager = ThemeManager::load_config();\n        info!(\"⏱️ ThemeManager loaded in {:?}\", theme_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Initialize worker launcher and runtime (needed before chat backend for worker spawning)\n        let worker_launcher = WorkerLauncher::new();\n        let worker_runtime = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"Failed to create worker runtime\");\n\n        // Time chat backend initialization\n        let chat_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Initializing chat backend...\");\n        let chat_backend = config watcher for hot-reload (skip in test mode to save inotify instances)\n        let config_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Initializing config watcher...\");\n        let      match ConfigWatcher::new() {\n                Some((watcher, rx)) => {\n                    let config = watcher.current_config().clone();\n                    info!(\"⏱️ Config watcher initialized in {:?}\", config_start.elapsed());\n                    (Some(watcher), Some(rx), config)\n                }\n                None => {\n                    info!(\"⏱️ Config watcher not initialized (config file not found)\");\n                    (None, None, ForgeConfig::default())\n                }\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Calculate initial data poll interval from config\n        let data_poll_interval = Duration::from_millis(\n            forge_config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms.min(DEFAULT_DATA_POLL_INTERVAL_MS)\n        );\n\n        // Configure alert notifier from config\n        let mut data_manager = data_manager;\n        data_manager.configure_notifier(\n            forge_config.notifications.bell_on_critical,\n            forge_config.notifications.bell_on_warning,\n            forge_config.notifications.bell_interval_secs,\n            forge_config.notifications.visual_flash_on_critical,\n        );\n\n        // Initialize history manager and load previous history\n        let history_manager = forge_chat::HistoryManager::new().ok();\n\n        // Initialize workspace registry\n        let ws_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Initializing workspace registry...\");\n        let (workspace_registry, current_workspace_id) = Self::init_workspace_registry(&forge_config);\n        info!(\"⏱️ Workspace registry initialized in {:?}\", ws_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Initialize workspace panel data\n        let workspace_panel_data = WorkspacePanelData::new();\n\n        // Initialize audit logger\n        let audit_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Initializing audit logger...\");\n        let audit_logger = Self::init_audit_logger();\n\n        // Connect audit logger to BeadManager for bead status change tracking\n        if let Some(ref logger) = audit_logger {\n            data_manager.set_bead_audit_logger({\n                let logger = logger.clone();\n                move |event| {\n                    if let Err(e) = logger.log(event) {\n                        tracing::warn!(\"Failed to log audit event from BeadManager: {}\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n            });\n            info!(\"Connected audit logger to BeadManager\");\n        }\n\n        info!(\"⏱️ Audit logger initialized in {:?}\", audit_start.elapsed());\n\n        info!(\"⏱️ App::new() completed in {:?}\", start.elapsed());\n\n        Self {\n            current_view: View::default(),\n            previous_view: None,\n            focus_panel: FocusPanel::default(),\n            input_handler: InputHandler::new(),\n            should_quit: false,\n            show_help: false,\n            chat_input: String::new(),\n            status_message: None,\n            scroll_offset: 0,\n            data_manager,\n            theme_manager,\n            dirty: true,\n            cached_size: None,\n            last_poll_time: now,\n            cached_timestamp: None,\n            last_timestamp_update: now,\n            cached_layout_mode: None,\n            last_terminal_width: 0,\n            update_available: false,\n            update_in_progress: false,\n            update_progress: UpdateProgress::default(),\n            update_result_rx: None,\n            update_ready_for_restart: false,\n            last_update_check: now,\n            chat_backend,\n            chat_response_tx: None,\n            chat_response_rx: None,\n            chat_pending: false,\n            chat_spinner_frame: 0,\n            streaming_response: String::new(),\n            streaming_position: 0,\n            streaming_active: false,\n            pending_complete_response: None,\n            streaming_chunk_rx: None,\n            real_streaming_active: false,\n            streaming_query: None,\n            chat_history: Vec::new(),\n            chat_scroll_offset: 0,\n            worker_launcher,\n            worker_runtime,\n            show_kill_dialog: false,\n            kill_dialog_workers: Vec::new(),\n            kill_dialog_selected: 0,\n            kill_dialog_error: None,\n            priority_filter: None,\n            task_search_query: String::new(),\n            task_search_mode: false,\n            pending_chat_exchange: None,\n            show_task_detail: false,\n            selected_task_index: 0,\n            selected_alert_index: 0,\n            show_alert_detail: false,\n            show_workspace_detail: false,\n            _config_watcher: config_watcher,\n            config_rx,\n            forge_config,\n            data_poll_interval,\n            show_confirmation: false,\n            pending_action: None,\n            paused_workers: std::collections::HashSet::new(),\n            selected_worker_index: 0,\n            error_recovery: SharedErrorRecoveryManager::new(),\n            last_failed_query: None,\n            retry_available: false,\n            network_available: true,\n            network_error_message: None,\n            network_unavailable_since: None,\n            history_manager,\n            show_config_menu: false,\n            config_menu_type: None,\n            config_menu_selected: 0,\n            config_menu_editing: false,\n            config_menu_input: String::new(),\n            workspace_registry,\n            workspace_panel_data,\n            current_workspace_id,\n            last_workspace_poll_time: now,\n            audit_logger,\n            audit_filter: AuditFilter::new(),\n            audit_scroll_offset: 0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new app with a custom status directory (for testing).\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub fn with_status_dir(status_dir: std::path::PathBuf) -> Self {\n        let now = Instant::now();\n        Self {\n            current_view: View::default(),\n            previous_view: None,\n            focus_panel: FocusPanel::default(),\n            input_handler: InputHandler::new(),\n            should_quit: false,\n            show_help: false,\n            chat_input: String::new(),\n            status_message: None,\n            scroll_offset: 0,\n            data_manager: DataManager::with_status_dir(status_dir),\n            theme_manager: ThemeManager::new(),\n            dirty: true,\n            cached_size: None,\n            last_poll_time: now,\n            cached_timestamp: None,\n            last_timestamp_update: now,\n            cached_layout_mode: None,\n            last_terminal_width: 0,\n            update_available: false,\n            update_in_progress: false,\n            update_progress: UpdateProgress::default(),\n            update_result_rx: None,\n            update_ready_for_restart: false,\n            last_update_check: now,\n            chat_backend: None, // Don't initialize in test mode\n            chat_response_tx: None,\n            chat_response_rx: None,\n            chat_pending: false,\n            streaming_response: String::new(),\n            streaming_position: 0,\n            streaming_active: false,\n            pending_complete_response: None,\n            streaming_chunk_rx: None,\n            real_streaming_active: false,\n            streaming_query: None,\n            chat_history: Vec::new(),\n            chat_scroll_offset: 0,\n            worker_launcher: WorkerLauncher::new(),\n            worker_runtime: tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n                .enable_all()\n                .build()\n                .expect(\"Failed to create worker runtime\"),\n            show_kill_dialog: false,\n            kill_dialog_workers: Vec::new(),\n            kill_dialog_selected: 0,\n            kill_dialog_error: None,\n            priority_filter: None,\n            pending_chat_exchange: None,\n            show_task_detail: false,\n            selected_task_index: 0,\n            selected_alert_index: 0,\n            show_alert_detail: false,\n            show_workspace_detail: false,\n            _config_watcher: None, // Don't initialize in test mode\n            config_rx: None,\n            forge_config: ForgeConfig::default(),\n            data_poll_interval: Duration::from_millis(DEFAULT_DATA_POLL_INTERVAL_MS),\n            chat_spinner_frame: 0,\n            show_confirmation: false,\n            pending_action: None,\n            task_search_query: String::new(),\n            task_search_mode: false,\n            paused_workers: retry_available: false,\n            network_available: true,\n            network_error_message: None,\n            network_unavailable_since: None,\n            history_manager: None, // Don't initialize in test mode\n            show_config_menu: false,\n            config_menu_type: None,\n            config_menu_selected: 0,\n            config_menu_editing: false,\n            config_menu_input: String::new(),\n            workspace_registry: WorkspaceRegistry::new(),\n            workspace_panel_data: WorkspacePanelData::new(),\n            current_workspace_id: None,\n            last_workspace_poll_time: now,\n            audit_logger: None, // Don't initialize in test mode\n            audit_filter: AuditFilter::new(),\n            audit_scroll_offset: 0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Initialize chat backend from config.yaml with worker spawner support.\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if config is missing or initialization fails.\n    /// Errors are logged but don't prevent app startup.\n    fn init_chat_backend_with_worker_spawner(\n        _worker_runtime: &tokio::runtime::Runtime,\n        _worker_launcher: &WorkerLauncher,\n    ) -> Option<ChatBackend> {\n        use forge_chat::config::{AuditConfig, ConfirmationConfig, RateLimitConfig};\n        use forge_chat::spawner::RealWorkerSpawner;\n        use forge_chat::ChatProviderFactory;\n        use tracing::{error, info, warn};\n\n        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ init_chat_backend_with_worker_spawner() started\");\n\n        // Load config from ~/.forge/config.yaml\n        let config_path = match dirs::home_dir() {\n            Some(home) => home.join(\".forge/config.yaml\"),\n            None => {\n                warn!(\"⏱️ Could not determine home directory (took {:?})\", start.elapsed());\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        info!(\n            \"⏱️ Initializing chat backend from {}\",\n            config_path.display()\n        );\n\n        if !config_path.exists() {\n            warn!(\n                \"⏱️ Chat config not found at {} (took {:?})\",\n                config_path.display(),\n                start.elapsed()\n            );\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let read_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        let config_str = match std::fs::read_to_string(&config_path) {\n            Ok(s) => s,\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\n                    \"⏱️ Failed to read chat config: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n        info!(\"⏱️ Config file read in {:?}\", read_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Parse the full config YAML\n        let parse_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        let yaml: serde_yaml::Value = match serde_yaml::from_str(&config_str) {\n            Ok(v) => v,\n            Err(e) => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"⏱️ Failed to parse config YAML: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n        info!(\"⏱️ YAML parsed in {:?}\", parse_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Extract chat_backend section\n        let chat_backend = yaml.get(\"chat_backend\")?;\n        let command = chat_backend.get(\"command\")?.as_str()?;\n\n        // Read optional args (not required — providers may handle args internally)\n        let args: Vec<String> = chat_backend\n            .get(\"args\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_sequence())\n            .map(|seq| {\n                seq.iter()\n                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n                    .collect()\n            })\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        // Read model (optional, defaults to \"sonnet\")\n        let model = chat_backend\n            .get(\"model\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or(\"sonnet\")\n            .to_string();\n\n        // Determine provider: explicit `provider` field takes precedence; fall back to\n        // inferring from `command` for backwards compatibility (opencode → opencode,\n        // anything else → claude-cli).\n        let provider_name = chat_backend\n            .get(\"provider\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                if command == \"opencode\" {\n                    \"opencode\"\n                } else {\n                    \"claude-cli\"\n                }\n            });\n\n        info!(\"⏱️ Using provider: {}\", provider_name);\n\n        // Warn about command/provider field mismatches before delegating to factory.\n        match provider_name {\n            \"opencode\" if command != \"opencode\" => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"Provider is 'opencode' but command is '{}' — using command as binary path\",\n                    command\n                );\n            }\n            \"claude-cli\" if command == \"opencode\" => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"Provider is 'claude-cli' but command is 'opencode' — this may not work as \\\n                     expected. Set provider: opencode to use the OpenCode provider.\"\n                );\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n\n        // Read model_aliases (only consumed by the opencode provider).\n        let model_aliases = chat_backend\n            .get(\"model_aliases\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_mapping())\n            .map(|m| {\n                m.iter()\n                    .filter_map(|(k, v)| {\n                        let key = k.as_str()?.to_string();\n                        let val = v.as_str()?.to_string();\n                        Some((key, val))\n                    })\n                    .collect::<std::collections::HashMap<String, String>>()\n            });\n\n        // Use the factory to build the ProviderConfig from the provider name string.\n        let provider_config = match ChatProviderFactory::create_config(\n            provider_name,\n            command,\n            &model,\n            args,\n            model_aliases,\n        ) {\n            Ok(config) => config,\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\"Failed to create provider config: {}\", e);\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        let chat_config = ChatConfig {\n            provider: provider_config,\n            rate_limit: RateLimitConfig::default(),\n            audit: AuditConfig::default(),\n            confirmations: ConfirmationConfig::default(),\n        };\n\n        // Get workspace path\n        let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"/home/coder\".to_string());\n        let workspace = std::env::var(\"FORGE_WORKSPACE\")\n            .map(std::path::PathBuf::from)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|_| std::path::PathBuf::from(&home).join(\"forge\"));\n\n        // Create RealWorkerSpawner with default launcher detection\n        let worker_spawner: Arc<dyn forge_chat::spawner::WorkerSpawner> = match RealWorkerSpawner::with_default_launcher(workspace) {\n            Some(spawner) => Arc::new(spawner),\n            None => {\n                warn!(\"⏱️ No worker launcher script found, worker spawning via chat will not be available\");\n                Arc::new(forge_chat::spawner::NoOpWorkerSpawner)\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Initialize backend (async, but we block here during startup)\n        let runtime_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        match tokio::runtime::Runtime::new() {\n            Ok(rt) => {\n                info!(\n                    \"⏱️ Created tokio runtime for chat backend in {:?}\",\n                    runtime_start.elapsed()\n                );\n                let backend_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                match rt.block_on(ChatBackend::new(chat_config)) {\n                    Ok(backend) => {\n                        let backend = backend.with_worker_spawner(worker_spawner);\n                        info!(\n                            \"⏱️ ✅ Chat backend initialized successfully in {:?} (total: {:?})\",\n                            backend_start.elapsed(),\n                            start.elapsed()\n                        );\n                        Some(backend)\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        error!(\n                            \"⏱️ ❌ Failed to initialize chat backend: {} (took {:?})\",\n                            e,\n                            start.elapsed()\n                        );\n                        None\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\n                    \"⏱️ ❌ Failed to create tokio runtime: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                None\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Initialize workspace registry from config.\n    ///\n    /// Loads workspaces from the config file if multi-workspace mode is enabled.\n    /// Returns the registry and the current workspace ID.\n    fn init_workspace_registry(config: &ForgeConfig) -> (WorkspaceRegistry, Option<String>) {\n        use forge_core::WorkspaceConfig;\n        use std::path::PathBuf;\n\n        // If multi-workspace is not enabled, return empty registry with current directory\n        if !config.workspaces.enabled {\n            // Add current directory as a workspace\n            let current_dir = std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from(\"/home/coding\"));\n            let mut registry = WorkspaceRegistry::new();\n\n            // Try to detect if current directory is a workspace\n            if let Some(dir_name) = current_dir.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {\n                let ws = WorkspaceConfig::new(\n                    dir_name.to_string(),\n                    dir_name.to_string(),\n                    current_dir.clone()\n                );\n                registry.add(ws);\n                tracing::info!(\"Initialized workspace registry with current directory: {:?}\", current_dir);\n                return (registry, Some(dir_name.to_string()));\n            }\n\n            return (registry, None);\n        }\n\n        // Multi-workspace mode: load from config or registry file\n        let mut registry = WorkspaceRegistry::new();\n\n        // First, load from workspace_paths in config\n        for (id, path_str) in &config.workspaces.workspace_paths {\n            let path = PathBuf::from(path_str);\n            if path.exists() {\n                let ws = WorkspaceConfig::new(\n                    id.clone(),\n                    id.clone(),\n                    path\n                );\n                registry.add(ws);\n                tracing::info!(\"Added workspace from config: {} -> {:?}\", id, path_str);\n            } else {\n                tracing::warn!(\"Workspace path does not exist: {} -> {:?}\", id, path_str);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Try to load from registry file if specified\n        if let Some(ref registry_path) = config.workspaces.registry_path {\n            let path = PathBuf::from(registry_path);\n            if path.exists() {\n                match WorkspaceRegistry::load_from_file(&path) {\n                    Ok(loaded_registry) => {\n                        tracing::info!(\"Loaded workspace registry from {:?}\", path);\n                        registry = loaded_registry;\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        tracing::warn!(\"Failed to load workspace registry from {:?}: {}\", path, e);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                tracing::debug!(\"Workspace registry file not found: {:?}\", path);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Get current workspace (first enabled by priority)\n        let current_id = registry.current().map(|ws| ws.id.clone());\n\n        tracing::info!(\n            \"Workspace registry initialized with {} workspaces, current: {:?}\",\n            registry.len(),\n            current_id\n        );\n\n        (registry, current_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Initialize the audit logger from ~/.forge/audit.db.\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if initialization fails (audit logging is optional).\n    fn init_audit_logger() -> Option<AuditLogger> {\n        use std::path::PathBuf;\n\n        let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"/home/coder\".to_string());\n        let audit_db_path = PathBuf::from(home).join(\".forge/audit.db\");\n\n        match AuditLogger::open_with_retention(\n            &audit_db_path,\n            RetentionPolicy::new(90, true)\n        ) {\n            Ok(logger) => {\n                info!(\"Audit logger initialized at {}\", audit_db_path.display());\n                Some(logger)\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                warn!(\"Failed to initialize audit logger: {}. Audit logging disabled.\", e);\n                None\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Log an audit event if the audit logger is available.\n    fn log_audit(&self, event: AuditEvent) {\n        if let Some(ref logger) = self.audit_logger {\n            if let Err(e) = logger.log(event) {\n                warn!(\"Failed to log audit event: {}\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Export audit logs to JSON file.\n    fn export_audit_json(&mut self) {\n        use forge_core::audit::AuditLogger;\n\n        if let Some(ref logger) = self.audit_logger {\n            let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"/home/coder\".to_string());\n            let export_dir = std::path::PathBuf::from(home).join(\".forge/exports\");\n\n            // Create export directory if it doesn't exist\n            if let Err(e) = std::fs::create_dir_all(&export_dir) {\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Failed to create export directory: {}\", e));\n                self.mark_dirty();\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Generate filename with timestamp\n            let timestamp = chrono::Local::now().format(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\");\n            let export_path = export_dir.join(format!(\"audit_{}.json\", timestamp));\n\n            match logger.export_json(&self.audit_filter, &export_path) {\n                Ok(()) => {\n                    self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Exported audit log to {}\", export_path.display()));\n                    // Log the export action itself\n                    self.log_audit(AuditEvent::new(\n                        forge_core::audit::EventType::UserAction,\n                        \"user\",\n                        \"audit\",\n                        \"export_json\"\n                    ).with_metadata(format!(\"path: {}\", export_path.display()))\n                      .with_severity(forge_core::audit::Severity::Info));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Export failed: {}\", e));\n                }\n            }\n            self.mark_dirty();\n        } else {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"Audit logger not available\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Export audit logs to CSV file.\n    fn export_audit_csv(&mut self) {\n        use forge_core::audit::AuditLogger;\n\n        if let Some(ref logger) = self.audit_logger {\n            let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"/home/coder\".to_string());\n            let export_dir = std::path::PathBuf::from(home).join(\".forge/exports\");\n\n            // Create export directory if it doesn't exist\n            if let Err(e) = std::fs::create_dir_all(&export_dir) {\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Failed to create export directory: {}\", e));\n                self.mark_dirty();\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Generate filename with timestamp\n            let timestamp = chrono::Local::now().format(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\");\n            let export_path = export_dir.join(format!(\"audit_{}.csv\", timestamp));\n\n            match logger.export_csv(&self.audit_filter, &export_path) {\n                Ok(()) => {\n                    self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Exported audit log to {}\", export_path.display()));\n                    // Log the export action itself\n                    self.log_audit(AuditEvent::new(\n                        forge_core::audit::EventType::UserAction,\n                        \"user\",\n                        \"audit\",\n                        \"export_csv\"\n                    ).with_metadata(format!(\"path: {}\", export_path.display()))\n                      .with_severity(forge_core::audit::Severity::Info));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Export failed: {}\", e));\n                }\n            }\n            self.mark_dirty();\n        } else {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"Audit logger not available\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Show audit filter overlay.\n    fn show_audit_filter(&mut self) {\n        // For now, cycle through predefined filters\n        // In a full implementation, this would show a filter selection UI\n        let current_filter = &self.audit_filter;\n\n        // Cycle through filter modes: none -> worker -> error -> config -> none\n        if current_filter.event_types.is_none() && current_filter.entity_type.is_none() && current_filter.severity.is_none() {\n            // No filter active, show worker events\n            self.audit_filter = forge_core::audit::AuditFilter::new()\n                .with_entity(\"worker\", \"\");\n            self.status_message = Some(\"Filter: Worker events\".to_string());\n        } else if current_filter.entity_type.as_deref() == Some(\"worker\") {\n            // Currently showing workers, switch to errors\n            self.audit_filter = forge_core::audit::AuditFilter::new()\n                .with_severity(forge_core::audit::Severity::Error);\n            self.status_message = Some(\"Filter: Error events\".to_string());\n        } else if current_filter.severity.is_some() {\n            // Currently showing errors, switch to config changes\n            self.audit_filter = forge_core::audit::AuditFilter::new()\n                .with_event_types(vec![forge_core::audit::EventType::ConfigChange]);\n            self.status_message = Some(\"Filter: Config changes\".to_string());\n        } else {\n            // Reset filter\n            self.audit_filter = forge_core::audit::AuditFilter::new();\n            self.status_message = Some(\"Filter: All events\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        self.audit_scroll_offset = 0;\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n    /// Reset audit filter to show all events.\n    fn reset_audit_filter(&mut self) {\n        self.audit_filter = forge_core::audit::AuditFilter::new();\n        self.audit_scroll_offset = 0;\n        self.status_message = Some(\"Filter reset: All events\".to_string());\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n    /// Show audit statistics.\n    fn show_audit_stats(&mut self) {\n        use forge_core::audit::AuditLogger;\n\n        if let Some(ref logger) = self.audit_logger {\n            match logger.stats() {\n                Ok(stats) => {\n                    let msg = format!(\n                        \"Audit Stats: {} events | Oldest: {} | Newest: {} | By type: {:?}\",\n                        stats.total_events,\n                        stats.oldest_timestamp\n                            .map(|t| t.format(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M\").to_string())\n                            .unwrap_or_else(|| \"N/A\".to_string()),\n                        stats.newest_timestamp\n                            .map(|t| t.format(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M\").to_string())\n                            .unwrap_or_else(|| \"N/A\".to_string()),\n                        stats.event_type_counts\n                    );\n                    // Truncate if too long\n                    let truncated = if msg.len() > 150 {\n                        format!(\"{}...\", &msg[..147])\n                    } else {\n                        msg\n                    };\n                    self.status_message = Some(truncated);\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Failed to get stats: {}\", e));\n                }\n            }\n            self.mark_dirty();\n        } else {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"Audit logger not available\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the current view.\n    pub fn current_view(&self) -> View {\n        self.current_view\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the current focus panel.\n    pub fn focus_panel(&self) -> FocusPanel {\n        self.focus_panel\n    }\n\n    /// Returns whether the app should quit.\n    pub fn should_quit(&self) -> bool {\n        self.should_quit\n    }\n\n    /// Returns whether help overlay is visible.\n    pub fn show_help(&self) -> bool {\n        self.show_help\n    }\n\n    /// Returns whether kill dialog is visible.\n    pub fn show_kill_dialog(&self) -> bool {\n        self.show_kill_dialog\n    }\n\n    /// Mark the UI as dirty (needs redraw).\n    fn mark_dirty(&mut self) {\n        self.dirty = true;\n    }\n\n    /// Handle local chat commands (commands processed by TUI, not sent to backend).\n    ///\n    /// Returns true if the command was handled locally, false if it should be sent to backend.\n    fn handle_chat_command(&mut self, query: &str) -> bool {\n        let trimmed = query.trim();\n\n        // Check for slash commands\n        if !trimmed.starts_with('/') {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Parse command and arguments\n        let parts: Vec<&str> = trimmed[1..].splitn(2, ' ').collect();\n        let command = parts[0].to_lowercase();\n        let args = parts.get(1).map(|s| s.trim());\n\n        match command.as_str() {\n            \"save\" => {\n                self.save_chat_history();\n                true\n            }\n            \"load\" => {\n                self.load_chat_history();\n                true\n            }\n            \"export\" => {\n                let path = args.map(std::path::PathBuf::from);\n                self.export_chat_history(path);\n                true\n            }\n            \"clear\" => {\n                self.chat_history.clear();\n                self.chat_scroll_offset = 0;\n                self.status_message = Some(\"Chat history cleared\".to_string());\n                info!(\"Chat history cleared by user command\");\n                true\n            }\n            \"history\" => {\n                // Show history info\n                let count = self.chat_history.len();\n                let msg = if count == 0 {\n                    \"No chat history. Use /load to restore previous sessions.\".to_string()\n                } else {\n                    format!(\"Chat history: {} exchanges in current session\", count)\n                };\n                self.status_message = Some(msg);\n                true\n            }\n            \"stats\" => {\n                self.show_history_stats();\n                true\n            }\n            \"help\" => {\n                // Show available commands\n                let help_text = \"Commands: /save /load /export [path] /clear /history /stats /help\";\n                self.chat_history.push(ChatExchange {\n                    user_query: \"/help\".to_string(),\n                    assistant_response: help_text.to_string(),\n                    timestamp: chrono::Local::now().format(\"%H:%M:%S\").to_string(),\n                    is_error: false,\n                    tool_calls: vec![],\n                    side_effects: vec![],\n                    confirmation: None,\n                    metadata: ResponseMetadata::default(),\n                    error_guidance: None,\n                });\n                // Limit history to 10 exchanges\n                if self.chat_history.len() > 10 {\n                    self.chat_history.remove(0);\n                }\n                true\n            }\n            _ => {\n                // Unknown slash command - let it go to the backend\n                false\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Save current chat history to disk.\n    fn save_chat_history(&mut self) {\n        let history_manager = match &self.history_manager {\n            Some(hm) => hm,\n            None => {\n                self.status_message = Some(\"History manager not initialized\".to_string());\n                return;\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Convert ChatExchange to HistoryEntry\n        let entries: Vec<forge_chat::HistoryEntry> = self\n            .chat_history\n            .iter()\n            .map(|exchange| forge_chat::HistoryEntry {\n                timestamp: exchange.timestamp.clone(),\n                user_query: exchange.user_query.clone(),\n                assistant_response: exchange.assistant_response.clone(),\n                is_error: exchange.is_error,\n                metadata: Some(forge_chat::HistoryMetadata {\n                    duration_ms: exchange.metadata.duration_ms,\n                    cost_usd: exchange.metadata.cost_usd,\n                    provider: exchange.metadata.provider.clone(),\n                }),\n                tool_calls: exchange\n                    .tool_calls\n                    .iter()\n                    .map(|tc| forge_chat::HistoryToolCall {\n                        name: tc.name.clone(),\n                        success: tc.success,\n                        message: Some(tc.message.clone()),\n                    })\n                    .collect(),\n                session_id: None,\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        // Save in background (avoid blocking UI)\n        let history_path = history_manager.history_path().clone();\n        let session_id = history_manager.session_id().to_string();\n        std::thread::spawn(move || {\n            use std::io::Write;\n            match std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n                .create(true)\n                .append(true)\n                .open(&history_path)\n            {\n                Ok(mut file) => {\n                    let mut saved = 0;\n                    for mut entry in entries {\n                        entry.session_id = Some(session_id.clone());\n                        if let Ok(json) = serde_json::to_string(&entry) {\n                            if writeln!(file, \"{}\", json).is_ok() {\n                                saved += 1;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    info!(\"Saved {} entries to history\", saved);\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    error!(\"Failed to save history: {}\", e);\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        self.status_message = Some(format!(\n            \"Saved {} exchanges to history\",\n            self.chat_history.len()\n        ));\n    }\n\n    /// Load chat history from disk.\n    fn load_chat_history(&mut self) {\n        let history_manager = match &self.history_manager {\n            Some(hm) => hm,\n            None => {\n                self.status_message = Some(\"History manager not initialized\".to_string());\n                return;\n            }\n        };\n\n        let history_path = history_manager.history_path().clone();\n\n        // Load history synchronously (quick operation)\n        if !history_path.exists() {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"No saved history found\".to_string());\n            return;\n        }\n\n        match std::fs::File::open(&history_path) {\n            Ok(file) => {\n                use std::io::{BufRead, BufReader};\n                let reader = BufReader::new(file);\n                let mut loaded = 0;\n\n                // Load last 10 entries\n                let mut entries: Vec<forge_chat::HistoryEntry> = Vec::new();\n                for line in reader.lines().map_while(Result::ok) {\n                    if line.trim().is_empty() {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    if let Ok(entry) = serde_json::from_str::<forge_chat::HistoryEntry>(&line) {\n                        entries.push(entry);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Take last 10 entries\n                let start = entries.len().saturating_sub(10);\n                for entry in &entries[start..] {\n                    self.chat_history.push(ChatExchange {\n                        user_query: entry.user_query.clone(),\n                        assistant_response: entry.assistant_response.clone(),\n                        timestamp: entry.timestamp.clone(),\n                        is_error: entry.is_error,\n                        tool_calls: entry\n                            .tool_calls\n                            .iter()\n                            .map(|tc| ToolCallInfo {\n                                name: tc.name.clone(),\n                                success: tc.success,\n                                message: tc.message.clone().unwrap_or_default(),\n                            })\n                            .collect(),\n                        side_effects: vec![],\n                        confirmation: None,\n                        metadata: entry.metadata.as_ref().map(|m| ResponseMetadata {\n                            duration_ms: m.duration_ms,\n                            cost_usd: m.cost_usd,\n                            provider: m.provider.clone(),\n                        }).unwrap_or_default(),\n                        error_guidance: None,\n                    });\n                    loaded += 1;\n                }\n\n                // Trim to 10 entries if we loaded more than we had room for\n                while self.chat_history.len() > 10 {\n                    self.chat_history.remove(0);\n                }\n\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Loaded {} exchanges from history\", loaded));\n                info!(\"Loaded {} entries from history\", loaded);\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Failed to load history: {}\", e));\n                error!(\"Failed to load history: {}\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Export chat history to a file.\n    fn export_chat_history(&mut self, path: Option<std::path::PathBuf>) {\n        let history_manager = match &self.history_manager {\n            Some(hm) => hm,\n            None => {\n                self.status_message = Some(\"History manager not initialized\".to_string());\n                return;\n            }\n        };\n\n        let export_path = path.unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            dirs::home_dir()\n                .unwrap_or_else(|| std::path::PathBuf::from(\".\"))\n                .join(\"chat-export.json\")\n        });\n\n        let history_path = history_manager.history_path().clone();\n\n        // Load and export in background\n        let export_path_clone = export_path.clone();\n        std::thread::spawn(move || {\n            // Load all entries\n            let entries: Vec<forge_chat::HistoryEntry> = if history_path.exists() {\n                use std::io::{BufRead, BufReader};\n                match std::fs::File::open(&history_path) {\n                    Ok(file) => {\n                        let reader = BufReader::new(file);\n                        reader\n                            .lines()\n                            .map_while(std::result::Result::ok)\n                            .filter(|line| !line.trim().is_empty())\n                            .filter_map(|line| serde_json::from_str(&line).ok())\n                            .collect()\n                    }\n                    Err(_) => Vec::new(),\n                }\n            } else {\n                Vec::new()\n            };\n\n            // Export as pretty JSON\n            match serde_json::to_string_pretty(&entries) {\n                Ok(json) => {\n                    if let Err(e) = std::fs::write(&export_path_clone, json) {\n                        error!(\"Failed to write export file: {}\", e);\n                    } else {\n                        info!(\"Exported {} entries to {}\", entries.len(), export_path_clone.display());\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    error!(\"Failed to serialize history: {}\", e);\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Exporting history to {}\", export_path.display()));\n    }\n\n    /// Show history statistics.\n    fn show_history_stats(&mut self) {\n        let history_manager = match &self.history_manager {\n            Some(hm) => hm,\n            None => {\n                self.status_message = Some(\"History
